ref: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
text: Na kayawi kalɔ kesekɔ ƒui ni, Katɔti údie katɔ ku kalɔ.

ref: The earth was barren, with no form of life; it was under a roaring ocean covered with darkness. But the Spirit of God was moving over the water. The First Day
text: Na kalɔ kammɔ keeebo udu ɔdi. Na lɛla lɛdi lɛnɔ liiibo-ka kemmo, na lɛdisa kani náƒɔ kalɔ kammɔ lɛlɔ ƒui. Na Katɔti Kɔyu kani nkɛ mɔɔví ntu bɔni náƒɔ-ka anumi mmɔ ƒui lɛlɔ.

ref: God said, “I command light to shine!” And light started shining.
text: Amu na Katɔti údie lɛdi ɔnini, “Bukeye bubo,” na bukeye búbo.

ref: God looked at the light and saw that it was good. He separated light from darkness
text: Na Katɔti ɔ́nu ka bukeye bɔmmɔ bɔdia. Na úle bukeye bɔmmɔ na lɛdisa kudɔ.

ref: and named the light “Day” and the darkness “Night.” Evening came, then morning—that was the first day. The Second Day
text: Na bukeye bɔmmɔ na Katɔti ɔ́lɔ ɔnii kadi nwaa, na ɔ́lɔ lɛdisa ɔnii usien. Na useyi údu na kadi kégye na limeyi lideedi lɛ́vi.

ref: God said, “I command a dome to separate the water above it from the water below it.”
text: Amu na Katɔti údie lɛdi ɔnini, “Kuƒi kuduo ntu bɔmɛni kemmo, na kule ntu bɔmɛni bɔsiku ɔkpanadi kudɔ.”

ref: and named it “Sky.” Evening came, then morning—that was the second day. The Third Day
text: Na kuƒi kɔmmɔ na Katɔti ɔ́lɔ ɔnii kayaa. Na useyi údu na kadi kégye na limeyi linyɔdi lɛ́vi.

ref: God said, “I command the water under the sky to come together in one place, so there will be dry ground.” And that's what happened.
text: Amu na Katɔti údie lɛdi ɔnini, “Ntu bɔni náƒɔ kayaa kalɔ ni, bɔsala likpo linwi. Na kesii kani nákwii kakɔ kalɛ.” Na úbo úte lɛni.

ref: God named the dry ground “Land,” and he named the water “Ocean.” God looked at what he had done and saw that it was good.
text: Na kesii kani nákwii mmɔ na Katɔti ɔ́lɔ ɔnii, “kalɔ,” na ntu bɔni násala lɛlɔ buwi likpo linwi mmɔ na Katɔti ɔ́lɔ ɔnii, “ɛpu.” Na Katɔti ɔ́nu ka ɔdia.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to produce all kinds of plants, including fruit trees and grain.” And that's what happened.
text: Na údie lɛdi ɔnini, “Kalɔ kamɛni kakwɛ egyii ku livo lidu lidu ku adingyii lidu lɛnɔ.” Na úbo úte lɛni.

ref: The earth produced all kinds of vegetation. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Anwuɔsu kalɔ kammɔ kénwini lɛku ku livo ku adingyii lidu lidu. Na Katɔti ɔ́nu ka ɔdia.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the third day. The Fourth Day
text: Na useyi údu na kadi kégye na limeyi lɛtɛdi lɛ́vi.

ref: God said, “I command lights to appear in the sky and to separate day from night and to show the time for seasons, special days, and years.
text: Amu na Katɔti údie lɛdi ɔnini, “Bukeye lidu libo kayaa na bule usien ku kadi nwaa ntɛ. Na ɔmɛni ɔbla likposela ute uwi ku emeyi ku akwa.

ref: I command them to shine on the earth.” And that's what happened.
text: Na bukeye lidu lɛmɛni lɛsɔ lɛkya kayaa na buteekeye teewi kalɔ lɛlɔ.” Na úbo úte lɛni.

ref: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
text: Katɔti ɔ́bla bukeye bonwu akpa anyɔ. Bukeye bunengudi bɔni ndi ɔƒɛ bɔdanku kadi nwaa, na kɔbuli kɔni nsia lɛnɔ kɔdanku usien. Na ɔ́bla ayabi lɛnɔ.

ref: Then God put these lights in the sky to shine on the earth,
text: Katɔti ɔ́ya na bukeye lidu lɛmɛni ƒui lɛkya kayaa. Na buukeye teewi kalɔ lɛlɔ.

ref: to rule day and night, and to separate light from darkness. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Na ɔ́ya na bukeye bɔmɛni bɔɔdi kadi nwaa ku usien lɛlɔ, na buule lɛdisa ku bukeye ntɛ. Na Katɔti ɔ́nu ka ɔdia.

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fourth day. The Fifth Day
text: Na useyi údu na kadi kégye na limeyi linedi lɛ́vi.

ref: God said, “I command the ocean to be full of living creatures, and I command birds to fly above the earth.”
text: Amu na Katɔti údie lɛdi ɔnini, “Kɔbɛ ku kɔkpaku ku ketu kemmo bebuo benengudi lidu lidu bɛƒɔ ntu bɔmmɔ kemmo ƒui. Na lɛla biala lɛni mbo avwi lɛnɔ liteeveyi taavi kayaa.”

ref: So God made the giant sea monsters and all the living creatures that swim in the ocean. He also made every kind of bird. God looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu Katɔti údie bebweyi benengudi bani nkya ɛpu kemmo ku babambadi bani mɔɔví ntu kemmo ɔdi biala ku nwuna udu. Na údie bani mbo avwi lɛnɔ, ɔdi biala ku nwuna udu. Na Katɔti ɔ́nu ka ɔdia.

ref: Then he gave the living creatures his blessing—he told the ocean creatures to increase and live everywhere in the ocean and the birds to increase everywhere on earth.
text: Katɔti úte-ma kusɛ kusɛ na ɔ́su ɔnini, “Bɛwɔ na bimomo na bɛƒɔ ntu bɔmmɔ kemmo ƒui. Na bani mbo avwi lɛnɔ bemomo.”

ref: Evening came, then morning—that was the fifth day. The Sixth Day
text: Na useyi údu na kadi kégye na limeyi lɛlɔndi lɛ́vi.

ref: God said, “I command the earth to give life to all kinds of tame animals, wild animals, and reptiles.” And that's what happened.
text: Amu na Katɔti údie lɛdi ɔnini, “Bebuo lidu lidu bɛƒɔ kalɔ kamɛni ƒui lɛlɔ, bebuo benengudi ku bebliidi ku bebweyi lidu lidu ƒui.” Na úbo úte lɛni.

ref: God made every one of them. Then he looked at what he had done, and it was good.
text: Anwuɔsu Katɔti ɔ́bla bebuo benengudi ku bebliidi ku bebweyi lidu lidu, na báƒɔ kalɔ kammɔ ƒui lɛlɔ. Na Katɔti ɔ́nu ka ɔdia.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Amu na Katɔti ɔ́su ɔnini, “Bɛya na bɔbla ukyuli na ɔlɛ-bu na bula udu kenye kalɛ na utaadi ɛpu ku kalɔ ku kayaa bebweyi ƒui lɛlɔ.”

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu Katɔti údie ukyuli na ɔ́lɛ-nwu na nwuna udu kenye. Údie-ma, na badi ɔnana ku uleku.

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Na Katɔti úte-ma kusɛ kusɛ, na ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Bɛwɔ biyile kalɔ kamɛni ƒui lɛlɔ. Na bɛdi-ka lɛlɔ ku bebweyi bani ƒui nkya-ka kemmo.”

ref: I have provided all kinds of fruit and grain for you to eat.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Katɔti ɔ́su bekyuli bammɔ ɔnini, “Binu! Líti livo lidu ku adingyii lidu ƒui líte-mii nnii enwini bina adila.

ref: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.
text: Na líti livo líte bebuo bani nkya kalɔ lɛlɔ ku baasɛ ku bebweyi ƒui nnii linwini bana adila.”

ref: God looked at what he had done. All of it was very good! Evening came, then morning—that was the sixth day.
text: Katɔti ɔ́nu ala ani ƒui na ɔ́bla na adia-nwu sɔsɔ. Na useyi údu na kadi kégye na limeyi lɛkundi lɛ́vi.

ref: So the heavens and the earth and everything else were created. The Seventh Day
text: Lɛni na Katɔti úti ɔ́wɔlɔ katɔ ku kalɔ ku ala ani ƒui nkya-nya kemmo budie.

ref: By the seventh day God had finished his work, and so he rested.
text: Na údie lɛla biala ɔ́wɔlɔ asa na limeyi maatɛdi lídu. Anwuɔsu limeyi maatɛdi lɛlɔ ni, Katɔti ɔ́da kabla ƒui ɔ́ya na ɔ́nwuna.

ref: God blessed the seventh day and made it special, because on that day he rested from his work.
text: Na limeyi lɛmmɔ lɛdanku kusɛ kusɛ na lise kedee ka ɔ́ƒuna-ni úse ɔsu. Ɔsika limeyi lɛmɛni lɛlɔ na ɔ́nwuna ɔni na údie ala ƒui ɔ́wɔlɔ. Adam Ku Hawa Lɛlɔ Kɔtɛ

ref: That's how God created the heavens and the earth. The Garden of Eden When the Lord God made the heavens and the earth,
text: Kalɛ na bédie katɔ ku kalɔ kɔtɛ ndi amɛni. Uwi ɔni na Katɔti údie katɔ ku kalɔ ni,

ref: no grass or plants were growing anywhere. God had not yet sent any rain, and there was no one to work the land.
text: edula ku egyiibi adi átakwɛ tɔ na kalɔ lɛlɔ. Ɔsika Unwoti Katɔti útaya na ɔnantu ɔna uwi kalɔ lɛlɔ. Na ukyuli ɔdi lɛnɔ uuubo na utaatuma kalɔ kammɔ lɛlɔ.

ref: But streams came up from the ground and watered the earth.
text: Nsu ntukpa buuyi taasi kalɔ kemmo na buute kalɔ kammɔ ntu.

ref: The Lord God took some soil from the ground and made a man. God breathed life into the man, and the man started breathing.
text: Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́vɔ ukyuli ɔ́lɛku kalɔ kammɔ lɛti kemmo. Na ɔ́nwuna ɔƒɛƒɛɛ ɔni muute ngba ɔ́kya ukyuli ɔmmɔ enwu kemmo aani ɔyawine.

ref: The Lord made a garden in a place called Eden, which was in the east, and he put the man there.
text: Amu na Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́bla kemo kadi na kesii kani baalɔ banii Eden na ɔƒɛ katukɔ kakɛ. Na mmɔ ni, úti ukyuli ɔni na ɔ́vɔ mmɔ ɔ́kya.

ref: The Lord God filled the garden with all kinds of beautiful trees and fruit trees. Two other trees were in the middle of the garden. One of these gave life—the other gave the wisdom to know the difference between right and wrong.
text: Na kemo kammɔ kemmo ni, Katɔti ɔ́ya na egyii ákwɛ enye na adi egyii abuni ani mɔɔdia bɔnu ku edula ani ndia kaduɛ na adia ete ka badi. Na kemo kammɔ ntɛntɛ ni, kugyii kɔni muute ukyuli ngba kunye na kɔni mɔɔyá na ɔɔnu lɛbuni ku likpidi taati lɛnɔ kunye.

ref: From Eden a river flowed out to water the garden, then it divided into four rivers.
text: Na ketu kani muute kemo kammɔ ntu kaagbá taasi Eden mmɔ. Na ketu kammɔ kábwɛ kéle lɛlɔ ntukpa ine.

ref: The first one is the Pishon River that flows through the land of Havilah,
text: Ketu kedeedi lɛnyi ndi Pison. Na kaagbá taavi kalɛkɛ kani baalɔ Hawila kemmo, kesii kani na sika ugyiedi ɔkya.

ref: where pure gold, rare perfumes, and precious stones are found.
text: Kesii kammɔ ni, sika ugyiedi ɔni mɔɔdia bɔnu geen ku atabi ani náƒɔ kuye na baalɔ-nya banii Onis akya.

ref: The second is the Gihon River that winds through Ethiopia.
text: Na ketu kanyɔdi lɛnyi ndi Gihon na kaagbá taavi kalɛkɛ kani na baalɔ banii Kus mmɔ kemmo ƒui.

ref: The Tigris River that flows east of Assyria is the third, and the fourth is the Euphrates River.
text: Na ketu katɛdi lɛnyi ndi Hidekel na kaagbá taavi kalɛkɛ kani baalɔ Asiria ɔƒɛ katukɔ kakɛ. Na ketu kenedi lɛnyi ndi Euƒrate.

ref: The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to take care of it and to look after it.
text: Amu na Unwoti Katɔti úti ukyuli ɔni na ɔ́vɔ mmɔ ɔ́kya Eden kemo kammɔ kemmo ka utaabla-ka lɛlɔ kabla na utaanu-ka lɛlɔ.

ref: But the Lord told him, “You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Na ɔ́su ɔnana ɔmmɔ ɔnini, “Ebo kuƒi ka adi egyii ani nnye kemo kamɛni kemmo ebi ƒui.

ref: except the one that has the power to let you know the difference between right and wrong. If you eat any fruit from that tree, you will die before the day is over!”
text: Nsu kugyii kɔni mɔɔyá na ukyuli ɔɔnu lɛbuni ku likpidi taati aku ni, tadi-ku ebi. Ɔsika si ádi kugyii kɔmmɔ ebi ni, bukpi kani na adubo bukpi.”

ref: The Lord God said, “It isn't good for the man to live alone. I will make a suitable partner for him.”
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Katɔti ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ɔɔɔdia si ɔnana lɛti ɔkya. Ndubɔbla ubunkune ɔni námanku-nwu lite-nwu.”

ref: So the Lord God made him fall into a deep sleep, and he took out one of the man's ribs. Then after closing the man's side,
text: Ɔmɛni ɔsu ni, Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́ya na Adam úduo lɛlɛɛ kemmo úsie ɔgyu geen. Na uwi ɔni na ɔtɛ ɔlɛɛ mmɔ ni, Katɔti údie-nwu ɔƒɛnyi likubi linwi na úti ɔlɔna útile likpo lɛmmɔ.

ref: the Lord made a woman out of the rib. The Lord God brought her to the man,
text: Amu na Unwoti Katɔti úti likubi lɛni na údie ɔ́lɛku ɔnana ɔmmɔ ɔƒɛnyi mmɔ ɔ́bla uleku. Na ɔ́kpanku-nwu úboku ɔnana ɔmmɔ.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Mmɔ ni, ɔnana ɔmmɔ ɔ́su ɔnini, “Likubi lɛmɛni lɛ́si mina ekubi kemmo. Na ɔlɔna ɔmɛni ɔ́si mina ɔlɔna kemmo. Ndubɔlɔ-nwu nnii uleku. Ɔsika ɔnana kemmo na bédie-nwu bálɛku.”

ref: That's why a man will leave his own father and mother. He marries a woman, and the two of them become like one person.
text: Ɔmɛni ɔsu na ɔnana ɔdubɔmami ɔti ku uni lɛlɔ na uti lɛlɔ utámana ulekudi lɛlɔ. Na bana inyɔ badubɔbla ɔlɔna unwi.

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: Na uwi ɔmɛni kemmo ni, ɔnana ku uleku ɔmmɔ beli kasɔngya ɔni na kɔƒa kɔdi kulekpo-ma.

ref: The snake was sneakier than any of the other wild animals that the Lord God had made. One day it came to the woman and asked, “Did God tell you not to eat fruit from any tree in the garden?”
text: Na mmɔ ni, kɔlakpi ndi kebweyi kani mbo lɛkwɛ geen ntulo bebuo bani ƒui na Unwoti Katɔti údie akɔ. Na limeyi lɛdi ni, kɔlakpi kɔmmɔ kúsie uleku ɔmmɔ kudɔ na kútéye-nwu kɔnini, “Sɔsɔ geen na Katɔti ɔ́su-ɔ ɔnii, ‘Tadi egyii ammɔ adi biala ebi’?”

ref: The woman answered, “God said we could eat fruit from any tree in the garden,
text: Amu na uleku ɔmɛni údie kenye ɔnini, “Bɔkana na bɔdi kugyii biala kɔni nkya kemo kamɛni kemmo ebi.

ref: except the one in the middle. He told us not to eat fruit from that tree or even to touch it. If we do, we will die.”
text: Mmomu bɔsu na Katɔti ɔ́su ɔnii butadi kugyii kɔni nnye kemo kamɛni ntɛntɛ mmɔ ebi. Na butetuku-ku lɛnɔ. Si ɔɔɔdi lɛni ni, bɔdubo bukpi.”

ref: “No, you won't!” the snake replied.
text: Amu na kɔlakpi kɔmmɔ kɔ́su-nwu kɔnini, “Kudii! Ɔɔɔdi bukpi na bɛdubo bukpi.

ref: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”
text: Mmomu Katɔti ɔgyi ka si bɛ́di kugyii kɔmmɔ ebi ni, bɛdubo anu bɔda. Na bɛdubo bɔbla aani anwu Katɔti kalɛ. Na bɛdubɔnu lɛbuni ku likpidi lɛnɔ bɛti.”

ref: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
text: Amu ka uleku ɔmɛni ɔ́nu ɔ́ti ka kugyii kɔmmɔ ebi adia bɔdi, na aadia bɔnu, na aayá na ukyuli ɔɔdá anu ɔsu ni, úde adi na ɔ́di. Na úde ɔdi úte ɔgyani ɔni nkya mmɔ lɛnɔ na ɔ́di.

ref: At once they saw what they had done, and they realized they were naked. Then they sewed fig leaves together to cover themselves.
text: Mmɔ na bana inyɔ ƒui anumi ébimi na bánu báti ka bakɛ kasɔngya. Anwuɔsu béde kugyii evete bonwu adi básala na béti bábɛla lɛlɔ kasɔngya.

ref: Late in the afternoon, when the breeze began to blow, the man and woman heard the Lord God walking in the garden. So they hid behind some trees. Sin Brings a Curse
text: Amu ka useyi uwi údu ni, ɔnana ɔmmɔ ku ulekudi bénu Unwoti Katɔti kɔku uwi ɔni na ɔkɛ ɔɔví kemo kammɔ kemmo. Amu na bátábɛ na egyii akɔ kalɛ na Unwoti Katɔti ulanu-ma.

ref: The Lord God called out to the man and asked, “Where are you?”
text: Nsu Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́kpali ɔnana ɔmmɔ ɔnini, “Wɛ na akya?”

ref: The man answered, “I was naked, and when I heard you walking through the garden, I was frightened and hid!”
text: Amu na údie kenye ɔnini, “Línu-ɔ kɔku na kemo kammɔ kemmo na liƒu lɛ́da-mi, ɔsika nkɛ kasɔngya. Anwuɔsu líkeli ńtábɛ.”

ref: “How did you know you were naked?” God asked. “Did you eat any fruit from that tree in the middle of the garden?”
text: Mmɔ na Katɔti úye-nwu ɔnini, “Ɔma násu-ɔ ɔnii akɛ kasɔngya? Munuu kugyii kɔni lɛlɔ ebi na líbo-ɔ ɔda nnii tadi mmɔ ni, aku na átádi?”

ref: “It was the woman you put here with me,” the man said. “She gave me some of the fruit, and I ate it.”
text: Amu na ɔnana ɔmmɔ údie kenye ɔnini, “Uleku ɔni na éti éte-mi ni, anwu néde ebi ammɔ adi úte-mi na lɛ́di.”

ref: The Lord God then asked the woman, “What have you done?” “The snake tricked me,” she answered, “and I ate some of that fruit.”
text: Mmɔ na Unwoti Katɔti úye uleku ɔmmɔ ɔnini, “Bi na ábla mɛni?” Amu na uleku ɔmmɔ údie kenye ɔnini, “Kɔlakpi kɔmmɔ nátɔlɔ-mi na lɛ́di.”

ref: So the Lord God said to the snake: “Because of what you have done, you will be the only animal to suffer this curse— For as long as you live, you will crawl on your stomach and eat dirt.
text: Anwuɔsu Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́su kɔlakpi kɔmmɔ ɔnini, “Lɛla lɛni na ábla mɛni ɔsu ni, “Ewida adubɔsiaku-ɔ etulo bebuo ku alakpi akɔ bebweyi ƒui. Kaƒɔ lɛlɔ na adubo butaakpɛ. Na lɛti na adubo butaadi ƒula ngba uwi ƒui.

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: Na ndubɔya na awɔ ku uleku ɔmɛni binwini bekisine, na ƒula ubi ku uleku ɔmɛni ubi lɛnɔ benwini bekisine. Ɔdubo-ɔ lili bubo bɔbwɛ, na awɔ adu-nwu lɛnikpo.”

ref: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “You will suffer terribly when you give birth. But you will still desire your husband, and he will rule over you.”
text: Amu na Katɔti ɔ́su uleku ɔmmɔ ɔnini, “Ndubɔya na ebo abi geen uwi ɔni na ada kaƒɔ. Na adubunu ɔlɛ asa na awɔ bebi. Ina amu muedi ni, ƒula anu adubɔsia ƒ'ɔgyani lɛlɔ, na akya-nwu kalɔ.”

ref: The Lord said to the man, “You listened to your wife and ate the fruit I told you not to eat. And so, the ground will be under a curse because of what you did. As long as you live, you will have to struggle to grow enough food.
text: Na Adam anwu ni, Katɔti ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ka énu ƒ'ulekudi litemi na ádi kugyii kɔni ebi na líbo-ɔ ɔda nnii, ‘Tadi-ku ebi’ ɔsu ni, “Líwida kalɔ kammɔ ƒui na awɔ ɔsu. Na adubo abi bubo kana lɛlɔ asa na akɛ-ka lɛla lɛdi adi na ƒula ngba emeyi kemmo ƒui.

ref: Your food will be plants, but the ground will produce thorns and thistles.
text: Liyu ku lɛnyɛ ku ana udu adubo bɔkwɛ na kalɔ kammɔ lɛlɔ na awɔ ɔsu. Na kalɔ kammɔ kemmo na adubɔkɛ ƒula lɛdila.

ref: You will sweat all your life to earn a living; you were made out of soil, and you will once again turn into soil.”
text: Na adubuni ewulu na ƒula lɛtɛntɛ asa na akɛ ƒula lɛdila weti wetédu uwi ɔni na adubɔsɔ enwini esie kalɔ kemmo. Ɔsika aka na líti lɛ́vɔ-ɔ. Lɛti na adi na lɛti kani na adubɔsɔ enwini.”

ref: The man Adam named his wife Eve because she would become the mother of all who live.
text: Na Adam ɔ́sia ulekudi lɛnyi ɔnii Hawa ɔni kalɔ ndi ngba. Ɔsika anwu ndubɔbla ukyuli biala nli ngba uni.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Unwoti Katɔti úti ebuo ɔku ɔ́bla adala úte Adam ku ulekudi na báda. Bábɔku Adam Ku Hawa Bálɛku Eden Kemmo

ref: The Lord said, “They now know the difference between right and wrong, just as we do. But they must not be allowed to eat fruit from the tree that lets them live forever.”
text: Amu na Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ɔƒaani ni, ukyuli ɔgyi lɛbuni ku likpidi lɛnɔ aani abu kalɛ. Anwuɔsu ɔ́sɔ útalɛka ɔkɛ kuƒi na ude kugyii ebi ani mɔɔyá na ukyuli uulie ngba mmɔ ɔdi na ɔsɔ ulekpi.”

ref: So the Lord God sent them out of the Garden of Eden, where they would have to work the ground from which the man had been made.
text: Anwuɔsu Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́bɔku-ma ɔ́lɛku Eden kemo kammɔ, na ɔ́su-ma ɔnii batátaatuma kalɔ kani kemmo na údie-ma ɔlɛku mmɔ.

ref: Then God put winged creatures at the entrance to the garden and a flaming, flashing sword to guard the way to the life-giving tree.
text: Na ka ɔ́bɔku-ma ɔ́lɛku ɔ́wɔlɔ ni, ɔ́ya na nwuna batu bagyumanane badi benye ku kewii kadi mɔɔbwɛ ogya teesie anumi teebo kama taabala Eden kemo kammɔ ɔƒɛ katukɔ. Na bétile ukyuli biala kuƒi kalɛ na ulakana kugyii kɔni mɔɔyá na ukyuli uulie ngba mmɔ kudɔ bɔni bude.

ref: Adam and Eve had a son. Then Eve said, “I'll name him Cain because I got him with the help of the Lord.”
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Adam ku Hawa bélie aani ɔgya ku ule kalɛ. Na Hawa ɔ́da kaƒɔ na ɔ́wɔ Kain. Na ɔ́su ɔnini, “Katɔti úbunku-mi na lɛ́wɔ ɔnanabi.”

ref: Later she had another son and named him Abel. Abel became a sheep farmer, but Cain farmed the land.
text: Na ɔmmɔ kama ɔ́sɔ ɔ́wɔ ɔnwani Abel. Na Abel ɔmɛni ɔdi bebuo ɔnyuɛne. Na Kain ɔdi umonsene.

ref: One day, Cain gave part of his harvest to the Lord,
text: Na uwi ɔdi kama ni, Kain úti nwuna kemo edula adi úboku úbete Unwoti Katɔti.

ref: and Abel also gave an offering to the Lord. He killed the first-born lamb from one of his sheep and gave the Lord the best parts of it. The Lord was pleased with Abel and his offering,
text: Na Abel lɛnɔ útáƒuna nwuna baƒonu bedeedi bani na báka bɔwɔ akɔ unwi na úti kakɛ kani nélie lɛmwi kaduɛ úboku úbete Unwoti Katɔti. Na Abel lɛlɔ lɛ́da Unwoti Katɔti kubiti geen ku nwuna etela.

ref: but not with Cain and his offering. This made Cain so angry that he could not hide his feelings.
text: Nsu Kain ku nwuna etela átada Unwoti Katɔti kubiti. Ɔmɛni ɔsu Kain úti kɔnyi kati kati na nwuna anumi ávumana.

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
text: Na Unwoti úye Kain ɔnini, “Bi ɔsu na éti kɔnyi kati kati na ƒula anumi ávumana lɛni?

text: Si ábla lɛla lɛni na mɔɔyɔ́ ni, nka ƒula kayi kemmo kadubɔbla ƒaa. Nsu likpidi lɛɛtú-ɔ na lɛɛyɔ́ ka lɛdi-ɔ lɛlɔ. Anwuɔsu nye kagyumana na adi likpidi lɛmmɔ lɛlɔ.”

ref: Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let's go for a walk.” And when they were out in a field, Cain attacked and killed him.

text: Amu na Unwoti Katɔti úye Kain ɔnini, “Wɛ na ƒ'ɔnwani Abel ɔ́vi?” Mmɔ na Kain údie kenye ɔnini, “Lɛɛɛgyi. Ɔɔɔdi mina kudɔ na uli.”

ref: Then the Lord said: Why have you done this terrible thing? You killed your own brother, and his blood flowed onto the ground. Now his blood is calling out for me to punish you.
text: Amu na Unwoti úye Kain ɔnini, “Bi ɔsu na ábla likpidi bonwu lɛmɛni udu? Nu! Mɔɔnu ƒ'ɔwani ubugya ɔni nwi kalɔ. Use aani ukyuli mɔɔkpali ɔnii nkpo nte-nwu.

ref: And so, I'll put you under a curse. Because you killed Abel and made his blood run out on the ground, you will never be able to farm it again.
text: Ɔƒɛni ni, ewida asiaku-ɔ. Na kalɔ kammɔ kéni-ɔ ka ádu ƒ'ɔnwani na nwuna ubugya úwi kalɔ kammɔ lɛlɔ ɔsu.

ref: When you try to farm the land, it won't produce anything for you. From now on, you'll be without a home, and you'll spend the rest of your life wandering from place to place.
text: Si átuma-ka lɛlɔ ni, kasɔ kaladia-a edula. Na adubo ukeline bunwini na aaadubo keliekɔ kadi bɔkɛ na kayawi kammɔ ƒui lɛlɔ.”

ref: “No!” the Lord answered. “Anyone who kills you will be punished seven times worse than I am punishing you.” So the Lord put a mark on Cain to warn everyone not to kill him.

text: Amu na ɔ́tala Abloduma ɔdi lɛnɔ ɔnii utánu si ntu bɔmmɔ bɔ́ɔkpɛ bɔ́wɔlɔ ƒui.

ref: Deep water was still everywhere, and when the dove could not find a place to land, it flew back to the boat. Then Noah held out his hand and helped it back in.
text: Nsu Abloduma ɔmmɔ útakɛ kesii kani na ɔyu ulie, ɔsika ntu bɔmmɔ bútakwii tɔ. Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ɔ́sɔ únwini úsie ɔkɔlɔ bonwu ɔmmɔ kudɔ na Noa úƒie-nwu ɔ́kya ɔkɔlɔ kemmo.

ref: Seven days later Noah sent the dove out again.
text: Amu na Noa ɔ́kywɛ na emeyi maatɛ ávi asa na ɔ́sɔ ɔ́tala Abloduma ɔmmɔ úmene.

ref: It returned in the evening, holding in its beak a green leaf from an olive tree. Noah knew the water was finally going down.
text: Amu ka Abloduma ɔmmɔ únwini úbo ni, nnɔ kugyii livete lɛwɔdi lɛtu-nwu kenye. Mmɔ na Noa ɔ́ti ka ntu bɔmmɔ bɔ́ɔkpɛ bɔ́wɔlɔ.

ref: He waited seven more days before sending the dove out again, and this time it did not return.
text: Nsu Noa ɔ́sɔ ɔ́ya na emeyi maatɛ bamba ásɔ ávi asa na ɔ́sɔ ɔ́tala Abloduma ɔmmɔ. Nsu ka Abloduma ɔmmɔ úsie ni, ɔ́sɔ útenwini útebo-nwu kudɔ lɛnɔ.

ref: years old. And by the first day of that year, almost all the water had gone away. Noah made an opening in the roof of the boat and saw that the ground was getting dry.
text: ) nwuna kɔbuli kudeedi limeyi lideedi lɛlɔ ni, ntu bɔmmɔ bɔ́kwii na kalɔ kammɔ lɛlɔ ƒui. Mmɔ na Noa ɔ́lɛmi ɔkɔlɔ ɔmmɔ katɔ na ɔ́nu ka ntu bɔmmɔ bɔ́kwii na kalɔ kammɔ ƒui lɛlɔ.

ref: By the twenty-seventh day of the second month, the earth was completely dry.
text: Na kɔbuli kunyɔdi kɔni nsia limeyi aavlanyɔ-maatɛdi lɛlɔ ni, kalɔ kammɔ kákwii kede kede.

ref: God said to Noah,
text: Amu na Katɔti ɔ́su Noa ɔnini,

ref: “You, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law may now leave the boat.
text: “Lɛ ɔkɔlɔ ɔmmɔ kemmo bo, awɔ ku ƒ'ulekudi ku ƒula bebi bananabi ku bana beleku.

ref: Let out the birds, animals, and reptiles, so they can mate and live all over the earth.”
text: Na aya na bebweyi bani nkya-a kudɔ mmɔ bana ƒui lidu lɛnɔ balɛ bebo kalɛ na ama lɛnɔ bawɔ na bemomo na kalɔ kammɔ ƒui lɛlɔ.”

ref: After Noah and his family had left the boat,
text: Anwuɔsu Noa ɔ́lɛ úbo ku ulekudi ku nwuna bebi bananabi ku bana beleku.

ref: Noah built an altar where he could offer sacrifices to the Lord. Then he offered on the altar one of each kind of animal and bird that could be used for a sacrifice.
text: Amu na Noa ɔ́tu lɛkpabi lɛdi úte Unwoti na mmɔ. Na ɔmmɔ kama ni, úƒie bebuo bani nse sɛɛn mmɔ akɔ badi ku baasɛ bani nse sɛɛn na úti ɔ́kpa úte Unwoti na lɛkpabi lɛmmɔ lɛlɔ.

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Na Unwoti ɔ́ƒɔ akpala ammɔ ku kayi ƒui na ɔ́su ɔnini, “Nsɔ lɛɛɛdubo kalɔ kamɛni buwida na ukyuli ɔsu ina ka likpidi kasusu káƒɔ-nwu lɛlɔ kemmo ƒui ɔ́si nwuna lɛnamu uwi. Na nsɔ lɛɛɛdubo bebweyi ƒui udu bɔyila aani kalɛ na lɛ́bla.

ref: As long as the earth remains, there will be planting and harvest, cold and heat; winter and summer, day and night.
text: “Na ɔ́si ɔƒɛni wetédu kayawi kawɔlɔkɔ ni, edula ɔvawi ku udewi, ketudi uwi ku liwulu uwi, kaƒɛndi ku kɔyɔ uwi, usien ku ɔƒɛ ulada bubo ulaya kudii.”

ref: God said to Noah and his sons: I am giving you my blessing. Have a lot of children and grandchildren, so people will live everywhere on this earth.
text: Amu na Katɔti úte Noa ku nwuna bebi kusɛ kusɛ na ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Bɛwɔ na bimomo na bɛƒɔ kalɔ lɛlɔ ƒui.

ref: All animals, birds, reptiles, and fish will be afraid of you. I have placed them under your control,
text: Na beƒiebuo bani nkya kalɔ lɛlɔ ƒui ku baasɛ bani mɔɔví kayaa ku bebweyi bani mɔɔví kalɔ lɛlɔ ku kɔbɛ kɔni nkya ɛpu kemmo ƒui ni, líti-ma lɛ́kya-mi enu kemmo ka bɛdi-ma lɛlɔ.

ref: and I have given them to you for food. From now on, you may eat them, as well as the green plants that you have always eaten.
text: Na beƒiebuo ku baasɛ ku bebweyi bamɛni ƒui badubo-mii bɔyia. Na lɛla biala lɛni mbo ngba na liisié taavi linwini adila lite-mii. Kalɛ na líti lɛku kemmo evete lidu lidu líte-mii nnii bɛdi ni, lɛni kani na líti lɛla biala muute-mii ɔƒɛni.

ref: But life is in the blood, and you must not eat any meat that still has blood in it.
text: “Nsu bitakun ebuo ɔni bélii-nwu limɔ bádu na ubugya ɔmu ɔkya-nwu kemmo.

ref: I created humans to be like me, and I will punish any animal or person that takes a human life. If an animal kills someone, that animal must die. And if a person takes the life of another, that person must be put to death.
text: “Ukyuli biala ɔni mewi nwuna ɔkpana ubugya kalɔ ni, anwu lɛnɔ ukyuli ndubo-nwu ubugya kalɔ buwi. Ɔsika Katɔti udu kenye na Katɔti údie ukyuli.

ref: I want you and your descendants to have many children, so people will live everywhere on earth.
text: Na amii ni, bɛwɔ na bimomo biyile kalɔ kammɔ ƒui lɛlɔ.”

ref: Again, God said to Noah and his sons:
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Katɔti ɔ́su Noa ku nwuna bebi ɔnini,

ref: I am going to make a solemn promise to you and to everyone who will live after you.
text: “Ɔƒaani ni, línyimi lɛla lɛdi ku-mii ku bina bawɔ bani masia-mii kama.

ref: This includes the birds and the animals that came out of the boat.
text: Na linyese lɛmɛni lɛdanku baasɛ ku ɔmantɛ bebuo ku beƒiebuo bani nákya-a kudɔ na bálɛ ɔkɔlɔ bonwu ɔmmɔ kemmo.

ref: I promise every living creature that the earth and those living on it will never again be destroyed by a flood.
text: Na lise mina bunyimi líse ka nsɔ lɛɛɛdubo lɛla lɛdi biala ngba udu bɔyila ku ntu. Na ɔnantu ɔsɔ ulana ulaƒɔ kayawi ƒui na wayila-ka udu lɛnɔ.”

ref: The rainbow that I have put in the sky will be my sign to you and to every living creature on earth. It will remind you that I will keep this promise forever.
text: Lɛ́ya na kɔdigbo kɔdubɔlɛ kayaa kemmo, na aku ndi likposela lɛni nkya ami ku kayawi bekyuli ƒui ntɛ.

ref: When I send clouds over the earth, and a rainbow appears in the sky,
text: Na uwi biala na ulɔyɔ iye liƒlukutu lɛ́sia kayaa na kɔdigbo kɔ́lɛ-nwu kemmo ni,

ref: I will remember my promise to you and to all other living creatures. Never again will I let floodwaters destroy all life.
text: ndubo bunyimo mina lɛla lɛni na línyimiku-mii ku kayawi bebweyi lidu ƒui ka ntu bɔsɔ buleyi buláƒɔ kayawi ƒui na bɔyila kayawi ala ammɔ udu.

ref: When I see the rainbow in the sky, I will always remember the promise that I have made to every living creature.
text: Na uwi biala na kɔdigbo kɔ́lɛ na kayaa ni, ndubo bunyimo lɛla lɛni na ami Katɔti línyimi ku lɛla biala nli ngba.”

ref: The rainbow will be the sign of that solemn promise. Noah and His Family
text: Anwuɔsu Katɔti ɔ́su Noa ɔnini, “Lɛla lɛmɛni ndi likposela ute mina bunyimi bɔni na línyimi ku lɛla biala mbo ngba na kayawi kamɛni lɛlɔ.” Noa Ku Nwuna Bebi

ref: Noah and his sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, left the boat. Ham later had a son named Canaan.
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ ni, Noa bebi bammɔ anyi ndi Sem ku Ham ku Yaƒet na ama ku-nwu nálɛ ɔkɔlɔ bonwu ɔmmɔ kemmo. Na Ham náwɔ Kanaan.

ref: Noah farmed the land and was the first to plant a vineyard.
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ ni, Noa úse kalɔ ɔɔƒɔ kemo na ɔ́bla adingyii kemo kadi.

ref: Ham entered the tent and saw him naked, then went back outside and told his brothers.
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ lɛlɔ ni, Ham ɔni náwɔ Kanaan úbo úbanu ka ɔti Noa ɔtɛ kasɔngya. Amu na útásu nwuna banwani bananabi inyɔ bakladi.

ref: Shem and Japheth put a robe over their shoulders and walked backwards into the tent. Without looking at their father, they placed it over his body.
text: Nsu Sem ku Yaƒet ama béti awu gyɔlɔɔ ɔdi na bakɛ kama kama béti bétébi-nwu lɛlɔ. Na bénwini bana anumi bésienku kesii bamba kalɛ na balanu bana ɔti kasɔngya.

ref: When Noah sobered up and learned what his youngest son had done,
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ka Noa ɔ́ta kalɛɛkɔ ni, básu-nwu lɛla lɛni na Ham ɔni ndi nwuna kawɔkpulo ɔ́bla.

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: Amu na úwida Kanaan ɔnini, “Adubɔnuku-ni! Aaadubo kenyekɔ kadi bɔkɛ adubo bunwini ƒula banwani ƒui kabla ɔblane ukplodi.”

ref: I ask the Lord my God to bless Shem and make Canaan his slave.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, ɔ́su ɔnini, “Bimwi Unwoti ɔni ndi Sem Katɔti. Na ɔya na Kanaan unwini Sem kabla ɔblane.

ref: I pray God will give Japheth more and more land and let him take over the territory of Shem. May Canaan be his slave.”
text: Na ɔya na Yaƒet kalɔ lɛmii lisie kama libu. Na ɔya na Yaƒet bawɔ bawɔ ku Sem bawɔ bawɔ badi bete lɛlɔ. Na Kanaan unwini Yaƒet kabla ɔblane.”

ref: Japheth's descendants had their own languages, tribes, and land. They were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras. Gomer was the ancestor of Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah. Javan was the ancestor of Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim, who settled along the coast. The Descendants of Ham
text: Na kaba kamɛni négyingya bákya kayawi ekpo ƒui lɛlɔ na bana adua ku kadi kenye. Na baatɛmi bana kadi lidu adi lɛnɔ. Ham Ɔ́wɔ Bebi

ref: At first everyone spoke the same language,
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, lɛdi linwi kani na bayawine ƒui bátaatɛmi.

ref: they said: Let's build a city with a tower that reaches to the sky! We'll use hard bricks and tar instead of stone and mortar. We'll become famous, and we won't be scattered all over the world.
text: Na básu lɛlɔ banii, “Bibo na bɔda ɔma unengudi ɔdi bute lɛlɔ. Na bɔtu katu taasia ɔtɔ bonwu ɔdi na ɔkuku útédu Katɔti kudɔ. Na bɔƒɔ lɛnyi linengudi na bulegyingya bulakya kayawi.”

ref: But when the Lord came down to look at the city and the tower,
text: Amu na Unwoti ɔ́yu úbo kalɔ na úbanu ɔma ku ɔtɔ ɔni na bekyuli bammɔ badaku bɔtu mmɔ.

ref: he said: These people are working together because they all speak the same language. This is just the beginning. Soon they will be able to do anything they want.
text: Mmɔ na ɔ́su ɔnini, “Si bekyuli bamɛni badi benwi na baatɛmi lɛdi linwi na béese kalɔ badaku lɛla lɛmɛni udu bɔbla ni, amu lɛla lɛdi liiibo na si béti-ni kasusu na balakana bɔbla.

ref: Let's go down and confuse them! We'll make them speak different languages, and they won't be able to understand each other.
text: Anwuɔsu bɛya na bɔyu busie-ma kudɔ. Na butéƒu bana lɛdi lɛni na baatɛmi kalɛ na ɔdi ɔsɔ ulenu ɔkpanadi butemi kalɔ.”

ref: So the people had to stop building the city, because the Lord confused their language and scattered them all over the earth. That's how the city of Babel got its name. The Descendants of Shem
text: Anwuɔsu baalɔ lɛla lɛni nálɛ mmɔ banii Babel ɔni kalɔ ndi buƒu bɔkya, ɔsika mmɔ na Unwoti ɔ́ya na bayawine lɛdi lɛni na bátaatɛmi liƒu lɛkya. Na ɔ́si ɔmmɔ kama ni, Unwoti ɔ́ya na bégyingya bákya kayawi kammɔ ƒui lɛlɔ. Ala Ani Nalɛ Ɔ́si Sem Uwi Útédu Abram Uwi

ref: Terah decided to move from Ur to the land of Canaan. He took along Abram and Sarai and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran. But when they came to the city of Haran, they settled there instead.
text: Tera ɔ́kpanku ubidi Abram ku Haran ubidi Lot ɔni ndi Tera uwɔ, ku Abram ulekudi Sarai ɔni ndi Tera ɔmɛni ubi ulekubi. Na bana ƒui báyu ulu bási Ur mmɔ na Kaldea kalɔ lɛlɔ ka beteesie Kanaan kalɔ lɛlɔ. Nsu ka bébedu kalɔ kani baalɔ banii Haran ni, báda keliekɔ mmɔ na bélie mmɔ.

ref: years old and died in Haran.
text: ) na úkpi na Haran kalɔ kammɔ lɛlɔ.

ref: The Lord said to Abram: Leave your country, your family, and your relatives and go to the land that I will show you.
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Unwoti ɔ́su Abram ɔnini, “Tɔlɔ ƒula kalɔ lɛlɔ, ku ƒula bekyuli kudɔ ku ƒ'ɔti kaduli na eteesie kalɔ kani na matina-a lɛlɔ.”

ref: I will bless you and make your descendants into a great nation. You will become famous and be a blessing to others.
text: “Ndubɔya na kadi bekyuli pii balɛ basiku-ɔ lɛlɔ. Na ndubute-ɔ kusɛ kusɛ. Ndubɔya na aƒɔ lɛnyi geen, na bekyuli badubɔsia-a lɛlɔ na bakɛ kusɛ kusɛ

ref: I will bless those who bless you, but I will put a curse on anyone who puts a curse on you. Everyone on earth will be blessed because of you.
text: Bekyuli bani mete-ɔ kusɛ kusɛ ni, ami lɛnɔ ndubute-ma kusɛ kusɛ. Na ukyuli biala ɔni mewida-a ni, ami lɛnɔ ndubo-nwu buwida. Ndubute kalɔ lɛlɔ bekyuli ƒui kusɛ kusɛ ɔkɛnku awɔ lɛlɔ.”

ref: Abram went as far as the sacred tree of Moreh in a place called Shechem. The Canaanites were still living in the land at that time,
text: Abram úsie ɔ́vi kalɔ kammɔ ƒui kemmo útédu kesii kani na More kugyii kunengudi kunye na Sekem. Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, na Kanaan kadi bekyuli nli mmɔ.

ref: but the Lord appeared to Abram and promised, “I will give this land to your family forever.” Abram then built an altar there for the Lord.
text: Unwoti údie lɛlɔ ɔ́tina Abram na mmɔ na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ƒula uwɔ na ndubo kalɔ kamɛni buti bute.” Anwuɔsu ɔ́tu lɛkpabi lɛdi na mmɔ úte Unwoti ɔni nédie lɛlɔ ɔ́tina-nwu.

ref: Abram traveled to the hill country east of Bethel and camped between Bethel and Ai, where he built another altar and worshiped the Lord.
text: Ka ɔ́si mmɔ ni, údi úsie abi ammɔ kudɔ na Betel ɔƒɛ katukɔ kakɛ na ɔ́lɛ nwuna adi kevo na mmɔ na Betel ku Ai lɛma ntɛ. Anwuɔsu Betel ɔkya ɔƒɛ kanukɔ na Ai ɔkya ɔƒɛ katukɔ. Mmɔ lɛnɔ ɔ́lɛ lɛkpabi na ɔ́kpali Unwoti.

ref: Later, Abram started out toward the Southern Desert. Abram in Egypt
text: Ka Abram ɔ́ta mmɔ ni, úti anumi ɔ́tina Negev kalɔ kakwiidi. Abram Úsie Egyipte

ref: When the Egyptians see you, they will murder me because I am your husband. But they won't kill you.
text: Na si Egyipteƒuɔ bánu-ɔ ni, badubo bɔsu banii, ‘Ulekudi na ɔdi mɛni.’ Na badubo-mi bɔdu na baya na awɔ elie ngba.

ref: Please save my life by saying you are my sister.”
text: Anwuɔsu su-ma ka mina ɔnwani ulekubi na adi. Na ɔkɛnku awɔ ɔsu badubo-mi bɔda kaduɛ na basɔ baládu-mi.”

ref: As soon as Abram and Sarai arrived in Egypt, the Egyptians noticed how beautiful she was.
text: Ka Abram úbeduo Egyipte kalɔ lɛlɔ ni, Egyipteƒuɔ bánu ka uleku ɔmmɔ ɔdia sɔsɔ.

ref: The king's officials told him about her, and she was taken to his house.
text: Na ka Ƒarao benengu bammɔ bánu-nwu ni, bémwi-nwu bátina Ƒarao na bákpanku-nwu bésienku nwuna ɔga kaduli mmɔ.

ref: The king was good to Abram because of Sarai, and Abram was given sheep, cattle, donkeys, slaves, and camels.
text: Ɔ́da Abram kaduɛ na Sarai ɔsu na béte-nwu baƒonu ku ɔmantɛ bakɔ ku bebuo ala betine bagyani ku beledi ku bananabi kabla bablane ku belekubi kabla bablane ku baƒlumu lɛnɔ.

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.
text: Nsu Unwoti ɔ́ya na ɔlɛ kpakplaa ɔdi úbo Ƒarao ku nwuna kaduli bekyuli na Abram ulekudi Sarai ɔsu.

ref: Finally, the king sent for Abram and said to him, “What have you done to me? Why didn't you tell me Sarai was your wife?
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu Ƒarao ɔ́tala na bátálɔ Abram béboku-nwu na úye-nwu ɔnini, “Bi na ɔdi na ábla-bu mɛni, na átasu-mi ka ƒ'ulekudi na ɔdi?

ref: Why did you make me believe she was your sister? Now I've married her. Take her and go! She's your wife.”
text: Na bi ɔsu na ásu-mi anii, ‘Mina ɔnwani ulekubi na ɔdi.’ Ɔmmɔ ɔsu na líti-nwu lísienku mina kaduli ka unwini-mi uleku. Aanu ƒ'ulekudi, ɔƒaani tiku-nwu na biteesie!”

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Amu na Ƒarao úbo nwuna benengu bammɔ ɔda na Abram lɛlɔ na bédie-nwu ulu ku ulekudi ku ala ani ƒui na ubo na bátɔlɔ.

ref: Abram and Sarai took everything they owned and went to the Southern Desert. Lot went with them.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Abram ɔ́tɔlɔ Egyipte úti ɔ́kɔ úsie kesii kani baalɔ Negev. Na útiku ulekudi ku ala ani ƒui na ubo. Na Lot lɛnɔ ɔ́siaku-nwu.

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Abram úbenwini ɔlakɛne na bebuo bani na ubo ku sika ugyiedi ku ɔƒualadi ɔsu.

ref: Abram moved from place to place in the Southern Desert. And finally, he went north and set up his tents between Bethel and Ai,
text: Ka ɔ́si Negev mmɔ ni, úsie ɔ́vi ekpo ekpo laa na úbedu lɛma lɛni ndi Betel ku Ai. Lɛma lɛmɛni ntɛ na ɔ́ka nwuna kevo bɔtu.

ref: where he had earlier camped and built an altar. There he worshiped the Lord.
text: Na mmɔ na ɔ́ka lɛkpabi bɔtu bute Katɔti. Mmɔ na Abram útaakpali Katɔti lɛnyi teemwi-nwu.

ref: Lot, who was traveling with him, also had sheep, goats, and cattle, as well as his own family and slaves.
text: Uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Lot ɔni lɛnɔ nkɛ ɔsiaku Abram ni, anwu lɛnɔ ubo bebuo lidu lidu na bemomo geen na ɔ́tu nvo pii lɛnɔ.

ref: At this time the Canaanites and the Perizzites were still living in the same area, and so there wasn't enough pastureland left for Abram and Lot with all of their animals. Besides this, the men who took care of Abram's animals and the ones who took care of Lot's animals started quarreling.
text: Ɔmɛni úbaya na kadɔ keduo Abram bebuo lɛlɔ banune ku Lot bebuo lɛlɔ banune ntɛ. Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo lɛnɔ ni, Kanaanƒuɔ ku Perisiƒuɔ bakya kalɔ kammɔ kemmo.

ref: Abram said to Lot, “We are close relatives. We shouldn't argue, and our men shouldn't be fighting one another.
text: Na ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Abram ɔ́su Lot ɔnini, “Taya na kadɔ kakya ami ku-ɔ ntɛ iye ƒula bebuo lɛlɔ banune ku ami badi ntɛ. Ɔsika banwani na bɔdi.

ref: There is plenty of land for you to choose from. Let's separate. If you go north, I'll go south; if you go south, I'll go north.”
text: Aanu ka kalɔ kamɛni ƒui kewi ƒula anumi. Ya na bugyingyaku lɛlɔ. Na si awɔ ésie kɔdianu ni, na ami nteesie kɔmiinu.”

ref: This happened before the Lord had destroyed the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah. And when Lot looked around, he saw there was plenty of water in the Jordan Valley. All the way to Zoar the valley was as green as the garden of the Lord or the land of Egypt.
text: Amu na ka Lot ɔ́kɔliku anumi na ɔ́nu ɔ́mana ni, ɔ́nu ka Yordan kalɔ kani nwi késela ƒui ni, ntu bɔkya-ka kemmo geen. Na kese aani Katɔti kemo kani na ɔ́bla na Eden. Na kese aani Egyipte kalɔ kani nwi késie ɔma ɔni ndi Soar kakɛ. Na uwi ɔni na ɔɔnu ala amɛni ƒui lɛni ni, ɔdi uwi ɔni na Katɔti útakyula tɔ Sodom ku Gomora kalɔ.

ref: So Lot chose the whole Jordan Valley for himself, and as he started out toward the east, he and Abram separated.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu kalɔ kani nwi késie Yordan kakɛ mmɔ na Lot ɔ́ƒuna. Na ɔ́yu ulu úsie ɔƒɛ katukɔ. Mmɔ nkyuli inyɔ bamɛni bégyingyaku lɛlɔ.

ref: Abram stayed in the land of Canaan. But Lot settled near the cities of the valley and put up his tents not far from Sodom,
text: Abram úlie Kanaan kalɔ lɛlɔ. Na Lot ɔ́tu nwuna nvo na kalɔ ƒiaa ntɛ kesii kani nde Sodom.

ref: where the people were evil and sinned terribly against the Lord. Abram Moves to Hebron
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Sodom bekyuli bebo kuli ɔkplu na bátaabla alaekpidi lidu lidu taaƒiɔ Katɔti.

ref: After Abram and Lot had gone their separate ways, the Lord said to Abram: Look around to the north, south, east, and west.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, uwi ɔni na Lot ɔ́mami Abram lɛlɔ ni, Katɔti ɔ́su Abram ɔnini, “Talaku ƒula anumi na anu asi kesii kani na enye na anu esie katɔ kakɛ ku kada kakɛ. Na anu esie ɔƒɛ katukɔ ku ɔƒɛ kanukɔ.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: Na kalɔ kani ƒui na aanu ka kewi mmɔ ni, aka na ndubuti lite-ɔ ku ƒula bawɔ uwi ƒui.

ref: I will give you more descendants than there are specks of dust on the earth, and someday it will be easier to count those specks of dust than to count your descendants.
text: Na ndubɔya na ƒula bawɔ bemomo aani kɔnwinimi. Na si ukyuli ɔdi ɔ́kana na ɔka kɔnwinimi kɔmmɔ ni, ɔdubɔkana na ɔka ƒula bawɔ lɛnɔ.

ref: Now walk back and forth across the land, because I am going to give it to you.
text: Sie na atáka amana kalɔ kammɔ ƒui linene ku bɔbɛbɛ, ɔsika aka na liti muute-ɔ.”

ref: Abram took down his tents and went to live near the sacred trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he built an altar in honor of the Lord.
text: Anwuɔsu Abram ɔ́ta mmɔ ku nwuna ala ƒui na úsie kesii kani na Mamre egyii enengudi enye na Hebron kalɔ lɛlɔ. Na mmɔ na ɔ́tu lɛkpabi úte Katɔti.

ref: About this time, King Amraphel of Babylonia, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Amraƒel mɔɔdi lɛga na Sinear, na Ariok ɔdi ɔga na Elasar, na Kedorlaomer ɔdi ɔga na Elam, na Tidal ɔdi ɔga na Goyim.

ref: attacked King Bera of Sodom, King Birsha of Gomorrah, King Shinab of Admah, King Shemeber of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela, a city also known as Zoar.
text: Amu na uwi ɔmɛni kemmo ni, baga bedeedi bamɛni báta kekpo ku Bera ɔni ndi ɔga na Sodom ku Birsa ɔni ndi ɔga na Gomora ku Sinab ɔni ndi ɔga na Adma ku Semeber ɔni ndi ɔga na Seboim ku Bela, kalɔ kani lɛnɔ baalɔ Soar ɔga lɛlɔ.

ref: King Chedorlaomer and his allies had ruled these last five kings for twelve years, but in the thirteenth year the kings rebelled and joined forces in Siddim Valley, which is now covered by the southern part of the Dead Sea.
text: Akwa leevu-anyɔ sɔɔn na baga bekplodi bamɛni bakya Ɔga Kedorlaomer kalɔ. Na lɛkwa leevu-lɛtɛdi kemmo ni, baga bamɛni lɛnɔ báta bénye Kedorlaomer lɛlɔ.

ref: A year later King Chedorlaomer and his allies attacked and defeated the Rephaites in Ashteroth-Karnaim, the Zuzites in Ham, and the Emites in Shaveh-Kiriathaim.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama na lɛkwa leevu-linedi kemmo ni, Kedorlaomer ku baga bani na anwu ku-ma násala lɛlɔ mmɔ básɔ báta bésie bétékpoku Reƒaiƒuɔ na Asteroƒ Karnaim kalɔ lɛlɔ ku Susiƒuɔ na Ham kalɔ lɛlɔ ku Emiƒuɔ na Sare Kiriataim kalɔ lɛlɔ ku

ref: They also defeated the Horites in the hill country of Edom, as far as El-Paran, near the desert.
text: Horiƒuɔ na Seir abi lɛlɔ. Na ɔkɛ mmɔ útédu kalɔ kani baalɔ El-Paran na ketele kudɔ.

ref: They went back to the city of Enmishpat, better known as Kadesh. Then they captured all the land that belonged to the Amalekites, and they defeated the Amorites who were living in Hazazon-Tamar.
text: Mmɔ na básɔ bénwini bésie En-Misƒat, kesii kani basɔ baalɔ lɛnɔ banii Kades. Na bádi Amalek kalɛkɛ mmɔ ƒui lɛlɔ ku Amoriƒuɔ bani nli Hasason Tamar kalɔ lɛlɔ.

ref: At Siddim Valley, the armies of the kings of Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, Zeboiim, and Bela fought the armies of King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Babylonia, and King Arioch of Ellasar. The valley
text: Na békpoku Ɔga Kedorlaomer ɔni mɔɔdi Elam kalɔ lɛlɔ ku Tidal ɔni mɔɔdi lɛga na Goim kalɔ lɛlɔ ku Amraƒel ɔni mɔɔdi lɛga na Sinear ku Ariok ɔni mɔɔdi lɛga na Elasa kalɔ lɛlɔ. Na lɛkpa linwi lɛdi baga ine na beekpóku lɛkpa linyɔdi bani ndi baga ɛlɔ.

ref: was full of tar pits, and when the troops from Sodom and Gomorrah started running away, some of them fell into the pits. Others escaped to the hill country.
text: Ɔƒɛni Sidim Ɔkwalɛ ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, libu lɛdi lɛkya mmɔ lɛni kemmo na béku nnɔ tonwu mataa kɔni na beewi lilu. Na uwi ɔni na Sodom ku Gomora baga beekeli ni, bana bekyuli badi bánu libu lɛmmɔ kemmo. Na bakladi békeli bésie abi lɛlɔ.

ref: Their enemies took everything of value from Sodom and Gomorrah, including their food supplies.
text: Baga ine bammɔ béƒie Sodom ku Gomora bekeline bamɛni ala ku adila ƒui báƒɔ na bátɔlɔ.

ref: They also captured Abram's nephew Lot, who lived in Sodom. They took him and his possessions and then left.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu bétiku Abram ɔnwani Lot ku nwuna ala lɛnɔ, ɔsika Sodom mmɔ na ɔ́kya.

ref: At this time Abram the Hebrew was living near the oaks that belonged to Mamre the Amorite. Mamre and his brothers Eshcol and Aner were Abram's friends. Someone who had escaped from the battle told Abram
text: Na ukyuli ɔni kani nákana bukeli ni, anwu nébo ubedie litemi lɛmɛni kalɔ úte Abram, Hebrini ɔmmɔ. Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Abram kakyakɔ kedenku Mamre egyii enengudi ammɔ. Na Mamre ɔmɛni ɔdi Amorini ɔni ndi Eskol ku Aner ɔnwani. Na bekyuli bamɛni ƒui ni, ama ku Abram básala lɛlɔ na badi benwi.

ref: that his nephew Lot had been taken away. Three hundred and eighteen of Abram's servants were fighting men, so he took them and followed the enemy as far north as the city of Dan.
text: Amu ka Abram únu ka béƒie ɔnwani Lot bátɔlɔku ni, ɔ́lɔ nwuna kaduli nkyuli eti aku ku leevu-maane (

ref: That night, Abram divided up his troops, attacked from all sides, and won a great victory. But some of the enemy escaped to the town of Hobah north of Damascus,
text: Ka údu usien ni, Abram úle bekpone bamɛni na bétéyu-ma bátu, na békpoku-ma na bágya-ma bálɛku mmɔ ku usi útálɛ Hoba kalɔ lɛlɔ na Damasko katɔ kakɛ.

ref: and Abram went after them. He brought back his nephew Lot, together with Lot's possessions and the women and everyone else who had been captured. Abram Is Blessed by Melchizedek
text: Na ɔ́sɔ ɔ́ƒɔ Lot ku nwuna ala ani ƒui na béti békeliku mmɔ. Na ɔ́ƒɔ beleku ku bekyuli bamba bani ƒui na bákpanku bátɔlɔku mmɔ.

ref: Abram returned after he had defeated King Chedorlaomer and the other kings. Then the king of Sodom went to meet Abram in Shaveh Valley, which is also known as King's Valley.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ka Abram útádi Ɔga Kedorlaomer lɛlɔ ɔ́wɔlɔ ku baga bani na anwu ku-ma badi benwi mmɔ ni, Sodom ɔga úbo úbasiaku-nwu na ɔkwalɛ ɔni baalɔ Save kemmo na basɔ baalɔ mmɔ Ɔga Ɔkwalɛ.

ref: King Melchizedek of Salem was a priest of God Most High. He brought out some bread and wine
text: Amu na Katɔti ɔni Ntulo Ƒui ɔkpane ɔni baalɔ Melkisedek na ɔ́si Salem ɔma kemmo ni, údie bodobodo ku ntɛ bɔdi úte Abram.

ref: and said to Abram: “I bless you in the name of God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth.
text: Úvele kusɛ kusɛ úte-nwu ɔnini, “Katɔti Ɔni Ntulo Ƒui na anwu nédie katɔ ku kalɔ ni, ɔdubɔya na nwuna kusɛ kusɛ ɔsiaku Abram.

ref: All praise belongs to God Most High for helping you defeat your enemies.” Then Abram gave Melchizedek a tenth of everything.
text: Na kusɛ kusɛ kani beete Katɔti ɔni Ntulo Ƒui, ɔsika anwu néti ƒula bekisine ɔ́kya-a enu kemmo.” Amu na Abram úti ala ani ƒui na úƒie mmɔ ana lɛkpa leevudi úte ɔkpane ɔmɛni.

ref: The king of Sodom said to Abram, “All I want are my people. You can keep everything else.”
text: Sodom ɔga úvele Abram ɔnini, “Nyonu bekyuli bammɔ te-mi, na eti ala ammɔ ƒui.”

ref: Abram answered: The Lord God Most High made the heavens and the earth. And I have promised him
text: Nsu Abram údie kenye na ɔ́su Sodom ɔga ɔnini, “Katɔti ɔni Ntulo Ƒui na anwu nédie katɔ ku kalɔ ni, anwu na lɛkɔliku mina enu lite. Na lɛ́kpali ala lɛ́lɔ-nwu lɛnyi lɛ́su nnini,

ref: that I won't keep anything of yours, not even a sandal strap or a piece of thread. Then you can never say that you are the one who made me rich.
text: ‘Lɛɛɛdubo lɛla lɛni ndi awɔ lɛdi lɛdi bɔƒɔ ina ƒula lɛgbanvuo kabuli muedi.’ Na alasu anii ami ɔsu na Abram únwini ɔlakɛne.

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: Lɛɛɛdubo lɛla lɛdi bɔƒɔ butulo lɛla lɛni na mina bekyuli bamɛni bádi ku kadikɔ kani ndubuti lite ama bani násiaku-mi. Aka na ndubuti lite Ane ku Eskol ku Mamre bani na ami ku-ma bɔdi benwi.”

ref: Later the Lord spoke to Abram in a vision, “Abram, don't be afraid! I will protect you and reward you greatly.”
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Abram ɔ́nu kɔyu kemmo lɛla lɛdi na Katɔti ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Abram, sɔ tayia! Ami ndi ƒula katamikukɔ ku kesii kani na adubɔkɛ ƒula lɛyuƒɔla linengudi.”

ref: But Abram answered, “Lord All-Powerful, you have given me everything I could ask for, except children. And when I die, Eliezer of Damascus will get all I own.
text: Nsu Abram údie kenye ɔnini, “O Unwoti Ɔyikune Ƒui, bi na adubɔkana-mi bute ka liiibo ubi ɔni madi-mi ala muedi? Na Eleasa ɔni nási Damasko kani nkya-mi kudɔ.”

ref: You have not given me any children, and this servant of mine will inherit everything.”
text: Na Abram ɔ́sɔ ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ka étete-mi ubi ɔsu ni, kabla ɔblane ɔni nkya-mi kaduli ɔɔbla-mi kabla ni, anwu ndubo-mi ala bɔdi.”

ref: The Lord replied, “No, he won't! You will have a son of your own, and everything you have will be his.”
text: Amu na Katɔti ɔ́ya na Abram ɔ́ti ɔnini, “Ukyuli ɔmmɔ udu uladi-ɔ ala. Mmomu awɔ ɔlati kaƒɔ ubi ndubɔdi-ɔ ala.”

ref: Then the Lord took Abram outside and said, “Look at the sky and see if you can count the stars. That's how many descendants you will have.”
text: Mmɔ na ɔ́kpanku-nwu úsienku keyi na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Kɔliku anumi katɔ na anu kayaa na aka ayabi ani na aanu si adubɔkana-nya bɔka.” Amu na Katɔti ɔ́sɔ ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ƒula bawɔ lɛnɔ badubo bumomo lɛni kani.”

ref: Abram believed the Lord, so the Lord was pleased with him and accepted him. The Lord Makes Another Promise to Abram
text: Abram ɔ́ƒɔ Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́di, ɔmmɔ ɔsu na Katɔti ɔ́bu-nwu aani ukyuli ɔni nse siin.

ref: The Lord said to Abram, “I brought you here from Ur in Chaldea, and I gave you this land.”
text: Na Unwoti ɔ́sɔ ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ami ndi Unwoti Katɔti ɔni nákpanku-ɔ ɔ́siku Ur ɔma kemmo na Kaldea kalɔ lɛlɔ, kalɛ na liti kalɔ kamɛni lite-ɔ na kenwini awɔ kadi.”

ref: Abram asked, “Lord God, how can I know the land will be mine?”
text: Mmɔ na Abram ɔ́su ɔnini, “Unwoti Ɔyikune Ƒui, dɛ na mabla na mati ka kalɔ kamɛni ƒui kadubunwini ami kadi?”

ref: Then the Lord told him, “Bring me a three-year-old cow, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a dove, and a young pigeon.”
text: Amu na Unwoti ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Téti ɔmantɛ akɔ uledi ku ese ku koƒonu kɔgyani bani náaƒɔ akwa atɛ atɛ boku-mi. Na eboku abloduma ku lɛmangbɛ.”

ref: Abram obeyed the Lord. Then he cut the animals in half and laid the two halves of each animal opposite each other on the ground. But he did not cut the doves and pigeons in half.
text: Mmɔ ni, Abram útéti ala amɛni úboku. Ka ɔ́du-ma ni, úle ɔdi biala kemmo inyɔ na use kɔkɛ kɔmmɔ ɔ́sala. Na baasɛ bammɔ ama ni, útele-ma kemmo lɛni.

ref: And when birds came down to eat the animals, Abram chased them away.
text: Na ka baasɛ bamba béveyi bébo bábadi lɛna lɛmmɔ ni, Abram ɔ́gya-ma.

ref: As the sun was setting, Abram fell into a deep sleep, and everything became dark and frightening.
text: Ka ɔƒɛ úsela bɔnu ni, Abram úduo lɛlɛɛ kemmo úsie ɔgyu. Na kadi kábla suu na liƒu lɛ́da-nwu geen.

ref: Four generations later, your descendants will return here and take this land, because only then will the people who live here be so sinful that they deserve to be punished.
text: Uwi ɔni na ƒula bawɔ mmɔ bulie búdu akwa ammɔ ni, badubɔsɔ benwini bebo mɛni. Ɔsika Amoriƒuɔ ekpidi ani baabla ni, étedu tɔ kesii kani na lɛni-ma kɔtu kɔni meduku-ma.”

ref: Sometime after sunset, when it was very dark, a smoking cooking pot and a flaming fire passed between the two halves of each animal.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ka ɔƒɛ ɔ́nu na kadi kábla suu ni, libui bonwu lɛdi ku uyɔ lɛni kemmo na ɔgya ɔɔda lɛdi lɛ́da lɛ́lɛ. Libui lɛmɛni lɛkɛ lɛ́vi lɛna lɛmmɔ ƒui ntɛ.

ref: At that time the Lord made an agreement with Abram and told him: I will give your descendants the land east of the Shihor River on the border of Egypt as far as the Euphrates River.
text: Limeyi lɛmmɔ ni, Unwoti únyimi lɛla lɛdi ku Abram. Na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Líti kalɔ kamɛni ƒui muute ƒula bawɔ. Na ɔ́si ketu blii kani nkya Egyipte ɔmii útédu Euƒrate Ketu bonwu líti muute-ma.

ref: They will possess the land of the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites,
text: Na Keniƒuɔ ku Kenisiƒuɔ kalɔ ku Kadmoniƒuɔ

ref: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaites,
text: ku Hetiƒuɔ ku Perisiƒuɔ ku Reƒaiƒuɔ

ref: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
text: ku Amoriƒuɔ ku Kanaanƒuɔ ku Girgasiƒuɔ ku Yebusiƒuɔ kɔlɔ ƒui na líti muute-ma.”

ref: Abram's wife Sarai had not been able to have any children. But she owned a young Egyptian slave woman named Hagar,
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Abram ulekudi Sarai útawɔ ubi ɔdi útete-nwu tɔ. Nsu Egyipteni ulekubi ɔdi ɔ́kya-nwu kudɔ útaabla-nwu kabla ɔni baalɔ Hagar.

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu Sarai ɔ́su Abram ɔnini, “Unwoti ɔ́ya na naaawɔ. Anwuɔsu sie mina ulekubi kabla ɔblane kudɔ na bɛtɛ. Ɔƒɛni kalɛ ndubudie kaba bɔsiku nwuna bebi bani na ɔdubɔwɔ kemmo.” Abram lɛnɔ únu lɛla lɛni na Sarai ɔ́su-nwu kalɔ.

ref: and Sarai gave him Hagar to be his wife. This happened after Abram had lived in the land of Canaan for ten years.
text: Anwuɔsu ɔni na Abram úulie Kanaan kalɔ lɛlɔ aani akwa leevu kama ni, ulekudi Sarai úti Hagar nwuna Egyipteni ulekubi kabla ɔblane úte ɔgyani ɔnii uti-nwu aani ulekudi kalɛ.

ref: Later, when Hagar knew she was going to have a baby, she became proud and treated Sarai hatefully.
text: Na ka anwu ku Hagar bátɛ ni, ɔ́da kaƒɔ. Uwi ɔni na Hagar ɔ́nu ɔ́ti ka ɔ́ɔda kaƒɔ ni, ɔsɔ ulakpete nwuna kɔduni.

ref: Then Sarai said to Abram, “It's all your fault! I gave you my slave woman, but she has been hateful to me ever since she found out she was pregnant. You have done me wrong, and you will have to answer to the Lord for this.”
text: Mmɔ ni, Sarai ɔ́su Abram ɔnini, “Ukyuli tabu ala ani na Hagar ɔɔbla-mi ni, awɔ na lɛ́si. Ami néti mina kaduli kabla ɔblane ulekubi líte-ɔ. Na ka ɔ́nu ɔ́ti ka ɔ́ɔda kaƒɔ ɔsu ni, ɔsɔ ulábu-mi. Unwoti kani ndubuye litemi lɛmɛni na ami ku awɔ ntɛ.”

ref: Abram said, “All right! She's your slave—do whatever you want with her.” Then Sarai began treating Hagar so harshly that she finally ran away.
text: Amu na Abram ɔ́su Sarai ɔnini, “Ƒula ubi ulekubi na ɔdi ɔsu lɛla lɛni na ánu ka lɛ́manku ni, na ablaku-nwu.” Anwuɔsu Sarai úse kalɔ ɔɔbla-nwu ulikplu ala. Na ɔmmɔ ɔsu ulekubi ɔmmɔ úkeli-nwu kudɔ.

ref: Hagar stopped to rest at a spring in the desert on the road to Shur. While she was there, the angel of the Lord came to her
text: Ka úkeli ni, Katɔti ɔtu ɔdi ɔ́nu Hagar na libiɔ lɛdi kɔtu na ketele kemmo kesii kadi. Na libiɔ lɛmmɔ ndi libiɔ lɛni nkya ulu kudɔ si éti ésela ɔma ɔni baalɔ Sur kemmo.

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: Mmɔ na ɔ́kpali-nwu ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Hagar, Sarai ubi ulekubi, wɛ na ási na wɛ na ésela?” Amu na Hagar údie kenye ɔnini, “Bukeli na nda mina kɔduni Sarai kudɔ.”

ref: The angel said, “Go back to Sarai and be her slave.
text: Amu na Katɔti ɔtu ɔmmɔ ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Sɔ nwini teesie ƒula kɔduni kudɔ na eboku lɛlɔ kalɔ ete-nwu.”

ref: I will give you a son, who will be called Ishmael, because I have heard your cry for help. And someday I will give you so many descendants that no one will be able to count them all.
text: Na ɔ́sɔ ɔ́su-nwu lɛnɔ ɔnini, “Ada kaƒɔ ɔƒaani, na adubo ɔnanabi bɔwɔ. Na adubo-nwu lɛnyi bɔsia anii Ismael. Ɔsika Unwoti únu ƒula kɔnyana kɔni na eewulo.

ref: But your son will live far from his relatives; he will be like a wild donkey, fighting everyone, and everyone fighting him.”
text: Ɔdubo bɔbla ukyuli gyegyengye ɔni ndubɔkɛ utaavi aani alakpi akɔ ebuo ala utine kalɛ. Bekyuli ala aláyɔ-nwu kaƒɔ na nwuna ala lɛnɔ aláyɔ bekyuli kaƒɔ. Na anwu ku nwuna banwani beléduku na baaadubo bɔsala bulie liku linwi.”

ref: Hagar thought, “Have I really seen God and lived to tell about it?” So from then on she called him, “The God Who Sees Me.”
text: Amu na ɔ́sia Unwoti ɔni nátɛmiku-nwu lɛnyi ɔnini, “Awɔ ndi Katɔti ɔni mɔɔnu-mi. Ɔsika ɔƒɛni ni, lɛ́nu awɔ ɔni mɔɔnu-mi.”

ref: That's why people call the well between Kadesh and Bered, “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu na básia libiɔ lɛmmɔ lɛnyi banii Beer-Lahar-Roi. Na libiɔ lɛmmɔ lɛmu lɛkya Kades ku Bared lɛma ntɛ.

ref: I will keep my solemn promise to you and give you more descendants than can be counted.”
text: Na lɛla lɛni na muunyimiku-ɔ mɛni ni, ndubɔlɛ-ni ugyii lite-ɔ na adubɔwɔ bebi na bemomo kaduɛ.”

ref: Abram bowed with his face to the ground, and God said:
text: Mmɔ ni, Abram ɔ́yu úbi kalɔ na Katɔti ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini,

ref: I promise that you will be the father of many nations. So now I'm changing your name from Abram to Abraham.
text: Ɔmɛni ɔsu ni, basɔ baaadubo-ɔ bɔlɔ banii Abram, ɔni kalɔ ndi Ati ɔni náƒɔ lɛnyi. Mmomu badubɔlɔ-ɔ banii Abraham, ɔsika líti-ɔ lɛ́bla kadi bekyuli pii ɔti.

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.
text: Na ndubɔya na ƒula bawɔ bemomo na benwini kadi pii bekyuli na badi benwini baga muedi.

ref: I will always keep the promise I have made to you and your descendants, because I am your God and their God.
text: Ndubɔlɛ bunyimi bɔmɛni ugyii. Na amu ndubɔkya ami ku-ɔ ntɛ ku ƒula bawɔ ku kaba bekyuli bani ƒui masia-a kama uwi ƒui. Na ami ndi ƒula Katɔti na madi ama lɛnɔ Katɔti.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Kanaan kalɔ kani lɛlɔ na adi ɔƒɔ mɛni ni, ndubuti-ka lite awɔ ku ƒula bawɔ na ami ndubɔbla bana Katɔti.”

ref: Abraham, you and all future members of your family must promise to obey me.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Katɔti ɔ́su Abraham ɔnini, “Awɔ ni, ɔlɛka adi mina bunyimi lɛlɔ, awɔ ku ƒula bawɔ ku kaba bekyuli bani ƒui masia-a kama.

ref: As the sign that you are keeping this promise, you must circumcise every man and boy in your family.
text: Ago bukulo bɔmmɔ ndubɔtina bunyimi bɔni nkya ami ku-mii ntɛ.

ref: Any man who isn't circumcised hasn't kept his part of the promise and cannot be one of my people.
text: Ɔnana biala na útekulo tɔ lego ni, ɔdubukulo kɔna kɔni nkya anwu ku mina bekyuli ntɛ, ɔsika útadi mina bunyimi lɛlɔ.”

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Katɔti ɔ́sɔ ɔ́su Abraham ɔnini, “Ƒ'ulekudi Sarai ni, ɔƒaamu badubɔlɔ-nwu Sara.

ref: I will bless her, and you will have a son by her. She will become the mother of nations, and some of her descendants will even be kings.
text: Ndubute-nwu kusɛ kusɛ. Na ɔdi litemi geen ka anwu ɔlati ɔdubɔwɔ ɔnanabi ute-ɔ. Ndubute-nwu kusɛ kusɛ ɔni na ɔdubɔya na unwini kaba bekyuli pii uni. Na baga bani madi bekyuli lɛlɔ badubɔlɛ basiku-nwu lɛlɔ kemmo.”

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Amu na Abraham ɔ́su Katɔti ɔnini, “Si adubute Ismael kusɛ kusɛ na ulie ngba ni, nka ɔdia!”

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Mmɔ na Katɔti ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ɛɛn! Nsu ƒ'ulekudi Sara ɔdubɔwɔ ɔnanabi ute-ɔ. Na adubɔsia-nwu lɛnyi anii Isak. Ndubɔlɛ mina bunyimi ugyii ku anwu ɔmmɔ aani bunyimi bɔni makya ami ku-nwu ntɛ usela uwi ƒui ku nwuna bawɔ lɛnɔ ntɛ.

ref: However, I have heard what you asked me to do for Ishmael, and so I will also bless him with many descendants. He will be the father of twelve princes, and I will make his family a great nation.
text: Na Ismael anwu ni, línu lɛla lɛni na éye-mi. Sɔsɔ ni, ndubute-nwu kusɛ kusɛ na lɛya na ɔwɔ bebi pii na bemomo geen. Anwu ndubɔdi lɛlɔ badine leevu-inyɔ ɔti. Na ndubɔya na nwuna kadi bekyuli bakɛ ɔyikun geen.

ref: But your son Isaac will be born about this time next year, and the promise I am making to you and your family will be for him and his descendants forever.
text: Nsu mina bunyimi ni, ami ku Isak bawɔ ntɛ na ndubɔlɛ-mu ugyii. Na anwu ndi ubi ɔni na Sara ɔdubɔwɔ ute-ɔ na lɛkwa lɛni ndubo aani uwi ɔmɛni kalɛ.”

ref: God finished speaking to Abraham and then left.
text: Na ka Katɔti ɔ́wɔlɔ Abraham bɔtɛmiku ni, ɔ́tɔlɔ úsie katɔ.

ref: On that same day Abraham obeyed God by circumcising Ishmael. Abraham was also circumcised, and so were all the other men and boys in his household, including his servants and slaves. He was
text: Na lɛni kani na bananabi bani ƒui nkya Abraham kaduli ku bani mɔɔbla-nwu kabla, anwu ku ama ƒui békulo ago.

ref: One hot summer afternoon while Abraham was sitting by the entrance to his tent near the sacred trees of Mamre, the Lord appeared to him.
text: Na uwi ɔdi kama ni, Unwoti údie lɛlɔ ɔ́tina Abraham na Mamre egyii enengudi kudɔ uwi ɔni na ulie nwuna kevo kenye ka kadi kábi ɔsu.

ref: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. He quickly ran to meet them, bowed with his face to the ground,
text: Mmɔ ni, Abraham ɔ́talaku anumi na ɔ́nu nkyuli ɛtɛ badi ka benye-nwu kɔmadɔ. Ka ɔ́nu-ma kani na ɔ́babla na ɔ́ta kesii kani na úlie mmɔ útásiaku-ma ulu na úboku lili kalɔ bana anumi.

ref: and said, “Please come to my home where I can serve you.
text: Amu na ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ati, si ánu-mi áti ka lɛ́manku ni, tebo tavi ami ƒula kabla ɔblane.

ref: I'll have some water brought, so you can wash your feet, then you can rest under a tree.
text: Ya na beboku ntu bɔdi na bina ƒui bɛƒɔ lɛgba na bɛnwuna na kugyii kɔmɛni kalɔ.

ref: Let me get you some food to give you strength before you leave. I would be honored to serve you.” “Thank you very much,” they answered. “We accept your offer.”
text: Na aya na lɛwia lɛla lɛdi lite-mii na bɛdi kalɛ na bɛsɔ bɛkɛ ɔlɔkplu na biteesie bina ulu ka ami ƒula kabla ɔblane na bíbeduo.” Amu na ama lɛnɔ banini, “Ƒaa, bla kalɛ na aayɔ́.”

ref: Abraham went quickly to his tent and said to Sarah, “Hurry! Get a large sack of flour and make some bread.”
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Abraham úkeli úsie ulekudi Sara kudɔ na kevo kemmo na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Babla na ama ɔlaaƒɔ lɛɛƒɛɛ ɛtɛ na anwu na eƒi bodobodo ɔdi.”

ref: After saying this, he rushed off to his herd of cattle and picked out one of the best calves, which his servant quickly prepared.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, úkeli úsie nwuna bebuo akɔ na útáƒuna ɔmantɛ akɔ ɔkɔdi ɔdi úte nwuna kabla ɔblane ɔnii utádu-nwu na ɔbla-nwu kaduɛ uboku.

ref: He then served his guests some yogurt and milk together with the meat. While they were eating, he stood near them under the tree,
text: Amu na bábla adila adi béboku-ma ku bebuo anyi ntu ku lɛna lɛni bágyi mmɔ na béti béte-ma banii badi. Na uwi ɔni na belie baadi ni, Abraham ɔ́sɔ únye-ma kudɔ mmɔ na kugyii kalɔ.

ref: and they asked, “Where's your wife Sarah?” “She is right there in the tent,” Abraham answered.
text: Amu na béye-nwu banini, “Wɛ na ƒ'ulekudi Sara ɔkya?” Údie kenye ɔnini, “Ɔkya kevo kemmo ngye.”

ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Ɔƒɛni Abraham ku ulekudi Sara ƒui ni, bana akwa éesie lɛtɔtɔ geen ɔsu báabi. Na Sara lɛnɔ ɔ́ɔvi uwi ɔni na nka ɔkana kaƒɔ bɔda.

ref: So she laughed and said to herself, “Now that I am worn out and my husband is old, will I really know such happiness?”
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Sara ɔ́ma na ɔ́su lɛlɔ ɔnini, “Ɔni na ami ku mina ɔtɔlati bɔ́ɔbi mɛni ni, ɔƒɛni na bɔdubo ebiti amɛni bɔkɛ?”

ref: The Lord asked Abraham, “Why did Sarah laugh? Does she doubt that she can have a child in her old age?
text: Amu na Unwoti úye Abraham ɔnini, “Bi ɔsu na Sara ɔ́ma na úye ɔnii, ‘Sɔsɔ na ndubɔsɔ lɛkana kaƒɔ bɔda ɔƒɛni?’

ref: I am the Lord! There is nothing too difficult for me. I'll come back next year at the time I promised, and Sarah will already have a son.”
text: Bɛɛkali bɛnii lɛla lɛdi libo ɔkplu litulo Unwoti ka ɔkana bɔbla? Ndubɔsɔ linwini libo-mii kudɔ na uwi ɔni námanku lɛlɔ na lɛkwa lɛni ndubo. Na Sara ɔdubɔwɔ ɔnanabi.”

ref: Sarah was so frightened that she lied and said, “I didn't laugh.” “Yes, you did!” he answered. Abraham Prays for Sodom
text: Ka Sara ɔ́yia ɔsu ni, ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ńtama!” Nsu Unwoti ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Sɔsɔ na ɔdi ka áma.” Abraham Úvele Na Sodom Lili

ref: When the three men got ready to leave, they looked down toward Sodom, and Abraham walked part of the way with them.
text: Amu uwi ɔni na bekyuli bammɔ báta banii baatɔlɔ ni, bénwini bánu bésienku kada kakɛ kesii kani na Sodom ɔma uwi, na Abraham lɛnɔ ɔkɛ ɔsiaku-ma kalɛ na use-ma ulu.

ref: The Lord said to himself, “I should tell Abraham what I am going to do,
text: Mmɔ ni, Unwoti úye lɛlɔ ɔnini, “Ɔdia si líti lɛla lɛni na nnii mɔɔbla lɛ́bala Abraham?

ref: since his family will become a great and powerful nation that will be a blessing to all the other nations on earth.
text: Abraham ɔdubo bɔwɔ na nwuna kadi bekyuli baƒɔ lɛnyi na bakɛ ɔyikun. Na ɔkɛnku Abraham ɔmɛni lɛlɔ ɔsu na ndubute kayawi kadi ƒui bekyuli kusɛ kusɛ.

ref: I have chosen him to teach his family to obey me forever and to do what is right and fair. If they do, I will give Abraham many descendants, just as I promised.”
text: Ɔsika ami náƒuna-nwu kalɛ na ɔtina nwuna bebi kavikɔ na nwuna kadi bekyuli bani masia-nwu kama ƒui basia ulu ɔni na mɔɔyɔ́ lɛlɔ. Na bataabla ala ani ndia na akɛ ulu lɛlɔ. Na ɔkɛnku ɔmmɔ lɛlɔ na ndubuti lɛla lɛni na lɛ́su líse ka mete Abraham lite-nwu.”

ref: ?” “Because of them, I won't destroy the city,” was the Lord's answer.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Unwoti ɔ́su Abraham ɔnini, “Muunú likyulikpi lɛni na bekyuli bani nkya Sodom ku Gomora baabla. Bana ekpidi ávi ntu.

ref: Now I am going down to see for myself if those people really are that bad. If they aren't, I would like to know.”
text: Anwuɔsu ndubɔyu lisie ntánu si sɔsɔ na baabla ala ani na muunú mmɔ. Na si uuuse lɛni ni, na lɛti.”

ref: The men turned and started toward Sodom. But the Lord stayed with Abraham.
text: Amu na bekyuli bammɔ bátu bénwini bésie Sodom kakɛ, nsu Abraham anwu ɔ́sɔ únye mmɔ ɔɔnu Unwoti anumi.

ref: And Abraham asked him, “Lord, when you destroy the evil people, are you also going to destroy those who are good?
text: Mmɔ ni, Abraham ɔ́ni úde Unwoti na úye-nwu ɔnini, “Buvele, adubɔyila bekyuli babuni udu lɛnɔ ebunku bekyuli bekpidi bammɔ?

ref: Wouldn't you spare the city if there are only
text: Si nkyuli liti badi bekyuli babuni na ɔma unengudi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, adubɔyila ɔma ɔmmɔ udu?

ref: You surely wouldn't let them be killed when you destroy the evil ones. You are the judge of all the earth, and you do what is right.”
text: Ńtaƒɔ ńtadi ka adubɔbla lɛni, na adu bekyuli babuni ku bekpidi ɔni mɔɔtina ka ábla bekyuli babuni ku bekpidi ulikplu likeke. Ɔmmɔ udu anwu ni, ngyi nwaa ka awɔ ɔni mɔɔtɛku kayawi ƒui litemi adubɔbla lɛla lɛni ndi ulu.”

ref: Abraham answered, “I am nothing more than the dust of the earth. Please forgive me, Lord, for daring to speak to you like this.
text: Ka ɔ́su lɛni ni, Abraham ɔ́sɔ údie kenye ɔnini, “Unwoti, ngyi ka líse kayi geen ina ka lɛti ku ntɔ kani na ndi asa muuye-ɔ ala amɛni.

ref: Then Abraham said, “I don't have any right to ask you, Lord, but what would you do if you find only
text: Mmɔ ni, Abraham ɔ́sɔ ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ngyi ka líise kayi na ndaku-ɔ ala amɛni udu buvele. Na si banii nkyuli aavlanyɔ kani nkya mmɔ nɔɔ?” Unwoti ɔnini, “Na nkyuli aavlanyɔ ɔsu ni, lɛɛɛdubo ɔma ɔmmɔ bɔkyula.”

ref: Finally, Abraham said, “Please don't get angry, Lord, if I speak just once more. Suppose you find only
text: Abraham ɔ́sɔ úvele ɔmmɔ kama ɔnini, “Unwoti, muuvele-ɔ nnii teti kɔnyi takya-mi, ya na lɛsɔ livele lɛla linwi. Si banii nkyuli leevu kani nkya mmɔ nɔɔ?” Unwoti ɔnini, “Na nkyuli leevu ɔsu ni, lɛɛɛdubo mmɔ bɔkyula.”

ref: After speaking with Abraham, the Lord left, and Abraham went back home.
text: Uwi ɔni na Unwoti ɔ́su Abraham ɔmɛni ɔ́wɔlɔ ni, ɔ́tɔlɔ. Na Abraham únwini úsie kaduli.

ref: That evening, while Lot was sitting near the city gate, the two angels arrived in Sodom. When Lot saw them, he got up, bowed down low,
text: Na ɔmmɔ kama ni, Unwoti batu inyɔ bammɔ bébeduo Sodom useyi uwi. Na Lot ulie ɔma ɔmmɔ kabanye na ɔban ɔmmɔ kayakun. Ka ɔ́nu-ma ni, ɔ́ta útásiaku-ma na úboku lili kalɔ.

ref: and said, “Gentlemen, I am your servant. Please come to my home. You can wash your feet, spend the night, and be on your way in the morning.” They told him, “No, we'll spend the night in the city square.”
text: Mmɔ na ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ati bene, buvele binwini bibo-mi kaduli mɛni na bibaƒɔ bina lɛgba na bɛtɛ-mi kudɔ. Na si kadi kégye ni, na bɛsɔ bɛyu ulu biteesie ƒaa.” Amu na bédie kenye banini, “Oowo, kalɔ ntɛ na kadi kasakɔ, na bɔdubo bunye usien umodi.”

ref: But Lot kept insisting, until they finally agreed and went home with him. He quickly baked some bread, cooked a meal, and they ate.
text: Nsu Lot únye-ma lɛlɔ kpakplaa útédu ka ɔ́ya na bánwa adila béte-ma. Na úƒi bodobodo ɔni kemmo na bɔnyii buuubo béte-ma na bádi.

ref: Before Lot and his guests could go to bed, every man in Sodom, young and old, came and stood outside his house
text: Ɔƒɛni asa na badubusie batátɛ ni, Sodom ɔma ɔmmɔ banana, banamu ku benengu ƒui, báasi ɔma ɔmmɔ ekpo ekpo ƒui bábaka bámana kaduli kammɔ.

ref: and started shouting, “Where are your visitors? Send them out, so we can have sex with them!”
text: Amu na bákpali Lot beye-nwu banini, “Ntele bekyuli bani néduo-ɔ. Kpanku-ma boku na abu ku-ma bɔtɛ aani ɔnana ku uleku.”

ref: Lot went outside and shut the door behind him.
text: Mmɔ ni, Lot ɔ́vi ɔlɛ útásiaku-ma na keyi, na ɔ́kalɛ ɔ́da-ma.

ref: Then he said, “Friends, please don't do such a terrible thing!
text: Na ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Bakpana, bitabla kalala kamɛni udu.

ref: I have two daughters who have never had sex. I'll bring them out, and you can do what you want with them. But don't harm these men. They are guests in my home.”
text: Bɛnu, mbo belekubi inyɔ bani nétesie tɔ ɔnana ɔdi kudɔ kudii. Bɛya na lɛkpanku-ma liboku-mii na bɛblaku-ma lɛla biala na bɛɛyɔ́. Na bekyuli bamɛni ama ni, bitabla-ma lɛla lɛdi. Ɔsika ami na bébeduo na ɔlɛka lɛgyumana-ma.”

ref: “Don't get in our way,” the crowd answered. “You're a foreigner. What right do you have to order us around? We'll do worse things to you than we're going to do to them.” The crowd kept arguing with Lot. Finally, they rushed toward the door to break it down.
text: Amu na Sodom banana bammɔ básu-nwu banini, “Gyu lɛgba bula anumi.” Na básu lɛnɔ banini, “Ɔƒɔ na Lot ɔmɛni ɔdi na úbeduo-bu, na ɔƒɛni amu úti lɛlɔ ɔ́bla bula litemi ɔtɛne. Bɔdubo awɔ ɔlati ɔmmɔ lɛla lɛdi bɔbla butulo bekyuli bani néduo-ɔ si átanu kaduɛ!” Na bénye-nwu lɛlɔ geen ɔni na baayɔ́ babwɛ kayakun kammɔ beduo ɔtɔ muedi.

ref: But the two angels in the house reached out and pulled Lot safely inside.
text: Mmɔ ni, Lot baƒɔ bammɔ báni Lot béduoku ɔtɔ na bákalɛ.

ref: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
text: Na báya na ogya ɔdi ɔ́bwɛ bekyuli bani ƒui nnye keyi mmɔ anumi na básɔ bátanu kayakun na beduo ɔtɔ ɔmmɔ kemmo.

ref: Lot went to the men who were engaged to his daughters and said, “Hurry up and get out of here! The Lord is going to destroy this city.” But they thought he was joking, and they laughed at him.
text: Ka básu-nwu lɛni ni, Lot ɔ́ta ɔ́lɛ na útásu nwuna bebi bagya bani néebo bébevele-nwu ka beti nwuna bebi. Na ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Ɔbabla-mii na bɛvi bɛlɛ ɔma ɔmɛni kemmo pla. Ɔsika Katɔti ɔdubo ɔma ɔmɛni bɔkyula.” Nsu nwuna bebi bagya bamɛni béti lɛla lɛmɛni bákya bɔƒia.

ref: Early the next morning the two angels tried to make Lot hurry and leave. They said, “Take your wife and your two daughters and get away from here as fast as you can! If you don't, every one of you will be killed when the Lord destroys the city.”
text: Amu ka kadi kadubo kegyekɔ ni, batu inyɔ bammɔ básu Lot kpakplaa banini, “Babla na akpanku ƒ'ulekudi ku ƒula bebi belekubi inyɔ bani nkya-a kudɔ mɛni alɛku ɔma ɔmmɔ kemmo. Si ɔɔɔdi lɛni amii lɛnɔ bɛdubo bɔkywi si Katɔti ɔɔkyula ɔma ɔmmɔ.”

ref: At first, Lot just stood there. But the Lord wanted to save him. So the angels took Lot, his wife, and his two daughters by the hand and led them out of the city.
text: Ka Lot unye ɔɔní litemi lɛmɛni teenene ni, batu bammɔ báda-nwu kɔnu ku ulekudi lɛnɔ enu ku nwuna bebi belekubi inyɔ bammɔ, ɔsika Katɔti ɔɔyɔ́ ka udie-ma na bákpanku-ma bálɛku ɔma unengudi ɔmmɔ kemmo.

ref: When they were outside, one of the angels said, “Run for your lives! Don't even look back. And don't stop in the valley. Run to the hills, where you'll be safe.”
text: Ka bálɛ ɔma ɔmmɔ kemmo kani na batu bammɔ kemmo unwi ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Ɔdi biala ukeli momoomo na udie lɛlɔ na ɔgbankpi ɔni ndubo bɔlɛ kemmo. Na ɔdi utenwini utanu kama. Na ɔdi lɛnɔ utasu ɔnii uudí unye na kalɔ ntɛ mmɔ kesii kadi. Bikeli siin biteesie abi lɛlɔ. Si ɔɔɔdi lɛni bɛdubo bɔkywi!”

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: Ami aani bina kabla ɔblane kalɛ ni, mina ala áƒɔ-mi anu. Na bɛ́nu-mi kɔnyana geen. Na bidie-mi ngba na ɔgbankpi kemmo. Nsu naaakana bukeli busie abi lɛlɔ. Amu ɔgbankpi ɔmɛni ɔdububo ubaka-mi ɔkya na likpi.

ref: There's a town near here. It's only a small place, but my family and I will be safe, if you let us go there.”
text: Bɛɛnu ɔma ɔni baalɔ Soar na nwuna kalɔ ndi ubliidi ka nde mɛni. Buvele, bitakyula-nwu. Bɛya na likeli lisie mmɔ. Munuu ulatɛ?”

ref: Hurry! Run! I can't do anything until you are safely there.” The town was later called Zoar because Lot had said it was small. Sodom and Gomorrah Are Destroyed
text: Mmomu bikeli bisie mmɔ pla, ɔsika naaakana lɛla lɛdi bɔbla wetédu uwi ɔni na bíkeli bísie mmɔ.” Ɔmmɔ ɔsu na baalɔ ɔma ɔmmɔ banii Soar.

ref: The sun was coming up as Lot reached the town of Zoar,
text: Ka Lot údu Soar ni, ɔƒɛ ɔmmɔ ɔ́ɔkuku geen na mmɔ.

ref: He destroyed those cities and everyone who lived in them, as well as their land and the trees and grass that grew there.
text: Ulu ɔmɛni lɛlɔ na Katɔti ɔ́sia na ɔ́kyula lɛma lɛmmɔ ku bekyuli bani ƒui nli mmɔ ku bana kalɔ ku aku ku edula ƒui.

ref: On the way, Lot's wife looked back and was turned into a block of salt.
text: Uwi ɔni na ala amɛni aalɛ́ ni, Lot ulekudi únwini ɔ́nu kama. Amu na únwini mma lɛbanta bonwu linye ulu ntɛ.

ref: That same morning Abraham got up and went to the place where he had stood and spoken with the Lord.
text: Limeyi lɛni nsia ala amɛni bɔlɛ kama ɔtanta tutuutu ni, Abraham ɔ́ta úténye likpo lɛni na únye Katɔti anumi úvele-nwu na ɔma ɔmɛni lili.

ref: He looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah and saw smoke rising from all over the land—it was like a flaming furnace.
text: Únye mmɔ na ɔɔnu kalɛ na uyɔ ɔɔkɔ́ taasi Sodom ku Gomora ku kalɔ kani nwi mmɔ ƒui lɛlɔ aani kalɛ na uyɔ ɔɔkɔ́ taalɛ ɔgya livongu bonwu na lɛyu bonwu lɛdi kemmo.

ref: When God destroyed the cities of the valley where Lot lived, he remembered his promise to Abraham and saved Lot from the terrible destruction. Moab and Ammon
text: Anwuɔsu uwi ɔni na Katɔti ɔɔkyula lɛma linengudi lɛmɛni ni, únyimo Abraham lɛla lɛni na úvele-nwu. Anwuɔsu údie Lot na ɔgbankpi ɔni na Katɔti ɔ́ya na úbakyula ɔma ɔni kemmo na Lot ulie mmɔ kemmo. Lot Ku Nwuna Bebi Belekubi

ref: Lot was afraid to stay on in Zoar. So he took his two daughters and moved to a cave in the hill country.
text: Na ala amɛni bɔlɛ kama ni, Lot ku nwuna bebi belekubi bátɔlɔ Soar mmɔ bésie bétélie abi lɛlɔ. Ɔsika ɔɔɔgyi kalɛ na welie Soar mmɔ. Na anwu ku nwuna bebi belekubi bammɔ bákya atabi ubu kemmo.

ref: One day his older daughter said to her sister, “Our father is old, and there are no men anywhere for us to marry.
text: Limeyi lɛdi ni, ulekubi unengudi ɔ́su ɔnwani ɔnamudi ɔnini, “Bula ɔti ɔ́ɔbi lɛlɔ na ɔnana ɔdi uuubo mɛni na bulieku-nwu na bula ɔdi kenye.

ref: Let's get our father drunk! Then we can sleep with him and have children.”
text: Ɔmɛni ɔsu ni, ya na bute bula ɔti ntɛ na ɔni ukpi na lisie ntátɛ-nwu kudɔ kalɛ na bula ɔti kaba kalayii.”

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and the older daughter got in bed with him, but he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Anwuɔsu usien ɔmmɔ ni, báya na ɔti ɔ́ni ntɛ úkpi na ulekubi unengudi útátɛ ɔti kudɔ. Ɔti útati uwi ɔni na ubidi ulekubi úbatɛ-nwu kudɔ ku uwi ɔni na ɔ́ta ɔ́tɔlɔ.

ref: The next day the older daughter said to her sister, “I slept with my father last night. We'll get him drunk again tonight, so you can sleep with him, and we can each have a child.”
text: Limeyi lɛni nsia ni, ulekubi unengudi ɔ́su ɔnwani ɔnamudi ɔnini, “Usien ɔni návi ni, ńtátɛ bula ɔti kudɔ. Anwuɔsu ya na bɔsɔ bute-nwu ntɛ na ɔ́ni ukpi ɔmwi lɛnɔ na awɔ lɛnɔ esie na atátɛ-nwu kudɔ ɔmwi usien kalɛ na bula ɔti kaba kalayii.”

ref: That night they got their father drunk, and this time the younger sister slept with him. But once again he was too drunk even to know she was there.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu béte bana ɔti ntɛ na ɔ́ni-mu úkpi usien ɔmmɔ lɛnɔ. Na ulekubi ɔnamudi lɛnɔ úsie útátɛ bana ɔti kudɔ. Na ɔƒɛni lɛnɔ ni, bana ɔti útati uwi ɔni na ulekubi ɔmmɔ úbatɛ-nwu kudɔ iye ɔ́ta ɔ́tɔlɔ.

ref: That's how Lot's two daughters had children.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Lot bebi belekubi inyɔ bammɔ ƒui bábada kaƒɔ béte bana ɔti.

ref: The older daughter named her son Moab, and he is the ancestor of the Moabites.
text: Na ulekubi unengudi úbawɔ ɔnanabi na ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Moab, na anwu ndi Moab kadi bekyuli ƒui ɔti.

ref: The younger daughter named her son Benammi, and he is the ancestor of the Ammonites.
text: Na ulekubi ɔnamudi lɛnɔ úbawɔ ɔnanabi na ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Ben-Ami na anwu ndi Amon kadi bekyuli bani na bakya ɔmwi ƒui ɔti.

ref: he told everyone that his wife Sarah was his sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar had Sarah brought to him.
text: Mmɔ ka Abraham údie ulekudi Sara ɔ́tina bekyuli bammɔ ni, ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Mina ɔnwani ulekubi na ɔdi.” Ɔmmɔ ɔsu Abimelek ɔni ndi Gerar ɔma ɔga ɔ́tala na bátákpanku Sara béboku-nwu.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”
text: Nsu Katɔti údie lɛlɔ ɔtina-nwu na kɔna kemmo limeyi lɛdi usien na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Uleku ɔni na áƒɔ eenwíni awɔ ɔdi mɛni ɔsu ni, si ékpi muedi ɔdia-a utulo. Ɔsika uleku ɔni nli kɔgya na ɔdi.”

ref: Abimelech said to the Lord, “Don't kill me! I haven't slept with Sarah. Didn't they say they were brother and sister? I am completely innocent.”
text: Ɔɔɔdi Abraham ɔlati násu-mi ɔnini, ‘Mina ɔnwani ulekubi na ɔdi’? Lɛla lɛni na lɛ́bla ni, mina kayi kaladu-mi kɔtɔ. Na aanu-mi enu na katɔ.”
