ref: Then God continued: I know you are innocent. That's why I kept you from sleeping with Sarah and doing anything wrong.
text: Amu na Katɔti ɔ́su-nwu na kɔna kemmo ɔnini, “Sɔsɔ na ɔdi, ngyi ka lɛla lɛni na ábla ni, ƒula kayi kaladu-ɔ kɔtɔ. Ɔmmɔ ɔsu na ńtaya na ablaku-nwu likpidi lɛdi iye etuku-nwu na aƒiɔ-mi.

ref: Her husband is a prophet. Let her go back to him, and his prayers will save you from death. But if you don't return her, you and all your people will die.
text: Anwuɔsu ni, ya na uleku ɔmmɔ ɔsɔ unwini usie ɔgyani kudɔ, ɔsika Katɔti Kenye Udiene na ɔdi. Na ɔdubɔkpali katɔ na ƒula lili na elie ngba. Nsu si átaya na ulekudi ɔmmɔ ɔsɔ unwini ni, na ati ka awɔ ku ƒula badi ƒui bɛdubo bukpi.”

ref: Early the next morning Abimelech sent for his officials, and when he told them what had happened, they were frightened.
text: Limeyi lɛni nsia ɔtanta tutuutu ni, Abimelek ɔ́lɔ nwuna benengu ƒui ɔ́sala. Na ka údie kenye na ɔ́su-ma ala ani ƒui nálɛ ni, báyia geen.

ref: Abimelech then called in Abraham and said: Look what you've done to us! What have I ever done to you? Why did you make me and my nation guilty of such a terrible sin?
text: Amu na Abimelek ɔ́lɔ Abraham úboku na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Bi ndi lɛla lɛni na ábla-bu mɛni? Munuu lɛla lɛdi na lɛ́bla lɛ́ƒiɔ-ɔ ɔsu na áyɔ ka likpidi bonwu lɛmɛni udu libo ami ku mina kadi bekyuli lɛlɔ? Ábla-mi lɛla lɛni na balabla ukyuli.”

ref: What were you thinking when you did this?
text: Na Abimelek úye Abraham ɔnini, “Bi ndi-ɔ kasusu kani ɔsu na ábla lɛla lɛmɛni udu?”

ref: Abraham answered: I did it because I didn't think any of you respected God, and I was sure that someone would kill me to get my wife.
text: Mmɔ na Abraham údie kenye ɔnini, “Na mina bɔnu kemmo ni, lɛ́su nnii, ‘Kadi kamɛni bekyuli balayia Katɔti, na ɔƒɛni kalɛ badubo-mi bɔdu na mina uleku ɔsu.’

ref: Besides, she is my half sister. We have the same father, but different mothers.
text: Na ɔmmɔ kama lɛnɔ ni, mina ɔnwani na ɔdi sɔsɔ na kalɛ na báwɔ-bu kenye. Ɔsika mina ɔti ubi ulekubi na ɔdi, ina ka ɔɔɔdi mina uni náwɔ-nwu.

ref: When God made us leave my father's home and start wandering, I told her, “If you really love me, then tell everyone that I am your brother.”
text: Ɔƒɛni uwi ɔni na Katɔti ɔ́ya na lɛ́tɔlɔ mina ɔti kaduli na línwini ukyuli ɔni nkɛ mɔɔví ni, lɛni na lɛ́su mina uleku nnii, ‘Ƒula bɔyɔ bɔni na mɔɔwia ka edie atina-mi ni, amu ndi ka kesii biala na búsie ni, na asu anini, “Ɔnana ɔmɛni ni, mina ɔnwani ɔnanabi na ɔdi.” ’ ”

ref: After Abimelech had given Abraham some sheep, cattle, and slaves, he sent Sarah back
text: Amu na Abimelek úti baƒonu ku ɔmantɛ bakɔ ku bananabi ku belekubi kabla bablane na uti-ma úte Abraham, na ɔ́sɔku ulekudi Sara lɛnɔ úte-nwu.

ref: and told Abraham he could settle anywhere in his country.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Abimelek ɔ́su Abraham ɔnini, “Aanu mina kalɔ nwi késela. Teesie na etélie kesii biala na aayɔ́.”

ref: Meanwhile, God had kept Abimelech's wife and slaves from having children. But Abraham prayed, and God let them start having children again.
text: ɔsika nka Katɔti útile Abimelek ku nwuna kaduli bekyuli ƒui kɔwɔ kenye, ɔkɛnku Abraham ulekudi Sara litemi lɛmɛni ɔsu.

ref: The Lord was good to Sarah and kept his promise.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Unwoti ɔ́nu Sara kɔnyana aani kalɛ na ɔ́su-nwu úse.

ref: Although Abraham was very old, Sarah had a son exactly at the time God had said.
text: Sara úbada kaƒɔ na ɔ́wɔ ɔnanabi úte Abraham uwi ɔni na ɔ́ɔbi lɛlɔ mmɔ. Na ɔ́wɔ uwi ɔni lɛlɔ na Katɔti ɔ́su úse ka ɔdubɔwɔ pɛpɛɛpɛ.

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
text: Na ka ubidi Isak ɔmɛni ɔ́ƒɔ emeyi maane ni, Abraham úkulo-nwu lego aani kalɛ na Katɔti úbo-nwu ɔda.

ref: and Sarah said, “God has made me laugh. And now everyone will laugh with me.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Sara ɔ́su ɔnini, “Katɔti ɔ́ya na mina anumi aƒɛnimi ku lɛma. Na ukyuli biala ɔni menu lɛla lɛmɛni ɔdubo bɔma ku-mi ku ebiti.”

ref: Who would have dared to tell Abraham that someday I would have a child? But in his old age, I have given him a son.”
text: Na ɔ́sɔ ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ɔma ndubɔkana na nka ɔsu Abraham ɔnini, ami Sara lɛnɔ ndubute ubi lɛnyi na ɔnyimi. Nsu ina ka Abraham ɔ́ɔbi ni, lɛ́wɔ lite-nwu na nwuna linengu uwi.” Abraham Ɔ́nyonu Hagar Ku Ubidi Ismael

ref: The time came when Sarah no longer had to nurse Isaac, and on that day Abraham gave a big feast. Hagar and Ishmael Are Sent Away
text: Isak úbabi lɛlɔ na basɔ belete-nwu lɛnyi. Na uwi ɔmɛni kemmo ni, Abraham ɔ́di limeyi libendi lɛdi.

ref: One day, Sarah noticed Hagar's son Ishmael playing, and she said to Abraham, “Get rid of that Egyptian slave woman and her son! I don't want him to inherit anything. It should all go to my son.”
text: Anwuɔsu ɔ́su Abraham ɔnini, “Bɔku uleku ɔni mɔɔbla kaduli kabla mmɔ ku ubidi na batɔlɔ kaduli mmɔ, ɔsika uleku ɔni mɔɔbla kaduli kabla ubi ku mina ubi Isak balakana bana ɔti ala bule bɔdi.”

ref: Abraham was worried about Ishmael.
text: Litemi lɛmɛni lɛ́kpulu Abraham kayi, ɔsika lɛdanku ubidi.

ref: But God said, “Abraham, don't worry about your slave woman and the boy. Just do what Sarah tells you. Isaac will inherit your family name,
text: Nsu Katɔti ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Awɔ, nu lɛla biala na Sara wasu-ɔ. Na kayi katakpulu-ɔ na ƒ'ubidi ku ƒula kabla ɔblane uleku ɔmɛni lɛlɔ. Ɔsika ɔkɛnku Isak lɛlɔ na adubudie kaba aani kalɛ na lɛ́su-ɔ líse.

ref: but the son of the slave woman is also your son, and I will make his descendants into a great nation.”
text: Na ƒula kabla ɔblane uleku ɔmmɔ ubidi ni, ndubɔya na udie kaba na benwini kadi ka anwu lɛnɔ ɔdi ƒ'ubidi ɔsu.”

ref: Early the next morning Abraham gave Hagar an animal skin full of water and some bread. Then he put the boy on her shoulder and sent them away. They wandered around in the desert near Beersheba,
text: Limeyi lɛni nsia ɔtanta tutuutu ni, Abraham úti adila ku ntu ɔsii ɔdi útile Hagar na údie anwu ku ubidi ɔnanabi ɔmmɔ ulu. Uleku ɔmmɔ ɔ́yu ulu na ɔkɛ ɔɔví Beer-Seba ketele kemmo.

ref: and after they had run out of water, Hagar put her son under a bush.
text: Ka ntu búbasa ɔsii ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, ɔda ubi ɔmmɔ ɔ́kya ɔyuli ɔdi kalɔ.

ref: Then she sat down a long way off, because she could not bear to watch him die. And she cried bitterly.
text: Na anwu ɔ́kpɛ ɔ́lɛ-nwu kudɔ úsie kama blii, ɔsika ɔ́su lɛlɔ ɔnini, “Naaakana bunye bɔnu kalɛ na mina ubi uukpi.” Na ka ulie kesii kammɔ ni, úse kalɔ uuwulo keku geen.

ref: When God heard the boy crying, the angel of God called out to Hagar from heaven and said, “Hagar, why are you worried? Don't be afraid. I have heard your son crying.
text: Katɔti únu ka ubi ɔmmɔ uuwulo. Amu na Katɔti ɔtu ɔdi ɔ́kpali úmene ɔ́si Katɔ na ɔ́su Hagar ɔnini, “Hagar, bi nébo? Tayia! Katɔti únu ka ɔnamu ɔmmɔ uuwulo na kesii kani na ɔtɛ.

ref: Help him up and hold his hand, because I will make him the father of a great nation.”
text: Sie na atáda-nwu kɔnu na ɔta unye, ɔsika ndubɔya na udie kaba na benwini kadi bekyuli bonwu.”

ref: Then God let her see a well. So she went to the well and filled the skin with water, then gave some to her son.
text: Mmɔ ni, Katɔti ɔ́ya na uleku ɔmmɔ anumi ábla nwaa na ɔ́nu libiɔ lɛdi na mmɔ ka ntu buwi. Anwuɔsu úsie na útédu ntu úyile ɔsii ɔmmɔ na úte ubidi na ɔ́ni.

ref: God blessed Ishmael, and as the boy grew older, he became an expert at hunting with his bow and arrows. He lived in the Paran Desert, and his mother chose an Egyptian woman for him to marry. A Peace Treaty
text: Na uwi ɔni na ɔkya ketele kani baalɔ Paran kemmo mmɔ ni, uni ɔ́wia uleku ɔ́siku Egyipte úte-nwu na úti. Abimelek Ku Abraham Bényimi Lɛla Lɛdi Na Beer-Seba

ref: About this time Abimelech and his army commander Phicol said to Abraham, “God blesses everything you do!
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Abimelek ku nwuna bekpone ɔtɔnkune ɔni baalɔ Pikol básu Abraham banini, “Bɔɔnu ka Katɔti uliku-ɔ na ƒula lɛla biala na aabla kemmo.

ref: Now I want you to promise in the name of God that you will always be loyal to me and my descendants, just as I have always been loyal to you in this land where you have lived as a foreigner.”
text: Ɔƒaani ni, mɔɔwia ka ese kenye kadi ete-mi na Katɔti anumi na mɛni ka aaadubo ami iye mina bebi iye bawɔ bɔtɔlɔ. Ka ɔƒɔ na adi na eli kalɔ kamɛni kemmo ɔsu ni, bla lɛbuni lɛni na lɛ́bla líte-ɔ te ami ku mina kalɔ kamɛni lɛlɔ bekyuli ƒui.”

ref: One day, Abraham told Abimelech, “Some of your servants have taken over one of my wells.”
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Abraham údie lɛla lɛni muukpóku-nwu ɔ́su Abimelek ɔ́si libiɔ ku ntu bɔni na nwuna kabla bablane béƒie báƒɔ-nwu kudɔ lɛlɔ.

ref: “This is the first I've heard about it,” Abimelech replied. “Why haven't you said something before? I don't have any idea who did it.”
text: Nsu Abimelek ɔ́su ɔnini, “Lɛɛɛgyi ukyuli ɔni nábla lɛla lɛmɛni udu. Na awɔ lɛnɔ átasu-mi ɔmɛni kudii. Na ńtenu lɛla lɛmɛni lɛnɔ kudii si ɔɔɔdi ɔmwi.”

ref: Abraham gave Abimelech some sheep and cattle, then the two men made a peace treaty.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu Abraham úti baƒonu ku ɔmantɛ bakɔ na úti-ma úte Abimelek na bana inyɔ bényimi lɛlakpa linwi.

ref: Abraham separated seven female lambs from his flock of sheep,
text: Abraham údie baƒonu beledi bakɔdi maatɛ úse kɔmadɔ na bakladi akɔ.

ref: Abraham replied, “I want you to accept these seven lambs as proof that I dug this well.”
text: Mmɔ na Abraham údie kenye ɔnini, “Mɔɔyɔ́ ka aƒɔ baƒonu maatɛ bamɛni asiku-mi kudɔ aani kenye budie likpose ka ami néku libiɔ lɛmɛni.”

ref: So they called the place Beersheba, because they made a treaty there.
text: Ɔmɛni ɔsu na baalɔ kesii kani na ala ammɔ álɛ banii Beer-Seba. Ɔsika mmɔ na bana inyɔ bákpali ala bényimi lɛla lɛdi bése.

ref: When the treaty was completed, Abimelech and his army commander Phicol went back to the land of the Philistines.
text: Na lɛla lɛmmɔ bunyimi kama na Beer-Seba mmɔ ni, Abimelek ku nwuna bekpone ɔtɔnkune Pikol bénwini bésie Ƒilistiƒuɔ kalɔ lɛlɔ.

ref: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba and worshiped the eternal Lord God.
text: Na Abraham ɔ́lɛ ɔyuli kugyii kɔdi na Beer-Seba mmɔ. Na mmɔ na ɔ́kpali Katɔti lɛnyi ɔnii, Unwoti, Uwi Ƒui Katɔti.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Na Abraham úlie Ƒilistiƒuɔ kalɔ lɛlɔ uwi gyɔlɔɔ.

ref: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text: Akwa adi kama ni, Katɔti ɔ́kɔku Abraham ɔ́nu na ɔ́kpali-nwu ɔnini, “Abraham!” Na Abraham lɛnɔ ɔ́tuna ɔnini, “Ati, aanu-mi.”

ref: The Lord said, “Go get Isaac, your only son, the one you dearly love! Take him to the land of Moriah, and I will show you a mountain where you must sacrifice him to me on the fires of an altar.”
text: Amu na Katɔti ɔnini, “Ta na eti ƒ'ubidi Isak unwi pɛ ɔni na aayɔ́ mmɔ, na eteesie Moria kalɛkɛ lɛlɔ. Mmɔ ni, tádu-nwu na akyula-nwu eti abla akpala ete-mi na kɔbi kɔni matina-a lɛlɔ.”

ref: So Abraham got up early the next morning and chopped wood for the fire. He put a saddle on his donkey and set out with Isaac and two servants for the place where God had told him to go.
text: Ka kadi kégye lɛdigye tutuutu ni, Abraham ɔ́ta na útile nwuna ebuo ala utine ala na ɔ́bala-nwu ala kaduɛ. Amu na útiku nwuna kabla bablane inyɔ ku ubidi Isak. Na ka ɔ́yɔ ekyweni pii na edu kalɛ na weti wakyula akpala ammɔ ni, ɔ́yu ulu úsie kesii kani na Katɔti ɔ́tina-nwu mmɔ.

ref: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
text: Na ka bákɛ ulu bésela limeyi lɛtɛdi lɛlɔ ni, Abraham ɔ́talaku anumi na ɔ́nu kesii kammɔ úmene.

ref: He told his servants, “Stay here with the donkey, while my son and I go over there to worship. We will come back.”
text: Amu na ɔ́su nwuna kabla bablane ɔnini, “Amii bɛsɔ binye mɛni ku ebuo ala utine ɔmmɔ. Na ami ku mina ubi busie butákpa Katɔti na bɔsɔ bunwini bubo.”

ref: Abraham put the wood on Isaac's shoulder, but he carried the hot coals and the knife. As the two of them walked along,
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Abraham úti ekyweni ammɔ útile ubidi Isak. Na anwu ɔlati ɔda ogya ku kewii. Amu ka bana inyɔ bakɛ bésela ni,

ref: Isaac said, “Father, we have the coals and the wood, but where is the lamb for the sacrifice?” “My son,” Abraham answered, “God will provide the lamb.” The two of them walked on, and
text: Amu na Abraham údie kenye ɔnini, “Ubi, Katɔti ɔlati ndubute-bu koƒonu kɔni na bɔdubuti bɔbla akpala ammɔ.” Ka ɔ́su-nwu lɛni ni, básɔ béti bákɔ bésela.

ref: when they reached the place that God had told him about, Abraham built an altar and placed the wood on it. Next, he tied up his son and put him on the wood.
text: Na ka bédu kesii kani na Katɔti ɔ́tina-nwu mmɔ ni, Abraham ɔ́tu lɛkpabi na ɔ́tɛ ekyweni ammɔ ɔ́sia-ni lɛlɔ. Na ɔ́da ubidi na únyimi-nwu kaduɛ na ɔ́gyu-nwu ɔ́sia lɛkpabi lɛmmɔ lɛlɔ na ekyweni ammɔ lɛlɔ.

ref: He then took the knife and got ready to kill his son.
text: Mmɔ na úsienku kɔnu na údie nwuna kewii kalɛ na ɔdu ubidi.

ref: But the Lord's angel shouted from heaven, “Abraham! Abraham!” “Here I am!” he answered.
text: Kɔnakunwi ni, Katɔti ɔtu ɔdi ɔ́kpali ɔ́siku Katɔ ɔnini, “Abraham! Abraham!” Amu na Abraham údie kenye ɔnini, “Aanu-mi mɛni.”

ref: “Don't hurt the boy or harm him in any way!” the angel said. “Now I know that you truly obey God, because you were willing to offer him your only son.”
text: Mmɔ ni, ɔtu ɔmmɔ ɔ́su Abraham ɔnini, “Ƒula kɔnu kutetuku ɔnamu ɔmmɔ. Na tabla-nwu lɛla lɛdi lɛnɔ. Ɔƒɛni na lɛ́nu lɛ́ti ka aayia Katɔti sɔsɔ. Ɔsika éteni ka eti ƒ'ubidi ɔni ndi ubi unwi pɛ ɔni na ebo ete-nwu.”

ref: Abraham looked up and saw a ram caught by its horns in the bushes. So he took the ram and sacrificed it instead of his son.
text: Amu ka Abraham ɔ́talaku anumi ɔɔnu ni, ɔ́nu koƒonu kɔgyani bonwu kɔdi ka kunye na kuna ese ábala kugyii kɔdi kɔgba. Amu na úsie útédie koƒonu kɔmmɔ na úti-ku ɔ́kpa ɔ́bla akpala na ubidi ɔmmɔ kenye.

ref: Abraham named that place “The Lord Will Provide.” And even now people say, “On the mountain of the Lord it will be provided.”
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Abraham ɔ́lɔ kesii kammɔ ɔnii Katɔti Ɔdubute. Na úti úbedu ku ɔmwi limeyi lɛmɛni lɛlɔ ni, ɔdi lɛsugba lɛdi na baasu banii, “Na Unwoti kɔbi lɛlɔ ni, anwu ɔlati ɔdubute lɛla lɛni bɔɔwia.”

ref: The Lord's angel called out from heaven a second time:
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Katɔti ɔtu ɔmmɔ ɔ́sɔ ɔ́kpali Abraham kunyɔdi.

ref: You were willing to offer your only son to the Lord, and so he makes you this solemn promise,
text: Na ɔ́su-nwu ka Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́su ɔnini, “Éteni ka eti ƒ'ubidi unwi pɛ akpa ete-mi. Anwuɔsu ni, lɛ́kpali lɛlɔ líse kenye ka

ref: “I will bless you and give you such a large family, that someday your descendants will be more numerous than the stars in the sky or the grains of sand along the seashore. They will defeat their enemies and take over the cities where their enemies live.
text: ndubute-ɔ kusɛ kusɛ na lɛya na ƒula kaba bebi bemomo betulo ayabi. Na badubo bumomo aani ɛpu kɔnwinimi. Na ƒula bawɔ badubo bukpo na baƒɔ bana bekisine lɛma linengudi ƒui.

ref: You have obeyed me, and so you and your descendants will be a blessing to all nations on earth.”
text: Na ɔkɛ ƒula kaba kemmo ni, ndubute kayawi ƒui bekyuli kusɛ kusɛ. Ɔsika énu-mi litemi na ádi-ni lɛlɔ.”

ref: Abraham and Isaac went back to the servants who had come with him, and they returned to Abraham's home in Beersheba. The Children of Nahor
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Abraham ɔ́sɔ únwini úsie nwuna kabla bablane kudɔ. Na bénwini bésie Beer-Seba. Na Abraham útélie Beer-Seba mmɔ. Bebi Bani Nahor Ɔ́wɔ

ref: Nahor also had another wife. Her name was Reumah, and she had four sons: Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
text: Na nwuna ɔsɔnɔ ɔni baalɔ Rehuma lɛnɔ ɔ́wɔ bananabi úte-nwu. Na bebi bammɔ ndi Teba ku Gaham ku Tahas ku Maaka.

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: “Kadi bamba ukyuli na ndi. Na ndi ɔƒɔ na bina akɔ. Mɔɔyɔ́ ka bibo kabuli kalɔ kadi bite-mi na liye na bina kudɔ mɛni, na liti linwini bekyuli kavakɔ kalɛ na lɛkana na lɛva mina uleku.”

ref: “Sir,” they answered, “you are an important man. Choose the best place to bury your wife. None of us would refuse you a resting place for your dead.”
text: “Ati, kywɛ-bu atu, ɔgadine ɔni na áƒɔ lɛnyi geen na adi na bula akɔ. Anwuɔsu ti ƒula ukpine táva kɔƒiɔtɔ kɔbuni kɔni na béku bése mmɔ akɔ unwi kemmo. Bula akɔ ɔdi biala uléni ka ava ƒula ukpine na nwuna kaƒiɔtɔ kadi kemmo.”

ref: and replied, “If you are willing to let me bury my wife here, please ask Zohar's son Ephron
text: Na ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Si bínu kalɔ ka lɛva mina ukpine ni, amu bɛkywɛ-mi atu na bivele Soha ubidi Eƒron bite-mi.

ref: to sell me Machpelah Cave at the end of his field. I'll pay what it's worth, and all of you can be witnesses.”
text: Na uti Makpela lɛtakpɛ ubu ɔni ndi anwu Eƒron ɔdi na ɔkya nwuna kalɔ kawɔlɔkɔ mmɔ uye ute-mi. Bɛsu-nwu na uye-ka ute-mi na kuye kɔni na ɔ́manku kenye. Na liti mmɔ linwini bekyuli kavakɔ.”

ref: Ephron was sitting there near the city gate, when Abraham made this request, and he answered,
text: Amu na Eƒron, Hitini ɔmmɔ údie kenye na ɔma ɔmmɔ ɔba kenye kesii kani na bása beli mmɔ, na ɔ́su Abraham ɔnini,

ref: “Sir, the whole field, including the cave, is yours. With my own people as witnesses, I freely give it to you as a burial place for your dead.”
text: “Ati, neeeye-ka neeete-ɔ! Líti kalɔ kammɔ líte-ɔ ƒaa ku lɛtakpɛ ubu ɔni nkya kalɔ kammɔ kemmo na bekyuli bamɛni ƒui anumi kalɛ na ava ƒula ukpine.”

ref: and said to Ephron, “In front of these witnesses, I offer you the full price, so I can bury my wife. Please accept my offer.”
text: Na ɔ́su Eƒron na bana ƒui anumi ɔnini, “Kywɛ-mi atu. Si adubo bɔtunaku-mi ni, ndubɔƒɔ kalɔ kammɔ ƒui kayu. Anwuɔsu nuku-mi kalɔ na aƒɔ sika ɔƒualadi ɔmmɔ na ɔdia na lɛva mina ukpine na mmɔ.”

ref: So Abraham buried his wife Sarah in Machpelah Cave that was in the field
text: Na ɔmmɔ kama ni, Abraham útáva ulekudi na Makpela lɛtakpɛ ubu na kalɔ kammɔ kemmo. Na kalɔ kammɔ kedenku Mamre na Hebron na Kanaan kalɔ kemmo.

ref: Abraham was now a very old man. The Lord had made him rich, and he was successful in everything he did.
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Abraham ɔ́ɔbi lɛlɔ geen ɔni na nwuna akwa éesie anumi. Na Katɔti úte-nwu kusɛ kusɛ na nwuna ngba keliela biala kemmo.

ref: One day, Abraham called in his most trusted servant and said to him, “Solemnly promise me
text: Limeyi lɛdi ni, Abraham ɔ́lɔ nwuna kabla ɔblane unengu ɔni mɔɔnu nwuna ala ƒui lɛlɔ na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Va enu mina lɛgba kalɔ mɛni na ese-mi kenye

ref: in the name of the Lord, who rules heaven and earth, that you won't choose a wife for my son Isaac from the people here in the land of Canaan.
text: na Unwoti Katɔti ɔni ndi katɔ ku kalɔ ƒui ɔti lɛnyi kemmo ka ni, alawia uleku ɔdi na Kanaanƒuɔ bani akɔ na akya mɛni elete mina ubi ɔnanabi.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for him from among my relatives.”
text: Mmomu adubusie ami ɔlati kalɔ lɛlɔ na mina ɔtɔ kemmo banwani akɔ atáwia uleku eboku mina ubi Isak.”

ref: But the servant asked, “What if the young woman I choose refuses to leave home and come here with me? Should I send Isaac there to look for a wife?”
text: Amu na nwuna kabla ɔblane ɔmmɔ úye Abraham ɔnini, “Na si uleku ɔmmɔ útayɔ ka ɔsiaku-mi ubo kalɔ kamɛni lɛlɔ nɔɔ? Aayɔ́ ka lɛkpanku ƒ'ubidi lɛsɔku lisienku ƒula kalɔ lɛlɔ kesii kani na ási?”

ref: “No!” Abraham answered. “Don't ever do that, no matter what.
text: Mmɔ na Abraham ɔ́su nwuna ala lɛlɔ ɔnune ɔmmɔ ɔnini, “Oowo! Tasɔku mina ubi tesienku mina kaduli bekyuli ɔma.”

ref: The Lord who rules heaven brought me here from the land where I was born and promised that he would give this land to my descendants forever. When you go back there, the Lord will send his angel ahead of you to help you find a wife for my son.
text: Na ɔ́sɔ ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Unwoti ɔni ndi Katɔti ni, anwu nálɔ-mi ɔ́siku mina ɔti kaduli ku mina kalɔ lɛlɔ. Na anwu nákpali lɛlɔ úse-mi kenye ɔnini, ‘Ƒula bawɔ na ndubuti kalɔ kamɛni lite.’ Anwu ɔmmɔ ndubɔya na nwuna ɔtu ɔtɔnku-ɔ. Na ɔdubɔya na akɛ uleku asiku mmɔ eboku mina ubi ɔnanabi.

ref: If the woman refuses to come along, you don't have to keep this promise. But don't ever take my son back there.”
text: Na si uleku ɔmmɔ nétenu kalɔ ka ɔsiaku-ɔ ubo mɛni ni, na lɛla lɛdi lɛsɔ lɛɛɛda-a na kenye kani na ése-mi mɛni lɛlɔ. Na lɛla lɛni kani na mɔɔwia ka asia anu kaduɛ ndi ka alasɔku mina ubi elésienku mmɔ.”

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
text: Anwuɔsu ala lɛlɔ ɔnune ɔmmɔ lɛnɔ úse kenye na Katɔti lɛnyi kemmo na nwuna ɔtɔlati Abraham anumi ka ɔdubɔdi litemi lɛmmɔ lɛlɔ aani kalɛ na ɔ́su-nwu.

ref: Soon after that, the servant loaded ten of Abraham's camels with valuable gifts. Then he set out for the city in northern Syria, where Abraham's brother Nahor lived.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, ala lɛlɔ ɔnune ɔmmɔ úti nwuna ɔtɔlati Abraham bebuo ala betine leevu. Na úti ala abuni lidu lidu ɔ́siku nwuna ɔtɔlati kudɔ na ɔ́yu ulu ɔ́tɔlɔ úsie kalɔ kani baalɔ Mesopotamia ɔ́sia úsela ɔma ɔni kemmo na Nahor uli.

ref: When he got there, he let the camels rest near the well outside the city. It was late afternoon, the time when the women came out for water.
text: Ka bédu libiɔ lɛdi kɔtu na ɔma ɔmmɔ kabanye ni, ala lɛlɔ ɔnune ɔmɛni ɔ́ya na bebuo bammɔ báyu bébi libiɔ lɛmmɔ kɔtu. Na uwi ɔmmɔ ɔ́ɔbla useyi uwi buɛɛ uwi ɔni na beleku beesié ketu.

ref: The servant prayed: You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships. Please keep your promise to him and let me find a wife for Isaac today.
text: Amu na ɔ́kpali katɔ ɔ́su ɔnini, “O Unwoti, mina ɔtɔlati Abraham Katɔti, ya na lɛla lɛni ɔsu na líbo mɛni lɛtɛ lite-mi ɔmwi. Na anu mina ɔtɔlati Abraham kɔnyana.

ref: The young women of the city will soon come to this well for water,
text: Aanu ka nnye libiɔ lɛmɛni kɔtu, na ɔma ɔmɛni belekubi báasi badubo bebeteedu ntu.

ref: and I'll ask one of them for a drink. If she gives me a drink and then offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen and that you have kept your promise to my master.
text: Anwuɔsu si lɛ́su ulekubi ɔdi nnii, ‘Yula ƒula libui kya libiɔ lɛmɛni kemmo na ete-mi ntu bɔdi na lɛni,’ na anwu lɛnɔ únu kalɔ na ɔ́su ɔnii, ‘Aanu ntu, ni ƒaa na lidu bɔdi lite ƒula bebuo lɛnɔ na bani’ ni, mmɔ na ndubɔnu lɛti ka anwu na áƒuna éte ƒula kabla ɔblane Isak. Na ɔkɛ ɔmɛni lɛlɔ ni, ndubɔnu lɛti ka ánu mina ɔtɔlati kɔnyana.”

ref: While he was still praying, a beautiful unmarried young woman came by with a water jar on her shoulder. She was Rebekah, the daughter of Bethuel, the son of Abraham's brother Nahor and his wife Milcah. Rebekah walked past Abraham's servant, then went over to the well, and filled her water jar. When she started back,
text: Rebeka ɔmɛni ɔdi adagyaa ɔni na anwu ku ɔnana ɔdi bétesie lɛlɔ kudɔ kudii, na ɔɔdia bɔnu geen. Úduo libiɔ lɛmɛni kemmo na útédu ntu úyile nwuna libui na ɔ́lɛ úbo.

ref: Abraham's servant ran to her and said, “Please let me have a drink of water.”
text: Amu na ala lɛlɔ ɔnune ɔmɛni ɔ́babla útásiaku-nwu na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Buvele nda-a, du ntu blii bɔdi na ƒula libui kemmo te-mi na lɛni.”

ref: “I'll be glad to,” she answered. Then she quickly took the jar from her shoulder and held it while he drank.
text: Kɔnakunwi ni, Rebeka ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ati, ni ntu ƒaa.” Na ɔ́yula libui lɛmmɔ ɔ́kya-nwu enu kemmo ɔnii ɔni.

ref: After he had finished, she said, “Now I'll give your camels all the water they want.” She quickly poured out water for them, and she kept going back for more, until his camels had drunk all they wanted.
text: Anwuɔsu ɔ́babla na úwilimi ntu bɔkladi úwi bana kalɛkɛ kani kemmo na baaní ntu kemmo. Na úsie útédu ntu bɔmmɔ ɔ́sia kama laa útédu uwi ɔni na bebuo bammɔ ƒui báni ntu na búdu-ma ɔkwɛ.

ref: Abraham's servant did not say a word, but he watched everything Rebekah did, because he wanted to know for certain if this was the woman the Lord had chosen.
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ ƒui ni, ala lɛlɔ ɔnune ɔmmɔ unye ɔɔnu Rebeka kpoo ɔnii ɔɔnu ɔti si Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́ya na nwuna ulu ɔni na úsie ɔ́lɛ-nwu kɔsia sɔsɔ.

ref: The servant had brought along an expensive gold ring and two large gold bracelets. When Rebekah had finished bringing the water, he gave her the ring for her nose and the bracelets for her arms.
text: Ka bebuo bammɔ báni ntu bɔmmɔ báwɔlɔ ni, ala lɛlɔ ɔnune ɔmmɔ údie sika ugyiedi kakwɛnimi kani kadi kammɔ bekyuli baakya enwu kemmo úte-nwu. Na úte-nwu sika ugyiedi kɔkwɛnimi inyɔ kɔni mbo unu na baakya enu.

ref: Then he said, “Please tell me who your father is. Does he have room in his house for me and my men to spend the night?”
text: Mmɔ na úye Rebeka ɔnini, “Ɔma ubidi na adi? Mɔɔyɔ́ lɛti si ndubɔkɛ keduokɔ kadi na ƒula ɔti kesii si lɛtɔ lɛdi lɛdubɔkya na ami ku mina badi bɔdubɔkɛ katɛnkɔ?”

ref: She answered, “My father is Bethuel, the son of Nahor and Milcah.
text: Amu na údie kenye ɔnini, “Betuel ɔni na Milka ku Nahor báwɔ ni, nwuna ubidi ulekubi na ndi.”

ref: We have a place where you and your men can stay, and we also have enough straw and feed for your camels.”
text: Na ɔ́sɔ ɔ́su lɛnɔ ɔnini, “Bubo kɔtɛnkɔ na awɔ ku ƒula badi bɛtɛ, na bubo livo lɛni lɛlɔ na bebuo baatɛ ku alakpi ani bakun kaduɛ.”

ref: Then the servant bowed his head and prayed,
text: Mmɔ ni, ɔnana ɔmmɔ úboku lili kalɔ na úmwi Katɔti.

ref: Rebekah ran straight home and told her family everything.
text: Rebeka úkeli na útásu nwuna kaduli bekyuli ala amɛni ƒui.

ref: Her brother Laban heard her tell what the servant had said, and he saw the ring and the bracelets she was wearing. So Laban ran out to Abraham's servant, who was standing by his camels at the well.
text: Na ɔnwani ɔnanabi ɔdi ɔkya na baalɔ-nwu Laban. Ka Laban ɔmɛni ɔ́nu kɔkwɛnimi kɔni nkya ɔnwani enwu ku enu ni, úkeli úsie útánu ala lɛlɔ ɔnune ɔmmɔ na bebuo bammɔ kudɔ na libiɔ kɔtu mmɔ.

ref: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
text: Na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Katɔti kusɛ kusɛ ɔsiaku-ɔ geen. Bo na buteesie. Tasɔ tenye libiɔ lɛmmɔ kɔtu lɛni. Lɛ́ɛklibi ɔtɔ ku bebuo bammɔ kakyakɔ ƒui líse.”

ref: Abraham's servant went home with Laban, where Laban's servants unloaded his camels and gave them straw and feed. Then they brought water into the house, so Abraham's servant and his men could wash their feet.
text: Amu na ɔnana ɔmmɔ ɔ́siaku Laban úsie kaduli na báƒɛnimi bebuo bammɔ etila. Na béti livo ku alakpi ani lɛlɔ na bebuo bammɔ badubɔtɛ béboku bebuo bammɔ. Na bédu ntu béboku bekyuli bani na anwu ku-ma bakɛ banii baƒɔ lɛgba.

ref: After that, they brought in food. But the servant said, “Before I eat, I must tell you why I have come.” “Go ahead and tell us,” Laban answered.
text: Na béti adila bébese-ma, nsu ala lɛlɔ ɔnune ɔmmɔ ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Lɛɛɛdubo bɔdi tɔ wetédu ka lɛ́su-mii lɛla lɛni na ndanku lɛ́wɔlɔ.” Mmɔ ni, Laban ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Taasu lɛla lɛni na adanku.”

ref: The servant explained: I am Abraham's servant.
text: Amu na ɔnana ɔmɛni úse kalɔ ɔnini, “Abraham kabla ɔblane na ndi.

ref: The Lord has been good to my master and has made him very rich. He has given him many sheep, goats, cattle, camels, and donkeys, as well as a lot of silver and gold, and many slaves.
text: Na Unwoti Katɔti úte mina ɔtɔlati kusɛ kusɛ geen na ubo lɛkɛ kaduɛ. Ubo baƒonu ku ɔmantɛ bakɔ ku sika ɔƒualadi ku ugyiedi. Ubo kabla bablane bananabi ku belekubi. Ubo bebuo ala betine benengudi bani mbo keƒu ku baƒlumu lɛnɔ.

ref: Sarah, my master's wife, didn't have any children until she was very old. Then she had a son, and my master has given him everything.
text: Na Sara ɔni ndi mina ɔtɔlati ulekudi ni, ɔ́ɔbi asa na ɔ́wɔ ubi unwi pɛ. Na ubi ɔmɛni ɔdi ɔnanabi ɔni na mina ɔtɔlati úti nwuna ala ani ƒui na ubo úte.

ref: I solemnly promised my master that I would do what he said. And he told me, “Don't choose a wife for my son from the women in this land of Canaan.
text: Mina ɔtɔlati ɔ́ya na lɛ́kpali Katɔti líse-nwu kenye, na ɔ́su-mi ɔnini, ‘Tawia uleku ɔdi tete mina ubi ɔsiku Kanaanƒuɔ kadi bani akɔ na nkya mɛni bebi belekubi akɔ.

ref: Instead, go back to the land where I was born and find a wife for my son from among my relatives.”
text: Mmomu teesie mina ɔti kaduli bekyuli akɔ na ami ɔlati kɔdua kemmo na atáwia uleku ete mina ubi ɔnanabi.’

ref: I asked my master, “What if the young woman refuses to come with me?”
text: “Amu na líye-nwu nnini, ‘Na si uleku ɔmmɔ nétenu kalɔ ka ɔsiaku-mi ubo mɛni nɔɔ?’

ref: My master answered, “I have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
text: “Mmɔ na údie kenye ɔnini, ‘Katɔti ɔni mɔɔnu kalɛ na nlie ngba nte-nwu ni, ɔdubɔtala nwuna ɔtu na ɔsiaku-ɔ na ulu ɔni na adubo busie kemmo na lɛla biala lɛtɛ. Na adubɔkɛ uleku eboku mina ubi asiku mina kɔdua ku mina ɔti kaduli bekyuli akɔ.

ref: But if they refuse to let her come back with you, then you are freed from your promise.”
text: Na si ésie mina kɔdua na ama nétenu kalɔ na bete-ɔ uleku ɔmmɔ ni, lɛla lɛdi lɛsɔ lilada-a.’

ref: When I came to the well today, I silently prayed, “You, Lord, are the God my master Abraham worships, so please lead me to a wife for his son
text: “Na uwi ɔni na ḿbedu libiɔ lɛmmɔ kɔtu ɔmwi ni, lɛ́kpali Katɔti lɛ́su-nwu nnini, ‘O Unwoti mina ɔtɔlati Abraham Katɔti, si ɔ́yɔ-ɔ kaƒɔ ni, na aya na lɛla lɛni ɔsu na muusié ulu ɔmɛni ubo-nwu kemmo lɛni.

ref: while I am here at the well. When a young woman comes out to get water, I'll ask her to give me a drink.
text: Aanu-mi ka nnye libiɔ lɛmɛni kɔtu, na si ulekubi ɔdi úbo ɔnii ubedu ntu ni, ndubɔsu-nwu nnii, “Buvele du ntu blii bɔdi na ƒula libui kemmo te-mi na lɛni”

ref: If she gives me a drink and offers to get some water for my camels, I'll know she is the one you have chosen.”
text: na si ɔ́su-mi ɔnii, “Ati, ni ntu ƒaa na lidu bɔdi lite ƒula bebuo lɛnɔ na bani” ni, na aya na ɔbla ka anwu na awɔ mina Unwoti áƒuna éte mina ɔtɔlati ubidi.’

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.
text: “Uwi ɔni na lɛ́kpali katɔ mɔɔwɔlɔ lɛni na mina lili kemmo kani na Rebeka ɔ́da ɔ́lɛ. Na nwuna libui lɛsia-nwu lɛmangba, na ɔ́yu úduo libiɔ lɛmmɔ kemmo útédu ntu. Na lɛ́su-nwu nnini, ‘Buvele, te-mi ƒula ntu bɔmmɔ bɔdi na lɛni.’

ref: She quickly lowered the jar from her shoulder and said, “Have a drink. Then I'll get water for your camels.” So I drank, and after that she got some water for my camels.
text: “Amu na ɔ́babla ɔ́yula nwuna libui ɔ́siku lɛmangba na ɔ́su-mi ɔnini, ‘Ati, ni ntu ƒaa. Na lidu bɔdi lite ƒula bebuo lɛnɔ na bani.’ Ɔmmɔ ɔsu lɛ́ni na údu bɔbambadi úte bebuo bammɔ lɛnɔ ƒui.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: “Amu na líye-nwu nnini, ‘Ɔma ubidi na adi?’ Mmɔ na údie kenye ɔnini, ‘Betuel ɔni na Milka ku Nahor báwɔ ni, nwuna ubidi ulekubi na ndi.’ “Amu na líti enwu kakwɛnimi kammɔ lɛ́kya-nwu enwu. Na líti kɔkwɛnimi inyɔ kɔmmɔ lɛnɔ lɛ́kya-nwu enu.

ref: Then I bowed my head and gave thanks to the God my master Abraham worships. The Lord had led me straight to my master's relatives, and I had found a wife for his son.
text: Na ɔmmɔ kama lɛ́kya lili kalɔ na límwi Unwoti Katɔti. Lɛ́kɔliku Unwoti mina ɔtɔlati Abraham Katɔti lɛnyi katɔ. Anwu nátɔnku-mi ɔ́vinku ulu ɔni geen lɛlɔ na ɔlɛka lɛsia. Na ḿbasiaku mina ɔtɔlati Abraham ɔlati ɔnwani uwɔ lite ubidi ɔnanabi ka uti-nwu.

ref: Now please tell me if you are willing to do the right thing for my master. Will you treat him fairly, or do I have to look for another young woman?
text: Ɔmɛni ɔsu ni, si bɛdubɔbla mina ɔtɔlati kaƒɔ buni na bɛdubɔdi nwuna litemi lɛlɔ bite-nwu ni, na bɛsu-mi. Na si ɔɔɔdi lɛni lɛnɔ ni, na bɛsu-mi na lɛti kesii kani na masia.”

ref: Laban and Bethuel answered, “The Lord has done this. We have no choice in the matter.
text: Amu na Laban ku Betuel bédie kenye banini, “Katɔti ɔlati nábla ala amɛni ƒui lɛni, anwuɔsu abu buuubo lɛla lɛdi na bɔɔsu-ɔ na ulu ɔdi biala lɛlɔ.

ref: Take Rebekah with you; she can marry your master's son, just as the Lord has said.”
text: Aanu Rebeka nnye. Tiku-nwu na biteesie. Na unwini ƒula ɔtɔlati ubidi uleku aani kalɛ na Katɔti ɔlati ɔ́tina.”

ref: Then he gave clothing, as well as silver and gold jewelry, to Rebekah. He also gave expensive gifts to her brother and her mother.
text: Na údie adi abuni ku sika ugyiedi ku ɔƒualadi adala abambadi ani náƒɔ kuye geen úte Rebeka. Na úti ala bamba ani lɛnɔ náƒɔ kuye geen úte Rebeka ɔnwani Laban ku bana uni lɛnɔ.

ref: Abraham's servant and the men with him ate and drank, then spent the night there. The next morning they got up, and the servant told Rebekah's mother and brother, “I would like to go back to my master now.”
text: Ɔmmɔ kama asa na anwu ku nwuna kabla bablane bakpana bani nsiaku-nwu bádi na báni na bátɛ mmɔ na kadi kégye. Kadi kégye ɔtanta ni, ɔnana ɔmɛni ɔ́su kaduli bekyuli ɔnini, “Bidie-mi ulu na nteesie mina ɔtɔlati kudɔ.”

ref: “Let Rebekah stay with us for a week or ten days,” they answered. “Then she may go.”
text: Nsu Rebeka ɔnwani ku bana uni básu banini, “Ya na ulekubi ɔmɛni ɔkya-bu kudɔ emeyi leevu asa na bɛtɔlɔ.”

ref: But he said, “Don't make me stay any longer. The Lord has already helped me find a wife for my master's son. Now let us return.”
text: Mmɔ na ɔnana ɔmmɔ lɛnɔ ɔ́su ɔnini, “Bɛsɔ bitaƒɔ-mi bitakya mɛni uwi ɔni na Katɔti ɔ́ɔya na lɛla biala líbo líte kalɛ na ɔ́tina na ulu ɔni na muusié kemmo. Anwuɔsu bidie-mi ulu na nteesie mina ɔtɔlati kudɔ.”

ref: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
text: Amu na básu-nwu banini, “Ya na bɔlɔ ulekubi ɔmmɔ ɔlati na ubo na buye-nwu kenye litemi bunu.”

ref: They called her and asked, “Are you willing to leave with this man at once?” “Yes,” she answered.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu bálɔ Rebeka na béye-nwu banini, “Aayɔ́ ka asiaku ɔnana ɔmɛni na biteesie?” Amu na údie kenye ɔnini, “Ndubusie.”

ref: So they agreed to let Rebekah and an old family servant woman leave immediately with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Ka ɔ́su lɛni ni, bédie Rebeka ulu ku ulekubi ɔni mɔɔnu-nwu lɛlɔ ku Abraham kabla ɔblane ɔmmɔ ku bekyuli bani nsiaku-nwu.

ref: They gave Rebekah their blessing and said, “We pray that God will give you many children and grandchildren and that he will help them defeat their enemies.”
text: Béte Rebeka kusɛ kusɛ na básu-nwu banini, “Bula ɔnwani ulekubi, buuvele Katɔti bɔnii ɔ́ya-a na awɔ bebi pii na bemomo liti liti. Na ɔya na bawɔ bamɛni badi bana bekisine ƒui lɛlɔ betédu bana kɔyakun muedi.”

ref: Afterwards, Rebekah and the young women who were to travel with her prepared to leave. Then they got on camels and left with Abraham's servant and his men.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Rebeka ku belekubi bani ƒui mɔɔbla-nwu kabla báklibi lɛlɔ na bákɔ bélie bebuo ala betine bammɔ lɛlɔ na ama ku ɔnana ɔmmɔ báyu ulu bátɔlɔ. Anwuɔsu ɔnana ɔmmɔ ɔ́kpanku Rebeka ɔ́tɔlɔku.

ref: At that time Isaac was living in the southern part of Canaan near a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.”
text: Uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo kani ni, Isak ɔ́si kesii kani baalɔ banii Beer Lahai Roi na Negev ketele kani kemmo na úlie ɔdubo.

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
text: “Ɔma ndi ɔnana ɔni mɔɔnu teemene ngye ka ɔ́si ɔdubo ubasiaku-bu?” Amu na ala lɛlɔ ɔnune ɔmmɔ údie kenye ɔnini, “Mina ɔtɔlati ɔmmɔ na ɔdi.” Mmɔ kani na Rebeka údie lɛdiku lɛni na bana beleku beebi anumi mmɔ úbi nwuna anumi.

ref: Isaac took Rebekah into the tent where his mother had lived before she died, and Rebekah became his wife. He loved her and was comforted over the loss of his mother.
text: Mmɔ ni, Isak ɔ́kpanku Rebeka na bétéduo uni Sara kevo kemmo na úti-nwu. Na ɔ́si uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Rebeka únwini Isak ulekudi ɔni na ɔ́yɔ-nwu geen, na Isak kayi kábla-nwu buɛɛ na uni kukpi kama.

ref: and they had six sons: Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
text: Uleku ɔmɛni ɔ́wɔ Simran ku Yoksan ku Medan ku Midian ku Isbak ku Sua úte Abraham.

ref: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text: Na Yoksan ɔ́wɔ Seba ku Dedan. Na Dedan ɔmɛni bebi ku bawɔ ndi Asurƒuɔ ku Letusƒuɔ ku Leumƒuɔ.

ref: Midian also had five sons: Ephah, Epher, Hanoch, Abida, and Eldaah.
text: Na Midian bebi ndi Heƒa ku Heƒer ku Hanok ku Abida ku Eldaa. Na bebi bamɛni ƒui badi Ketura bawɔ.

ref: While Abraham was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of Hagar and Keturah. He also sent their sons to live in the east far from his son Isaac, and when Abraham died, he left everything to Isaac. The Death of Abraham
text: Nsu na nwuna ngba kemmo ni, úte nwuna bebi bani na beleku bamba báwɔ béte-nwu ƒui ɔdi biala nwuna kadikɔ na údie-ma ulu na úbu Isak lɛti pɛ.

ref: God blessed Isaac after this, and Isaac moved to a place called “The Well of the Living One Who Sees Me.” Ishmael's Descendants
text: Na Abraham kukpi kama ni, Katɔti úte Isak kusɛ kusɛ. Na uwi ɔmmɔ ni, Isak ɔmu ɔkya kalɔ kani nde kesii kani baalɔ banii Beer Lahai-Roi kemmo. Ismael Bebi Anyi

ref: Ishmael was the son of Abraham and Hagar, the slave woman of Sarah.
text: Abraham ulekubi kabla ɔblane ɔni baalɔ Hagar ubi ɔni na ɔ́wɔ úte Abraham na baalɔ-nwu Ismael ni, nwuna lɛlɔ kɔtɛ na bɔdubo bunu mɛni.

ref: Ishmael had twelve sons, in this order: Nebaioth, Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam,
text: Ismael bebi bani na ɔ́wɔ atɔ asia ndi Nebaiot ubi unengudi, ɔmmɔ kama ni, ɔ́wɔ Kedar ku Adbeel ku Mibsam.

ref: Hadad, Tema, Jetur, Naphish, and Kedemah.
text: Nwuna kama ɔ́wɔ Hadad ku Tema ku Yetur ku Naƒis ku Kedema.

ref: Each of Ishmael's sons was a tribal chief, and a village was named after each of them.
text: Bebi bammɔ na Ismael ɔ́wɔ. Na bédie atɔƒi leevu-anyɔ ku bana batɔnkune na kalɛ na béti báda bana kuliekɔ kenye.

ref: Ishmael had settled in the land east of his brothers, and his sons settled everywhere from Havilah to Shur, east of Egypt on the way to Asshur. Ishmael was
text: Na nwuna bebi ku bawɔ ni, báda kuliekɔ na kalɔ kani nwi Hawila úsie Sur kesii kani nde Egyipte ɔmii kudɔ si éti ésela Asiria kakɛ. Nsu béteduku munu munu, mmomu bélie mmɔ aani bekisine. Isak Ɔ́wɔ Yakob Ku Esau

ref: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
text: Na Rebeka ɔmɛni ni, ɔdi udinkɔ. Anwuɔsu ni, Isak ɔ́kpali Katɔti na nwuna lili na nwuna tawɔ tawɔ ɔsu. Na Unwoti Katɔti lɛnɔ únu-nwu bɔkpali na ɔ́ya na ulekudi Rebeka úbada kaƒɔ.

ref: Before Rebekah gave birth, she knew she was going to have twins, because she could feel them inside her, fighting each other. She thought, “Why is this happening to me?” Finally, she asked the Lord why her twins were fighting,
text: Na kaƒɔ kani na ɔda mmɔ ni, Rebeka ɔ́ti ka alakpa anyɔ adi eekpóku lɛlɔ na nwuna kaƒɔ kemmo. Mmɔ na úye lɛlɔ ɔnini, “Bi ɔsu na lɛla lɛmɛni udu lɛɛlɛ́ku-mi?” Ɔmmɔ ɔsu úsie útákpali Katɔti na úvele-nwu ka ɔya na ɔti lɛla lɛmmɔ kalɔ.

ref: and he told her: “Your two sons will become two separate nations. The younger of the two will be stronger, and the older son will be his servant.”
text: Amu na Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Kadi bekyuli akpa anyɔ nkya-a kaƒɔ kemmo. Na nkyuli inyɔ bani nási-ɔ kaƒɔ kemmo ni, badubo lɛlɔ bule. Na lɛkpa linwi lɛdubɔkɛ ɔyikun litulo lɛkladi. Ubi unengudi ɔdubunwini ɔnamudi kabla ɔblane.”

ref: the first baby was covered with red hair, so he was named Esau.
text: Udeedi ɔni náka bɔlɛ ni, úgyie. Na nwuna lɛlɔ ƒui linwini lígyie. Ɔmmɔ ɔsu básia-nwu lɛnyi banii Esau.

ref: As Jacob and Esau grew older, Esau liked the outdoors and became a good hunter, while Jacob lived the quiet life of a shepherd.
text: Bebi bammɔ bálɛ lili na Esau úbenwini ɔgbini geen, na aku kemmo kani na ɔɔví. Na Yakob anwu ni, nwuna udu use kpoo ɔni na uwi biala kevo kemmo kani na ɔkya.

ref: Esau would take the meat of wild animals to his father Isaac, so Isaac loved him more, but Jacob was his mother's favorite son.
text: Ka Isak ɔɔyɔ́ alakpi akɔ lɛna ɔsu ni, anwu ɔɔyɔ́ Esau litemi geen. Nsu Rebeka anwu ni, Yakob na ɔɔyɔ́.

ref: One day, when Jacob was cooking some stew, Esau came home hungry
text: Limeyi lɛdi ka Yakob ɔkya ɔɔbla kutu ƒɛƒɛɛ kɔdi ni, Esau ɔ́si kabuinkɔ úbeduo na kɔka kɔ́du-nwu ƒui.

ref: and said, “I'm starving to death! Here and now give me some of that red stew!” That's how Esau got the name “Edom.”
text: Amu na ɔ́su Yakob ɔnini, “Babla na ete-mi ƒula kutu kugyiedi kɔmmɔ kɔdi, ɔsika kɔka kɔ́du-mi ƒui.” Ɔmmɔ ɔsu na basɔ baalɔ-nwu banii Edom ɔni kalɔ lɛnɔ ndi ugyiedi.

ref: “I'm about to die,” Esau answered. “What good will those rights do me?”
text: Mmɔ ni, Esau údie kenye ɔnini, “Nu, bukpi na nda! Bi na lɛwɔnengu lɛdubɔbla-mi?”

ref: But Jacob said, “Promise me your birthrights, here and now!” And that's what Esau did.
text: Amu na Yakob ɔnini, “Ka Katɔti bɔkpali na ese-mi kenye!” Anwuɔsu Esau ɔ́kpali Katɔti na úse-nwu kenye ka úti nwuna lɛwɔnengu úte Yakob.

ref: Jacob then gave Esau some bread and some of the bean stew, and when Esau had finished eating and drinking, he just got up and left, showing how little he thought of his rights as the first-born.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Yakob úti bodobodo ɔdi ku kutu buni kɔni na ɔ́bla mmɔ úte Esau na ɔ́di na ɔ́ni na ɔ́ta ɔ́tɔlɔ. Anwuɔsu Esau úni nwuna lɛwɔnengu ƒui.

ref: Once during Abraham's lifetime, the fields had not produced enough grain, and now the same thing happened. So Isaac went to King Abimelech of the Philistines in the land of Gerar,
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, kɔka kɔdi kúbo kalɔ kammɔ ƒui lɛlɔ aani kalɛ na ɔ́lɛ na Abraham uwi úbavi dii. Na Isak úsie Ƒilistiƒuɔ ɔga ɔni baalɔ banii Abimelek kudɔ na kesii kani baalɔ Gera.

ref: because the Lord had appeared to Isaac and said: Isaac, stay away from Egypt! I will show you where I want you to go.
text: Katɔti údie lɛlɔ ɔ́tina Isak na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Tatɔlɔ tesie Egyipte kalɔ lɛlɔ. Mmomu sɔ lie kalɔ kani na lɛ́tina-a lɛlɔ.

ref: You will live there as a foreigner, but I will be with you and bless you. I will keep my promise to your father Abraham by giving this land to you and your descendants.
text: Sɔ kya kalɔ kammɔ kemmo aani ɔƒɔ. Na ndubulieku-ɔ na lite-ɔ kusɛ kusɛ. Ɔsika awɔ ku ƒula bebi ku bawɔ na ndubuti kalɔ kamɛni lite. Na kenye kani na líse ƒ'ɔti Abraham líse ni, ndubɔbla lɛsia lɛni.

ref: I will give you as many descendants as there are stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of this land. They will be a blessing to every nation on earth,
text: Na ƒula bawɔ ni, ndubɔya na bemomo aani ayabi ani na aanu na kayaa. Na ɔkɛ ƒula kaba kemmo na ndubute kayawi ƒui bekyuli kusɛ kusɛ.

ref: with his beautiful wife Rebekah. He was afraid that someone might kill him to get her, and so he told everyone that Rebekah was his sister.
text: Amu ka kadi kammɔ bekyuli béye-nwu ulekudi lɛlɔ litemi ni, ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Mina ɔnwani na ɔdi.” Ɔsika na nwuna kakali kemmo ni, ɔ́su ɔnini, “Kadi kamɛni banana badubo-mi bɔdu na Rebeka ɔsu, ɔsika ɔɔdia bɔnu geen.”

ref: After Isaac had been there a long time, King Abimelech looked out a window and saw Isaac hugging and kissing Rebekah.
text: Ka Isak úlie mmɔ útu uwi geen kama ni, Abimelek unye nwuna ƒansili na ɔ́nu Isak úmene ka ɔda ulekudi ɔɔƒuƒwɛ na ɔɔƒianku-nwu.

ref: Abimelech called him in and said, “Rebekah must be your wife! Why did you say she is your sister?” “Because I thought someone would kill me,” Isaac answered.
text: Mmɔ ni, Abimelek ɔ́ya na bálɔ Isak béte-nwu na úye-nwu ɔnini, “Ƒ'ulekudi na ɔdi nka asa na ásu-bu anii ƒula ɔnwani na ɔdi?” Amu na Isak údie kenye ɔnini, “Lɛ́yia ka si lɛ́su nnii mina uleku na ɔdi ni, badubo-mi bɔdu.”

ref: “Don't you know what you've done?” Abimelech exclaimed. “If someone had slept with her, you would have made our whole nation guilty!”
text: Amu na Abimelek ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Bi ɔsu na ɔlɛka abla-bu lɛni? Si busie na ɔnana ɔdi úusie uleku ɔmɛni kudɔ ni, nka áya na bɔ́ƒiɔ Katɔti na ɔdubɔni-bu kɔtu.”

ref: Then Abimelech warned his people that anyone who even touched Isaac or Rebekah would be put to death.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu Abimelek ɔ́su nwuna kadi bekyuli ɔnini, “Ukyuli biala na mekpoku ɔgya ku ule ɔmɛni na ulu ɔdi lɛlɔ ni, ndubɔya na badu-nwu.”

ref: and Isaac was so successful that he became very rich.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Isak úbenwini ɔlakɛne. Na lɛni kani na nwuna lɛkɛ lɛmmɔ lɛɛkɔ́ katɔ teebu uwi biala kemmo.

ref: In fact, the Philistines were jealous of the large number of sheep, goats, and slaves that Isaac owned,
text: Ubo baƒonu ku ɔmantɛ bakɔ pii. Na ubo kabla bablane ɔni na ɔ́ya na Ƒilistiƒuɔ anumi égyie na Isak lɛlɔ.

ref: and they stopped up the wells that Abraham's servants had dug before his death.
text: Na ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Ƒilistiƒuɔ béti lɛti béyile abiɔ ani na Abraham ɔ́ya na nwuna kabla bablane béku mmɔ ƒui uwi ɔni na úlie ngba.

ref: Finally, Abimelech said, “Isaac, I want you to leave our country. You have become too powerful to stay here.”
text: Amu na Abimelek ɔ́su Isak ɔnini, “Tɔlɔ-bu kalɔ lɛlɔ mɛni. Bɔɔnu ka ábakɛ ɔyikun étulo-bu ƒui.”

ref: where he cleaned out those wells that the Philistines had stopped up. Isaac also gave each of the wells the same name that Abraham had given to them.
text: Ka ɔ́tɔlɔ mmɔ kama ni, Isak ɔ́ya na básɔ bálɛmi abiɔ ani na béku ɔ́si ɔti uwi ƒui ani na Ƒilistiƒuɔ béyile mmɔ. Na ɔ́sɔku anyi ani kani na ɔti ɔ́sia abiɔ ammɔ ɔ́sia-nya.

ref: While his servants were digging in the valley, they found a spring-fed well.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Isak ɔ́ya na nwuna kabla bablane béku ɔkwalɛ kesii kani na úsie uli mmɔ. Na bánu libiɔ ku ntu bɔbuni bɔdi.

ref: But the shepherds of Gerar Valley quarreled with Isaac's shepherds and claimed the water belonged to them. So this well was named “Quarrel,” because they had quarreled with Isaac.
text: Nsu Gerar bebuo badane ku Isak bebuo lɛlɔ banune bádɔ kadɔ geen na libiɔ lɛmmɔ ɔsu na básu banii, “Abu mbo ntu bɔmɛni.” Ɔmmɔ ɔsu na Isak ɔ́sia libiɔ lɛmmɔ lɛnyi ɔnii Unyeƒi, ɔsika mmɔ na ama ku nwuna badi bádɔ.

ref: Isaac's servants dug another well, and the shepherds also quarreled about it. So that well was named “Jealous.”
text: Na ɔ́ya na bésie bétéku libiɔ bamba. Na lɛmmɔ lɛnɔ líboku kadɔ. Na ɔ́sia libiɔ lɛmmɔ lɛnɔ lɛnyi ɔnii Lɛnungyie.

ref: Finally, they dug one more well. There was no quarreling this time, and the well was named “Lots of Room,” because the Lord had given them room and would make them very successful.
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, ɔ́sɔ ɔ́tɔlɔ mmɔ lɛnɔ úsie útéku libiɔ bamba. Na ɔdi ɔ́sɔ útakya-nwu kadɔ kadi. Na ɔ́sia-ni lɛnyi ɔnii Rehobot iye Kuƒi Kɔtɛ. Na údie-nwu kalɔ ɔnini, “Ɔƒɛni amu Unwoti Katɔti úte-bu keliekɔ, na bɔdubɔlɛ lili na kalɔ kamɛni kemmo.”

ref: Isaac went on to Beersheba,
text: Ka Isak ɔ́si mmɔ ni, úti ɔ́kɔ úsie Beer-Seba kakɛ.

ref: where the Lord appeared to him that night and told him, “Don't be afraid! I am the God who was worshiped by your father Abraham, my servant. I will be with you and bless you, and because of Abraham I will give you many descendants.”
text: Usien ɔmmɔ ni, Unwoti Katɔti údie lɛlɔ ɔtina-nwu na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ami ndi ƒula ɔti Abraham Katɔti. Tayia, ɔsika nsiaku-ɔ. Ndubute-ɔ kusɛ kusɛ na lɛya na ƒula bebi ku bawɔ bemomo bebu na mina kabla ɔblane Abraham ɔsu.”

ref: Isaac built an altar there and worshiped the Lord. Then he set up camp, and his servants started digging a well.
text: Na mmɔ ni, Isak ɔ́tu lɛkpabi lɛdi na ɔ́kpali Katɔti úmwi-nwu na mmɔ. Na ɔmmɔ kama ɔ́tu nwuna kevo, na ɔ́ya na nwuna kabla bablane béku libiɔ lɛdi na mmɔ.

ref: Meanwhile, Abimelech had left Gerar and was taking his advisor Ahuzzath and his army commander Phicol to see Isaac.
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Abimelek ɔ́si Gerar úbo Isak kudɔ na kesii kawɔdi kani na úsie útáda nwuna keliekɔ mɛni. Na nwuna kasusu betene akɔ unwi ɔni baalɔ Ahusa ku nwuna bekpone ɔtɔnkune ɔni baalɔ Pikol basiaku-nwu.

ref: When they arrived, Isaac asked, “Why are you here? Didn't you send me away because you hated me?”
text: Mmɔ ni, Isak úye-ma ɔnini, “Bi na bɛɛwia-mi kudɔ mɛni ka bɛ́bla-mi lili ɔkplu na bɛ́gya-mi na lɛ́tɔlɔ bina kudɔ?”

ref: They answered, “We now know for certain that the Lord is with you, and we have decided there needs to be a peace treaty between you and us. So let's make a solemn agreement
text: Mmɔ na bédie kenye banini, “Bɔ́nu bɔ́ti nwaa ka Unwoti Katɔti ɔsiaku-ɔ, anwuɔsu bɔ́su lɛlɔ bɔnini, ‘Bunyimi bɔdi ɔlɛka bɔkya abu ku-ɔ ntɛ.’ Anwuɔsu na búbo ka abu ku awɔ bunyimi lɛla lɛdi aani likposela ka bula ƒui bulie na atu bɔyɔ kemmo,

ref: not to harm each other. Remember, we have never hurt you, and when we sent you away, we let you go in peace. The Lord has truly blessed you.”
text: na alabla-bu lɛla likpidi lɛdi, kalɛ kani na abu lɛlɔ bútabla-a lɛla likpidi uwi ɔni na ákya-bu kudɔ, mmomu bɔ́da-a kaduɛ na bɔ́ya na átɔlɔ buɛɛ. Na ɔƒɛni ni, bɔ́nu bɔ́ti ka Unwoti Katɔti úte-ɔ kusɛ kusɛ.”

ref: Isaac gave a big feast for them, and everyone ate and drank.
text: Amu na Isak ɔ́ya na bánwa adila abuni geen béte-ma na bádi na báni na bákɛ ebiti kaduɛ.

ref: Early the next morning Isaac and the others made a solemn agreement, then he let them go in peace.
text: Ka kadi kégye ɔtanta ni, belusiene bammɔ báta na ama ku Isak bényimi ala kalɛ kani na básu. Na Isak údie-ma ulu na bátɔlɔ. Na anwu ɔ́kya keliekɔ kawɔdi kammɔ, na atu áyɔ-nwu.

ref: Later that same day Isaac's servants came and said, “We've struck water!”
text: Limeyi lɛmmɔ kani lɛlɔ ni, Isak kabla bablane bébo na bábasu-nwu ka libiɔ lɛni na bétéku ni, bákɛ ntu!

ref: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
text: Anwuɔsu Isak ɔ́sia libiɔ lɛmmɔ lɛnyi ɔnii Siba, ɔni kalɔ ndi Lilidia. Na úti úbedu ku ɔmwi ni, ɔma ɔmmɔ lɛnyi ndi Beer Seeba.

ref: But these two women brought a lot of grief to Esau's parents Isaac and Rebekah.
text: Lɛla lɛmɛni lɛdi lɛla lɛni nékpoku Isak ku ulekudi Rebeka geen.

ref: Isaac was old and almost blind, when he called in his first-born son Esau, who asked him, “Father, what can I do for you?”
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Isak ɔ́ɔbi geen na nwuna anumi muedi asɔ alákana bɔnu. Na ɔ́lɔ ubidi unengudi ɔnii, “Ubi Esau.” Na ubidi lɛnɔ ɔ́tuna ɔnini, “Ati, aanu-mi mɛni.”

ref: Isaac replied, “I am old and might die at any time.
text: Amu na Isak ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ɔƒɛni amu líinwini unengu geen na lɛɛɛgyi limeyi lɛni na ndubo bukpi.

ref: So go hunting with your bow and arrows and kill a wild animal.
text: Anwuɔsu ti ƒula kataminta na esie kabuinkɔ atáwia lɛna lɛdi eboku-mi.

ref: Cook some of that tasty food that I love so much and bring it to me. I want to eat it once more and give you my blessing before I die.”
text: Na si ébo ni, na anwa adila abuni ani na mɔɔyɔ́ teetulo ƒui eboku-mi na lɛdi kalɛ na liti mina kusɛ kusɛ lite-ɔ asa na likpi.”

ref: she said to Jacob, “I heard your father tell Esau
text: Rebeka úsie útásu ubidi Yakob ɔnini, “Nu, línu ka ƒ'ɔti ɔ́su ƒ'ɔnwani Esau ɔnini,

ref: to kill a wild animal and cook some tasty food for him before he dies. Your father said this because he wants to bless your brother with the Lord as his witness.
text: ‘Sie na atáwia lɛku kemmo ebuo ɔdi eboku-mi, na lite-ɔ mina kusɛ kusɛ na Katɔti anumi asa na mekpi.’

ref: Now, my son, listen carefully to what I want you to do.
text: Ɔƒɛni ni, mina ubi, se atu na enu kaduɛ. Na abla lɛla lɛni masu-ɔ.

ref: Go and kill two of your best young goats and bring them to me. I'll cook the tasty food that your father loves so much.
text: Sie bula bebuo akɔ na atádu bese bakɔdi inyɔ eboku-mi. Na ndubuti-ma lɛnwa adila abuni ani udu na ƒula ɔti ɔɔyɔ́ lite-nwu kalɛ geen na ɔwia-nya.

ref: Then you can take it to him, so he can eat it and give you his blessing before he dies.”
text: Na si lɛ́wɔlɔ ni, na eti-nya esienku ƒ'ɔti na ɔdi kalɛ na uti nwuna kusɛ kusɛ ute-ɔ asa na wekpi.”

ref: “My brother Esau is a hairy man,” Jacob reminded her. “And I am not.
text: Mmɔ ni, Yakob úye uni Rebeka ɔnini, “Átanu ka Esau ubo linwini na lɛlɔ na ami mina ɔlɔna use suɛlɛ suɛlɛ?

ref: If my father touches me and realizes I am trying to trick him, he will put a curse on me instead of giving me a blessing.”
text: Si mina ɔti útuku-mi ɔ́nu na ɔ́ti ka ami na ɔdi na líbo mɔɔtɔlɔ-nwu ni, ɔɔɔdi buwida mmomu na ɔdubo-mi?”

ref: Rebekah insisted, “Let his curse fall on me! Just do what I say and bring me the meat.”
text: Amu na uni ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Awɔ bla lɛla lɛni na lɛ́su-ɔ na ewida ammɔ ebo-mi lɛlɔ. Sie pla na etéti lɛna lɛmmɔ eboku-mi.”

ref: So Jacob brought the meat to his mother, and she cooked the tasty food that his father liked.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu úsie na úti lɛna lɛmmɔ úboku uni na ɔ́nwa adila abuni ammɔ aani kalɛ na ɔti ɔɔyɔ́.

ref: Then she took Esau's best clothes and put them on Jacob.
text: Na ka ɔ́wɔlɔ ni, Rebeka úsie útéti ubidi unengudi Esau lɛwu lɛbuni geen lɛni nse-nwu kudɔ na úti-ni úboku úbakya Yakob.

ref: She also covered the smooth part of his hands and neck with goatskins
text: Na ɔmmɔ kama úti bese lɛku lɛmmɔ úbi nwuna enu ku limɔ kakɛ kesii kani na awu ɔmmɔ útebi.

ref: and gave him some bread and the tasty food she had cooked.
text: Na úti adila ani na ɔ́nwa ku bodobodo ɔni na úƒi mmɔ úte Yakob ɔnii usienku ɔti.

ref: Jacob went to his father and said, “Father, here I am.” “Which one of my sons are you?” his father asked.
text: Amu na úti adila ammɔ úsienku na ɔ́kpali ɔnini, “Ati.” Mmɔ ni, ɔti lɛnɔ ɔ́tuna ɔnini, “Mina ubi, awɔ ɔma na ɔdi?”

ref: Jacob replied, “I am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.”
text: Na Yakob údie kenye ɔnini, “Ami, ƒula ubi unengudi Esau, na ɔdi. Na lɛla lɛni na anii mbla ni, lɛ́bla kalɛ kani na ásu-mi. Buvele, ta lie, na akun lɛna lɛni na ńtábui líboku-ɔ kalɛ na ete-mi ƒula kusɛ kusɛ.”

ref: Isaac asked, “My son, how did you find an animal so quickly?” “The Lord your God was kind to me,” Jacob answered.
text: Amu na Isak úye ubidi ɔnini, “Dɛ na ɔ́bla asa na ákɛ ebuo ádu pla lɛni?” Mmɔ na Yakob údie kenye ɔnini, “Ƒula Unwoti Katɔti náya na lɛku lɛtɛnku-mi lɛni.”

ref: “My son,” Isaac said, “come closer, where I can touch you and find out if you really are Esau.”
text: Amu na Isak ɔ́su Yakob ɔnini, “Sie bo-mi kudɔ na lituku-ɔ lɛnu si sɔsɔ na mina ubi Esau na adi.”

ref: Jacob went closer. His father touched him and said, “You sound like Jacob, but your hands feel hairy like Esau's.”
text: Mmɔ ni, Yakob ɔ́ni úde ɔti Isak na útuku-nwu. Na ɔ́su ɔnini, “Yakob lɛdi na muunú, nsu enu ammɔ adi Esau enu.”

ref: And so Isaac blessed Jacob, thinking he was Esau.
text: Na útanu-nwu útati, ɔsika linwini liwi-nwu enu aani Esau kalɛ. Ɔmmɔ ɔsu úte-nwu kusɛ kusɛ.

ref: Isaac asked, “Are you really my son Esau?” “Yes, I am,” Jacob answered.
text: Ka ɔ́wɔlɔ ni, Isak ɔ́sɔ úye Yakob ɔnini, “Sɔsɔ na mina ubi unengudi Esau na adi?” Amu na Yakob lɛnɔ údie kenye ɔnini, “Ami na ɔdi.”

ref: So Isaac told him, “Serve me the wild meat, and I can give you my blessing.” Jacob gave him some meat, and he ate it. He also gave him some wine, and he drank it.
text: Mmɔ kani Isak ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Mina ubi, ti lɛla lɛni na átádu mmɔ boku-mi na lɛkun na lite-ɔ mina kusɛ kusɛ.” Amu na Yakob úti ala ammɔ úboku-nwu na ɔ́di. Na úti ntɛ mini mini bɔdi lɛnɔ úboku-nwu na ɔ́ni.

ref: Then Isaac said, “Son, come over here and kiss me.”
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Isak ɔ́su ubidi ɔnini, “Ɔƒɛni ni, bo na abakya-mi kɔgbu.”

ref: While Jacob was kissing him, Isaac caught the smell of his clothes and said: “The smell of my son is like a field the Lord has blessed.
text: Anwuɔsu úsie na útákya-nwu kɔgbu. Na ka Isak únu Yakob adala ani na ɔkya ɔnyi ni, ɔ́su ɔnini, “Pɛ, mina ubi ɔnyi na ɔdi mɛni, na use aani katumalɔ ɔnyi kalɛ kani na Unwoti Katɔti úte-ka kusɛ kusɛ.

ref: God will bless you, my son, with dew from heaven and with fertile fields, rich with grain and grapes.
text: Muuvele Unwoti Katɔti nnii nwuna katɔ kusɛ kusɛ uwi-ɔ lɛlɔ aani amii, na ute-ɔ kalɔ kani nélie lɛmwi. Na akɛ edula pii ku ntɛ bɔwɔdi.

ref: Nations will be your servants and bow down to you. You will rule over your brothers, and they will kneel at your feet. Anyone who curses you will be cursed; anyone who blesses you will be blessed.”
text: Na kadi bekyuli ƒui benwini ƒula kabla bablane. Na bekyuli ƒui bakya-a lili kalɔ na adi ƒula banwani ƒui lɛlɔ. Na ƒ'ini bebi bananabi ƒui bakya-a lili kalɔ. Na bekyuli bani mewida-a ƒui ni, ewida asiaku-ma. Na bani mete-ɔ kusɛ kusɛ ni, kusɛ kusɛ ɔsiaku-ma.”

ref: Right after Isaac had given Jacob his blessing and Jacob had gone, Esau came back from hunting.
text: Uwi ɔni Isak úte Yakob kusɛ kusɛ ɔ́wɔlɔ na Yakob ɔ́vi ɔɔlɛ́ kani na Esau lɛnɔ ɔ́si lɛku kemmo úbeduo.

ref: He cooked the tasty food, brought it to his father, and said, “Father, please sit up and eat the meat I have brought you, so you can give me your blessing.”
text: Na adila ani na ɔ́nwa ni, úti-nya úboku ɔti. Na ɔ́su ɔti ɔnini, “Ati, buvele, ta lie kalɔ na akun lɛna lɛni na ńtádu na lɛ́nwa liboku-ɔ. Na ete-mi ƒula kusɛ kusɛ.”

ref: “Who are you?” Isaac asked. “I am Esau, your first-born son.”
text: Amu na Isak úye-nwu ɔnini, “Awɔ, ɔma na ɔdi?” Mmɔ na Esau údie kenye ɔnini, “Ƒula ubi unengudi Esau na ɔdi.”

ref: Isaac started trembling and said, “Then who brought me some wild meat right before you came in? I ate it and gave him a blessing that cannot be taken back.”
text: Amu na Isak ɔ́ta ku kɔnyi na lɛlɔ lɛɛtɔ́-nwu. Na úye ɔnini, “Na ɔma na ɔdi nésie útábui na úti lɛna úboku-mi na lɛ́kun? Ani na lɛ́kun mɔɔwɔlɔ mɛni asa na ébo, na líte-nwu mina kusɛ kusɛ. Na sɔsɔ ni, mina kusɛ kusɛ ɔdubo-nwu bɔsiaku!”

ref: Isaac answered, “Your brother tricked me and stole your blessing.”
text: Nsu ɔti ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ƒ'ɔnwani úbo úbatɔlɔ-mi na ɔ́ƒɔ ƒula kusɛ kusɛ.”

ref: Esau replied, “My brother deserves the name Jacob, because he has already cheated me twice. The first time he cheated me out of my rights as the first-born son, and now he has cheated me out of my blessing.” Then Esau asked his father, “Don't you have any blessing left for me?”
text: Amu na Esau ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ɔɔɔdi keeke na baalɔ-nwu banii Yakob ɔni kalɔ ndi ala bukpeli taaƒɔ. Ɔ́tɔlɔ-mi kunyɔ kɔmɛni ƒui. Ɔ́ƒɔ-mi lɛwɔnengu. Na ɔ́sɔ ɔ́ƒɔ-mi kusɛ kusɛ.” Na ɔmmɔ kama ni, úye ɔti ɔnini, “Anwuɔsu étebu kusɛ kusɛ ɔmmɔ ɔdi étese ami anwu?”

ref: “My son,” Isaac answered, “I have made Jacob the ruler over you and your brothers, and all of you will be his servants. I have also promised him all the grain and grapes that he needs. There's nothing left that I can do for you.”
text: Mmɔ na Isak údie kenye na ɔ́su Esau ɔnini, “Kusɛ kusɛ ɔni na líte-nwu ni, ɔ́ya na únwini lɛlɔ ɔdine. Na nwuna ɔtɔ kemmo bekyuli ƒui badubo-nwu kabla bablane bunwini. Na nwuna kaduli ni, antabi ku ntɛ bɔwɔdi bulása kudii. Na bi lɛnɔ na aakali ka ndubɔsɔ lɛkana bɔbla ɔƒɛni?”

ref: “Father,” Esau asked, “don't you have more than one blessing? You can surely give me a blessing too!” Then Esau started crying again.
text: Amu na Esau úye ɔti ɔnini, “Amu kunwi pɛ kani na adubɔkana ƒula kusɛ kusɛ bute? Ati, te ami lɛnɔ kusɛ kusɛ.” Ka ɔ́su ɔmɛni ɔ́wɔlɔ anwu kani na úse kalɔ uuwulo momoomo.

ref: So his father said: “Your home will be far from that fertile land, where dew comes down from the heavens.
text: Ka uuwulo lɛni ni, ɔti Isak ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Awɔ aaadubo lɛkɛ lɛdi bɔbla. Na adubo bɔgyunku kalɔ kabuni kani lɛlɔ na Katɔti kusɛ kusɛ ɔɔyu teewi aani amii kalɛ lɛlɔ.

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: Na awɔ ɔlati ƒula ɔkɛɛ na adubo bɔda asa na akɛ adi. Na ƒula ɔnwani kabla na adubo bɔbla. Nsu si uwi ɔni na nwuna ala asɔ eleduku-ɔ ni, adubɔlɛ nwuna kabla kalɔ aani kalɛ baada lɛla lɛni nényimi-ɔ teedie limɔ.” Yakob Úkeli Úsie Laban Kudɔ

ref: Esau hated his brother Jacob because he had stolen the blessing that was supposed to be his. So he said to himself, “Just as soon as my father dies, I'll kill Jacob.”
text: Na ala ani nálɛ ƒui ɔsu ni, Esau úti kɔnyi ɔ́kya Yakob geen, ɔsika ɔti Isak úti nwuna kusɛ kusɛ úte Yakob. Mmɔ na ɔ́su ɔnini, “Kébu blii na bula ɔti ukpi. Na si uwi ɔni na buuwulo-nwu ni, ndubo mina ɔnwani Yakob bɔdu.”

ref: When Rebekah found out what Esau planned to do, she sent for Jacob and told him, “Son, your brother Esau is just waiting for a chance to kill you.
text: Amu ɔni na básu Rebeka lɛla lɛni na ubidi unengudi ɔ́bla kasusu úse ka wabla ɔsu ni, ɔ́ya na bálɔ Yakob béte-nwu. Mmɔ na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ƒula ɔnwani úuse lɛlɔ kayi ku kasusu ka wadu-ɔ.

ref: Now listen carefully and do what I say. Go to the home of my brother Laban in Haran
text: Ɔmɛni ɔsu ni, babla na ekeli eteesie mina ɔnwani Laban kudɔ na Haraan ɔma kemmo.

ref: and stay with him for a while. When Esau stops being angry
text: Si ésie ni, na akya mmɔ blii wetédu uwi ɔni na ƒ'ɔnwani kɔnyi kɔ́yɔ-nwu.

ref: and forgets what you have done to him, I'll send for you to come home. Why should I lose both of my sons on the same day?”
text: Na si ƒ'ɔnwani ɔ́yiƒala lɛla lɛni na ábla-nwu na nwuna kɔnyi kɔ́yɔ-nwu ni, ndubɔtala bekyuli na babalɔ-ɔ. Lɛɛɛdubo bunye bɔnu na bina inyɔ ƒui bikpi-mi limeyi linwi.”

ref: Rebekah later told Isaac, “Those Hittite wives of Esau are making my life miserable! If Jacob marries a Hittite woman, I'd be better off dead.”
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Rebeka úsie útásu ɔgyani Isak ɔnini, “Mina ngba bɔni na nli buukpóku-mi na Hetiƒuɔ beleku bamɛni ablala ɔsu. Na si Yakob lɛnɔ úti Hetiƒuɔ beleku bamɛni akɔ ɔdi ni, amu nsɔ lɛɛɛgyi kesii kani na mina ngba búsela.”

ref: Isaac called in Jacob, then gave him a blessing, and said: Don't marry any of those Canaanite women.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Isak ɔ́ya na bálɔ Yakob béte-nwu na úte-nwu kusɛ kusɛ na úbo-nwu ɔda kpakplaa ɔnini, “Teti uleku ɔdi tasiku Kanaan.

ref: Go at once to your mother's father Bethuel in northern Syria and choose a wife from one of the daughters of Laban, your mother's brother.
text: Ta kɔnakunwi na eteesie Mesopotamia kalɔ lɛlɔ lɛlɔ na ƒula uni ɔti bene kaduli ɔni ndi Betuel kaduli. Na atáwia uleku eti na mmɔ na Laban ɔni ndi ƒula uni ɔnwani bebi belekubi akɔ.

ref: I pray that God All-Powerful will bless you with many descendants and let you become a great nation.
text: Unwoti Katɔti kusɛ kusɛ ɔsiaku-ɔ na ɔya na awɔ bebi na bemomo na benwini kadi bekyuli pii.

ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: Na Unwoti Katɔti uti kusɛ kusɛ ɔni na úte Abraham ute awɔ ku ƒula kaba kemmo bebi. Na ɔya na kalɔ kani kemmo na eli ɔmwi mɛni aani ɔƒɔ kalɛ kenwini amii kadi. Kalɔ kamɛni na Katɔti úti úte Abraham.”

ref: Isaac then sent Jacob to stay with Rebekah's brother Laban, the son of Bethuel the Aramean. Esau Marries the Daughter of Ishmael
text: Ɔmmɔ kama Isak údie Yakob ulu na ɔ́tɔlɔ úsie Mesopotamia kalɔ lɛlɔ na Laban kudɔ. Laban ɔdi Rebeka ɔni náwɔ Yakob ɔmɛni ku Esau ɔnwani, na bana ɔti ndi Betuel ɔni nási Aram.

ref: Esau found out that his father Isaac had blessed Jacob and had warned him not to marry any of the Canaanite women. He also learned that Jacob had been sent to find a wife in northern Syria
text: Ka Yakob ɔ́tɔlɔ ni, Esau úbenu ka ɔti Isak úte Yakob kusɛ kusɛ na údie-nwu ulu na ɔ́tɔlɔ úsie Mesopotamia kalɔ lɛlɔ ɔnii utéti uleku na mmɔ. Na uwi ɔni na úte-nwu kusɛ kusɛ ni, úbo-nwu ɔda ɔnini, “Teti Kanaanni uleku ɔdi.”

ref: and that he had obeyed his father and mother.
text: Na Yakob lɛnɔ únu ɔ́ti ku uni bɔlɛ na ɔ́tɔlɔ úsie Mesopotamia kalɔ lɛlɔ sɔsɔ.

ref: Esau already had several wives, but he now realized how much his father hated the Canaanite women.
text: Anwuɔsu Esau ɔ́nu ɔ́ti ka Kanaanƒuɔ beleku ala aláda ɔti Isak kubiti.

ref: So he married Ishmael's daughter Mahalath, who was the sister of Nebaioth and the granddaughter of Abraham. Jacob's Dream at Bethel
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Esau ɔ́tɔlɔ úsie Abraham ubidi Ismael kudɔ. Na útéti ubidi Mahalat ɔni ndi Nebaiot ɔnwani ulekubi úbunku Kanaanƒuɔ beleku inyɔ bani na úti. Yakob Úgye Kɔna Kɔdi Na Betel

ref: Jacob left the town of Beersheba and started out for Haran.
text: Na uwi ɔmmɔ kemmo ni, Yakob úuti ala na ɔ́yu ulu ɔ́si Beer-Seba úsela Haraan.

ref: At sunset he stopped for the night and went to sleep, resting his head on a large rock.
text: Ka útédu kesii kadi ni, ɔƒɛ úbasa-nwu, anwuɔsu ɔ́bla kasusu ka ɔdubɔtɛ mmɔ na kadi kégye. Amu na úti lɛtabi lɛdi ntɛ mmɔ na ɔ́sia lili nina lɛlɔ na ɔ́tɛ ɔlɛɛ.

ref: In a dream he saw a ladder that reached from earth to heaven, and God's angels were going up and down on it.
text: Ka ɔtɛ ni, úgye kɔna kɔdi na ɔ́nu ka ubili kɔnyakɔ kɔdi kɔ́si kalɔ kɔ́kɔ laa kútédu katɔ. Na ɔ́nu ka Katɔti batu baasia-nwu lɛlɔ taakɔ na baasia taayu.

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.
text: Na ubili kɔnyakɔ kɔmmɔ katɔ ƒui ni, ɔ́nu ka Unwoti Katɔti unye. Na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ami ndi Unwoti, ƒula ɔti Abraham ku Isak Katɔti. Na ndubuti kalɔ kani lɛlɔ na atɛ mɛni lite awɔ ku ƒula kaba kemmo bebi.

ref: Your descendants will spread over the earth in all directions and will become as numerous as the specks of dust. Your family will be a blessing to all people.
text: Na ƒula kaba kemmo bebi bamɛni badubo bumomo aani kɔnwinimi. Na bɛdubo bugyingya busie ɔƒɛ katukɔ ku kanukɔ. Na bisie katɔ ku kada kakɛ lɛnɔ. Na ɔkɛnku awɔ ku ƒula kaba kemmo bebi lɛlɔ na ndubute kayawi kadi bekyuli ƒui kusɛ kusɛ.

ref: Wherever you go, I will watch over you, then later I will bring you back to this land. I won't leave you—I will do all I have promised.
text: Nliku-ɔ na ndubɔsia-a anu kesii biala na aaví. Na ndubɔsɔku-ɔ liboku kalɔ kamɛni lɛlɔ. Lɛɛɛdubo-ɔ bɔnyonu buse uwi ɔdi wetédu ka lɛ́bla lɛla lɛni kenye na líse-ɔ ka mabla.”

ref: Jacob woke up suddenly and thought, “The Lord is in this place, and I didn't even know it.”
text: Ka Yakob ɔ́gyinya ni, ɔ́su ɔnini, “Sɔsɔ na ɔdi ka Unwoti Katɔti ɔkya mɛni na ami ńtati.”

ref: Then Jacob became frightened and said, “What a frightening place! It must be the house of God and the gateway to heaven.”
text: Ɔ́yia na ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ntɛ bɔmɛni bubo liƒu, ɔsika Unwoti Katɔti keliekɔ geen na kadi mɛni! Na kadi katɔ ɔban kayakun kammɔ geen kenye.”

ref: When Jacob got up early the next morning, he took the rock that he had used for a pillow and stood it up as a place of worship. Then he poured olive oil on the rock to dedicate it to God,
text: Ka kadi kégye ɔtanta ni, Yakob ɔ́ta na úti lɛtabi lɛni lɛlɔ na ɔ́sia lili mmɔ úku únyene na ɔ́bla akpala na mmɔ na úti nnɔ úwi-ni lili.

ref: and he named the place Bethel. Before that it had been named Luz.
text: Na ɔ́sia mmɔ lɛnyi ɔnii Betel, na nka ɔma ɔmmɔ na bátaalɔ banii Lus.

ref: Jacob solemnly promised God, “If you go with me and watch over me as I travel, and if you give me food and clothes
text: Amu na Yakob ɔ́kpali Unwoti Katɔti lɛnyi na úse kenye ɔnini, “Lieku-mi, na asia-mi anu na ulu busie bɔmɛni kemmo, na ete-mi adila ku adala.

ref: and bring me safely home again, you will be my God.
text: Na si lɛ́sɔ línwini líbo mina ɔti kaduli buɛɛ ni, ndubɔti ka awɔ ndi mina Unwoti Katɔti sɔsɔ.

ref: This rock will be your house, and I will give back to you a tenth of everything you give me.”
text: Na lɛtabi lɛni na lɛ́lɛ mɛni ni, lɛdubunye aani ƒula ɔtɔ kalɛ. Na ala ani ƒui na adubo-mi bute ni, ndubuti lɛkpa leevudi lite-ɔ.”

ref: he looked out in a field and saw a well where shepherds took their sheep for water. Three flocks of sheep were lying around the well, which was covered with a large rock.
text: Ka údu mmɔ ni, ɔ́nu ka baƒonu badi básala benye libiɔ lɛdi kɔtu na mmɔ, ɔsika mmɔ na beete-ma ntu na baaní. Na lɛtabi lɛni mbi libiɔ lɛmmɔ kenye ni, líbomo.

ref: Shepherds would roll the rock away when all their sheep had gathered there. Then after the sheep had been watered, the shepherds would roll the rock back over the mouth of the well.
text: Na si uwi ɔni na baƒonu bammɔ ƒui bábasa lisu ni, asa na baƒonu badane bammɔ beliiku lɛtabi lɛmmɔ na ubu ɔmmɔ kenye na bete baƒonu bammɔ ntu na bani. Na basɔku lɛtabi lɛmmɔ basia-nwu kenye.

ref: Jacob asked the shepherds, “Where are you from?” “We're from Haran,” they answered.
text: Amu na Yakob úye baƒonu badane bammɔ ɔnini, “Banwa, wɛ na bɛ́si?” Mmɔ na bédie kenye banini, “Bɔ́si Haran ɔma kemmo.”

ref: Then he asked, “Do you know Nahor's grandson Laban?” “Yes we do,” they replied.
text: Anwuɔsu Yakob úye-ma ɔnini, “Bɛgyi Laban ɔni na nwuna nana ndi Nahor?” Bédie kenye banini, “Ɛɛn, bɔgyi-nwu.”

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text: Mmɔ na Yakob úye-ma ɔnini, “Na ɔkya kaduɛ?” Bédie kenye banini, “Ɛɛn, ɔkya, aanu ubidi ulekubi ɔni baalɔ Rahel ndubo mmɔ ku baƒonu badi.”

ref: Jacob told them, “Look, the sun is still high up in the sky, and it's too early to bring in the rest of the flocks. Water your sheep and take them back to the pasture.”
text: Amu na ɔ́su-ma ɔnini, “Bɛnu, ɔƒɛ ɔmmɔ ɔɔbi na útedu tɔ ka baƒonu balɛ alakpi akɔ babasala tɔ kesii kadi. Anwuɔsu bite-ma ntu na bɛsɔku-ma binwini biteesienku kadikɔ.”

ref: But they replied, “We can't do that until they all get here, and the rock has been rolled away from the well.”
text: Mmɔ na bédie kenye banini, “Bulakana ɔmmɔ bɔbla tɔ, amu ntu bana ƒui bébo asa na buliiku lɛtabi lɛmmɔ na ubu ɔmmɔ kenye na bute-ma ntu.”

ref: While Jacob was still talking with the men, his cousin Rachel came up with her father's sheep.
text: Uwi ɔni na ɔmu unye ɔɔtɛmiku-ma ni, Rahel ɔ́da úbalɛ ku ɔti baƒonu, ɔsika baƒonu ɔdane na ɔdi.

ref: He then kissed Rachel and started crying because he was so happy.
text: Ka ɔ́wɔlɔ ni, ɔ́kya Rahel kɔgbu na úse kalɔ uuwulo momoomo.

ref: He told her that he was the son of her aunt Rebekah, and she ran and told her father about him.
text: Na ɔ́su Rahel ka ɔti lɛlɔ ukyuli na ɔdi na ɔdi Rebeka ubi ɔnanabi. Anwuɔsu Rahel úkeli na útásu ɔti lɛla lɛni mɔɔlɛ́.

ref: As soon as Laban heard the news, he ran out to meet Jacob. He hugged and kissed him and brought him to his home, where Jacob told him everything that had happened.
text: Ka Laban únu Yakob kɔku kani na úkeli útásiaku-nwu aani ɔnwani ubidi kalɛ. Útákya-nwu kɔgbu na ɔ́kpanku-nwu úsienku nwuna kaduli. Mmɔ na Yakob ɔ́sɔku lɛla biala ɔ́su-nwu kaduɛ.

ref: Laban said, “You are my nephew, and you are like one of my own family.” Jacob Marries Leah and Rachel After Jacob had been there for a month,
text: Amu Laban ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ami ɔlati ubugya ku ɔtɔ kemmo ubi geen na adi.” Yakob Úti Lea Ku Rahel Uwi ɔni na Yakob ɔ́ɔkya Laban kudɔ aani kɔbuli kunwi ni,

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Laban ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ina ka mina lɛlɔ ukyuli geen na adi ni, ɔɔɔdi anwuɔsu na ataabla-mi kabla na naaaƒɔ-ɔ kayu kadi. Anwuɔsu su-mi kayu kani na aayɔ́ ka ntaaƒɔ-ɔ.”

ref: Laban had two daughters. Leah was older than Rachel, but her eyes didn't sparkle, while Rachel was beautiful and had a good figure.
text: Na Lea ni, nwuna anumi aláda Yakob kayi. Nsu Rahel ni, nwuna anumi eekeye na ɔɔdia bɔnu geen.

ref: Since Jacob was in love with Rachel, he answered, “If you will let me marry Rachel, I'll work seven years for you.”
text: Na Yakob ni, Rahel ɔmɛni na ɔɔyɔ́ geen. Anwuɔsu na ɔ́su Laban ɔnini, “Ndubɔbla ƒula kabla akwa maatɛ na eti ƒula ubi Rahel ete-mi na liti.”

ref: Laban replied, “It's better for me to let you marry Rachel than for someone else to have her. So stay and work for me.”
text: Amu na Laban ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ɔdia ka liti ubi ɔmɛni lite-ɔ sɔsɔ utulo ka liti-nwu lite ɔnana bamba. Anwuɔsu sɔ kya-mi kudɔ mɛni.”

ref: Jacob worked seven years for Laban, but the time seemed like only a few days, because he loved Rachel so much.
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Yakob ɔ́bla kabla akwa maatɛ úte Laban kalɛ na ɔkɛ Rahel uti. Nsu ɔmɛni ƒui ɔ́bla Yakob aani emeyi alɛɛ kani na akwa ammɔ adi-nwu na kalɛ na ɔɔyɔ́ Rahel ɔsu.

ref: Jacob said to Laban, “The time is up, and I want to marry Rachel now!”
text: Ɔmmɔ kama ni, Yakob ɔ́su Laban ɔnini, “Ti mina uleku te-mi. Mina uwi úudu na mɔɔwia ka ami ku-nwu bulie ɔƒɛni amu.”

ref: So Laban gave a big feast and invited all their neighbors.
text: Amu na Laban ɔ́sala bekyuli bani nkya mmɔ ƒui. Na ɔ́ya na banwa adila kaduɛ na bádi limeyi lɛmmɔ.

ref: But that evening he brought Leah to Jacob, who married her and spent the night with her.
text: Nsu ka useyi uwi údu ni, úti ubidi unengudi ɔni ndi Lea úte Yakob. Na anwu ku-nwu bátɛ.

ref: Laban also gave Zilpah to Leah as her servant woman.
text: Na Laban úti uleku ɔni baalɔ Silpa úte Lea ka utaabla-nwu kabla.

ref: The next morning Jacob found out that he had married Leah, and he asked Laban, “Why did you do this to me? Didn't I work to get Rachel? Why did you trick me?”
text: Ka kadi kégye ni, aanu Lea ntɛ Yakob kudɔ. Anwuɔsu Yakob úye Laban ɔnini, “Bi ndi lɛla lɛni na ábla-mi mɛni? Lɛ́bla kabla líte-ɔ ka eti Rahel ete-mi. Na bi ɔsu na átɔlɔ-mi?”

ref: Laban replied, “In our country the older daughter must get married first.
text: Amu na Laban ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ɔɔɔdi bula ɔdi ka eti ubi ulekubi ɔnamudi ete kɔgya uwi ɔni na unengudi útelie tɔ kɔgya.

ref: After you spend this week with Leah, you may also marry Rachel. But you will have to work for me another seven years.”
text: Anwuɔsu wɔlɔ Lea buti ablala na uyolu ɔmɛni kemmo na liti Rahel lɛnɔ lite-ɔ. Na adubɔsɔ abla kabla akwa maatɛ abambadi na ɔmmɔ ɔsu.”

ref: At the end of the week of celebration, Laban let Jacob marry Rachel, and he gave her his servant woman Bilhah. Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, but he had to work another seven years for Laban.
text: Yakob ku Rahel lɛnɔ bátɛ. Na Yakob ɔ́yɔ Rahel útulo Lea. Na ɔ́sɔ ɔ́bla kabla úte Laban akwa maatɛ abambadi. Yakob Ɔ́wɔ Bebi

ref: The Lord knew that Jacob loved Rachel more than he did Leah, and so he gave children to Leah, but not to Rachel.
text: Ka Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́nu ka Yakob ulayɔ Lea kaduɛ ɔsu ni, ɔ́lɛmi-nwu kaƒɔ mmomu na anwu mɔɔwɔ. Na Rahel anwu uláwɔ.

ref: Leah gave birth to a son and named him Reuben. Then she said, “The Lord has taken away my sorrow. Now my husband will love me more than he does Rachel.”
text: Lea úbada kaƒɔ na ɔ́wɔ ubi ɔnanabi. Na ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Ruben, na nwuna kalɔ ndi Katɔti únu-mi kɔnyana buwulo. Na ɔ́su ɔnini, “Na ubi ɔmɛni ɔsu ni, mina ɔgyani ɔdubo-mi butaayɔ ɔƒɛni amu.”

ref: She had a second son and named him Simeon, because she said, “The Lord has heard that my husband doesn't love me.”
text: Lea ɔ́sɔ úbada kaƒɔ na ɔ́wɔ ɔnanabi unyɔdi. Na ɔmmɔ ɔsu ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ka Unwoti Katɔti únu ka mina ɔgyani ulayɔ-mi ɔsu na úte-mi ubi ɔmɛni lɛnɔ.” Anwuɔsu ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Simeon, na nwuna kalɔ ndi Katɔti únu.

ref: When Leah's third son was born, she said, “Now my husband will hold me close.” So this son was named Levi.
text: Na kɔtɛdi lɛnɔ ni, ɔ́sɔ ɔ́da kaƒɔ. Na ka ɔ́wɔ ɔnanabi ni, ɔ́su ɔnini, “Ɔƒɛni amu lɛ́ƒɔ lɛ́di ka mina ɔgyani ɔsɔ ulamami-mi lɛlɔ. Ɔsika lɛ́wɔ bananabi ɛtɛ sɔɔn líte-nwu.” Anwuɔsu ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Lewi, na nwuna kalɔ ndi lɛlɔ bɔdu bɔma.

ref: Rachel was very jealous of Leah for having children, and she said to Jacob, “I'll die if you don't give me some children!”
text: Ka Rahel ɔ́nu ka ɔɔɔdaku bebi badi bɔwɔ teete Yakob ni, úbo uukpóku ɔnwani Lea kogyulo. Anwuɔsu útásu Yakob ɔnini, “Te-mi bebi na lɛwɔ, amu ndubo bukpi!”

ref: But Jacob became upset with Rachel and answered, “Don't blame me! I'm not God.”
text: Ka ɔ́su Yakob lɛni ni, Yakob úbeti kɔnyi geen na úye-nwu ɔnini, “Ami nnye Unwoti Katɔti kenye na lítile-ɔ kɔwɔ?”

ref: “Here, take my servant Bilhah,” Rachel told him. “Have children by her, and I'll let them be born on my knees to show that they are mine.”
text: Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, Rahel ɔ́su Yakob ɔnini, “Aanu mina ubi ulekubi Bilha ɔni mɔɔbla-mi kabla. Awɔ ku-nwu bɛtɛ. Na ɔwɔ ubi ute-mi kalɛ na ɔkɛnku-nwu lɛlɔ na ami lɛnɔ lidie mina kaba.”

ref: and they had a son.
text: Na ka ɔ́da kaƒɔ ni, ɔ́wɔ ɔnanabi úte-nwu.

ref: Rachel named him Dan, because she said, “God has answered my prayers. He judged in my favor and has given me a son.”
text: Na Rahel ɔ́su ɔnini, “Unwoti Katɔti úte-mi ubo. Na únu-mi buvele na úte-mi ɔnanabi.” Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ni, ɔ́sia ɔnamu ɔmmɔ lɛnyi ɔnii Dan, na nwuna kalɔ ndi ubo bute.

ref: When Bilhah and Jacob had a second son,
text: Rahel kabla ɔblane Bilha ɔmɛni ɔ́sɔ ɔ́da kaƒɔ na ɔ́wɔ ɔnanabi unyɔdi.

ref: Rachel said, “I've struggled hard with my sister, and I've won!” So she named the boy Naphtali.
text: Amu na Rahel ɔ́su ɔnini, “Kekpo geen na líkpo ku mina ɔnwani ulekubi na lɛ́di lɛlɔ.” Anwuɔsu ɔ́sia ubi ɔmmɔ lɛnyi ɔnii Naƒtali, na nwuna kalɔ ndi kekpo.

ref: When Leah realized she could not have any more children, she let Jacob marry her servant Zilpah,
text: Amu ɔni na Lea ɔ́nu ka ɔsɔ uláwɔ ni, úti nwuna ulekubi ɔni mɔɔbla-nwu kabla na baalɔ-nwu Silpa úte Yakob aani ulekudi kalɛ.

ref: “I'm really lucky,” Leah said, and she named the boy Gad.
text: Mmɔ na Lea ɔ́su ɔnini, “Kuli kɔdia-mi geen!” Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Gad, na nwuna kalɔ ndi lilidia.

ref: When they had another son,
text: Lea ulekubi kabla ɔblane ɔmɛni ɔ́wɔ ɔnanabi unyɔdi.

ref: Leah exclaimed, “I'm happy now, and all the women will say how happy I am.” So she named him Asher. Love Flowers
text: Na Lea ɔ́su ɔnini, “Lɛlɔ lɛɛyɔ́-mi geen!” Na ɔ́sia ubi ɔmmɔ lɛnyi ɔnii Aser, na nwuna kalɔ ndi lɛlɔyɔ.

ref: During the time of the wheat harvest, Reuben found some love flowers and took them to his mother Leah. Rachel asked Leah for some of them,
text: Uwi ɔni na baaƒɛ edula ni, Lea ubidi Ruben úsie kemo na útákɛ alakpi adi ligyiitu na kadi kammɔ bekyuli bákali ka aayá na ukyuli ɔɔwɔ. Na úti-nya úboku uni Lea. Amu na Rahel ɔ́su Lea ɔnini, “Buvele, te-mi ƒula kɔwɔ alakpi ligyiitu lɛmmɔ lɛdi.”

ref: but Leah said, “It's bad enough that you stole my husband! Now you want my son's love flowers too.” “All right,” Rachel answered. “Let me have the flowers, and you can sleep with Jacob tonight.”
text: Nsu Lea údie kenye ɔnini, “Ɔni lɛti na áƒɔ-mi ɔgyani ébo lɛkpu mmɔ útabɔ-ɔ? Asɔ abaƒɔ-mi kɔwɔ alakpi ligyiitu lɛnɔ?” Amu na Rahel ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ɔdia ndubɔya na ubo na bɛtɛ ɔmwi usien ɔmɛni na ƒula ubidi kɔwɔ alakpi ligyiitu ɔsu.”

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Anwuɔsu uwi ɔni na Yakob ɔ́si kemo úbeduo useyi ɔmmɔ ni, Lea útásiaku-nwu ulu na ɔ́su-nwu ɔnini, “Ɔdi ka abatɛ-mi kudɔ ɔmwi. Ɔsika lɛ́ƒɔ-ɔ líse ɔkpa na kɔwɔ ligyiitu lɛni na Rahel ɔ́ƒɔ mina ubi kudɔ ɔsu.” Anwuɔsu úsie útátɛ Lea kudɔ usien ɔmmɔ.

ref: and God answered Leah's prayers by giving her a fifth son.
text: Unwoti Katɔti únu Lea bɔkpali na ɔ́ya na ɔ́sɔ ɔda kaƒɔ, na ɔ́wɔ ɔnanabi ɔlɔndi úte Yakob.

ref: Leah shouted, “God has rewarded me for letting Jacob marry my servant,” and she named the boy Issachar.
text: Amu na Lea ɔ́su ɔnini, “Unwoti Katɔti ɔ́ƒɔ-mi kayu ka liti mina ulekubi kabla ɔblane líte mina ɔgyani ɔsu.” Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Isakar, na nwuna kalɔ ndi lɛyuƒɔla.

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Na Lea ɔ́su ɔnini, “Unwoti Katɔti úte-mi litela lɛni náƒɔ kuye geen. Na ɔƒɛni ni, mina ɔgyani ɔdubo-mi ɔbu geen buteete. Ɔsika lɛ́wɔ bananabi ɛku líte-nwu.” Ɔmmɔ ɔsu ɔ́sia-nwu lɛnyi ɔnii Sebulon, na nwuna kalɔ ndi ɔbu.
